# Fix Registry — Regression Prevention

This file is the **machine-readable, checked-in mirror** of the verified-fix
catalogue maintained by the development team across multiple bug-fix sessions.
It is consumed by `.github/scripts/check-fix-registry.sh` (run by the
`fix-registry-guard` CI job) on every PR.

If your PR fails the guard, it means your changes silently undid a previously
verified fix. **Do not bypass the guard** — read the failing row, understand
why the fix existed (see `description`), and re-apply or re-implement it
before merging.

## How to add a new entry

When you ship a fix that should be regression-protected:

1. Add a new row to the table below.
2. Pick the smallest, most specific grep pattern that uniquely identifies
   the fix's presence.
3. Set `type` to either `present` (file MUST contain the pattern) or
   `absent` (file MUST NOT contain the pattern — for "we removed X" fixes).
4. Use ERE (extended regex) syntax — the script runs `git grep -E`.
5. The pattern is wrapped in backticks in the table; the script strips them.

## How to retire an entry

If a fix is genuinely no longer needed (e.g. the code area was deleted),
mark the row with status `retired` instead of removing it, so the history
of why the entry existed is preserved.

## Format

Columns: `ID | File | Type | Pattern | Description`

- `ID` — short stable identifier, used in PR descriptions to claim
  "this PR preserves <ID>".
- `File` — single source file path, relative to repo root.
- `Type` — `present` | `absent` | `retired`.
- `Pattern` — ERE pattern, wrapped in single backticks. May contain pipes
  if escaped (`\|`) but commas/spaces are fine.
- `Description` — one-line reason the fix exists. Keep it terse.

| TYPEDUP:TaskResponse | apps/web/src/features/tasks/types/taskTypes.ts | absent | `^export type TaskResponse\\b` | TaskResponse removed from frontend local types: shared is the single source of truth. | BILL-PAYMENT-RACE | apps/api/src/features/billing/billingRepository.ts | present | `trx\.raw\('\?\? \+ \?', \['paid_cents'` | Audit H1 (2026-04-16): createPayment had a read-then-write race on the paid counter per CLAUDE.md §1.6 — two concurrent payments could each read `paid=0`, compute `new=X`, and the second UPDATE silently overwrote the first's money. Fixed with `.forUpdate()` row lock on the SELECT + atomic `trx.raw('?? + ?', ['paid_cents', dto.amount])` expression on the UPDATE. Phase 0.7.5 c24-d7a (SD42) later renamed the code's column reference from `paid_amount` → `paid_cents` (the real DB column, verified via psql \d invoices) — the atomic-increment shape is preserved; the anchor tracks the current canonical column name |
| BILL-PAYMENT-CLINIC-ID | apps/api/src/features/billing/billingRepository.ts | present | `CLAUDE\.md §1\.3 violation` | Audit H1 (2026-04-16): the UPDATE in createPayment was missing `clinic_id` in its WHERE clause (only filtered by `id`) — CLAUDE.md §1.3 violation. Cross-tenant update would have been possible if an attacker could guess another tenant's invoice id. Fixed to `.where({ id: dto.invoiceId, clinic_id: clinicId })`. Anchor is the comment block that documents the fix |
| BILL-PAYMENT-NOT-FOUND | apps/api/src/features/billing/billingRepository.ts | present | `'NOT_FOUND',` | Audit H1 (2026-04-16): createPayment used to crash with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined` when the invoice didn't exist, exposed via 500 to the client. Now throws a clean `AppError('Invoice X not found', 404, 'NOT_FOUND')` the error middleware maps to a proper 404 |
| FHIR-BULK-EXPORT-ERR | apps/api/src/integrations/fhir/fhirRoutes.ts | present | `fhir bulk export worker crashed before its own handler` | Audit M1 (2026-04-16): `setImmediate(() => void processBulkExportJob(job.id))` swallowed synchronous-setup throws from the worker — would have surfaced as an unhandled rejection and possibly killed the API process per CLAUDE.md §2.2. Now chains `.catch(err => { logger.error(...); update fhir_bulk_export_jobs.status = failed })` so operators see the crash AND the client sees the job transition to failed via the status endpoint |
| SCRIBE-STREAM-INTERVAL-TRY | apps/api/src/mcp/scribeStreaming.ts | present | `\[ScribeWS\] cleanup interval threw` | Audit M2 (2026-04-16): `setInterval(async () => ...)` outer for-loop body was not wrapped in try/catch — CLAUDE.md §3.2 violation. A throw during the sessions iteration would become an unhandled rejection and silently kill the cleanup loop, letting abandoned sessions + stale tmp files accumulate. Fixed by wrapping the whole callback body in a single try/catch that logs at ERROR and keeps the interval alive |
| AUTH-IDLE-WINDOW-LOG | apps/api/src/features/auth/authController.ts | present | `logIdleWindowFailure` | Audit L2 (2026-04-16): three `void primeIdleWindow()` / `void clearIdleWindow()` calls in authController silently swallowed Redis failures per CLAUDE.md §2.2 — idle-store pressure was invisible to operators. Refactored to a `logIdleWindowFailure('prime'|'clear', staffId)` helper chained via `.catch()` so every failure logs at WARN with the op + staffId + error. Does NOT block login/logout (the idle store fails open per design). Same treatment applied to apps/api/src/features/patient-app/patientAppRoutes.ts for the Viva login path |
| API-BASE-URL-CONFIG | apps/api/src/config/config.ts | present | `apiBaseUrl:` | Audit L1 (2026-04-16): API_BASE_URL was read from process.env with a `?? 'http://localhost:4000'` fallback at five FHIR call sites (fhirRoutes bulk-export kickoff, bulkExportWorker download URLs, smartAuth /.well-known discovery, smartAuth aud validation, smartAppRegistry EHR-launch `iss`). If API_BASE_URL was ever unset in production every SMART app launch + bulk-export download would point at localhost. Hoisted to config.apiBaseUrl with an env zod entry + a production startup warning; every call site now reads from config. Local dev keeps the same fallback |
| API-BASE-URL-FHIR-ROUTES | apps/api/src/integrations/fhir/fhirRoutes.ts | absent | `process\.env\.API_BASE_URL` | Audit L1 (2026-04-16): fhirRoutes.ts no longer reads process.env.API_BASE_URL directly — it goes through config.apiBaseUrl so a single source of truth exists |
| API-BASE-URL-BULK-EXPORT | apps/api/src/integrations/fhir/bulkExportWorker.ts | absent | `process\.env\.API_BASE_URL` | Audit L1 (2026-04-16): bulkExportWorker.ts no longer reads process.env.API_BASE_URL directly |
| API-BASE-URL-SMART-AUTH | apps/api/src/integrations/fhir/smartAuth.ts | absent | `process\.env\.API_BASE_URL` | Audit L1 (2026-04-16): smartAuth.ts no longer reads process.env.API_BASE_URL directly — both the discovery endpoint and the aud validation now use config.apiBaseUrl |
| API-BASE-URL-SMART-APP | apps/api/src/integrations/fhir/smartAppRegistry.ts | absent | `process\.env\.API_BASE_URL` | Audit L1 (2026-04-16): smartAppRegistry.ts no longer reads process.env.API_BASE_URL directly — the EHR launch `iss` parameter uses config.apiBaseUrl |
| QKEY-SHARED-FACTORY | apps/web/src/shared/queryKeys.ts | present | `sharedStaffKeys` | Audit M3 (2026-04-16): created apps/web/src/shared/queryKeys.ts owning 8 factory groups for cross-cutting shared queries (sharedStaffKeys, sharedClinicProfileKeys, sharedContactFormKeys, sharedSpecialtyKeys, sharedPatientQuickTaskKeys, sharedCommandPaletteKeys, sharedBreadcrumbsKeys, sharedBrandingKeys, sharedTabConfigKeys, sharedModuleVisibilityKeys, sharedSseEventKeys). Guard Rule 2 now also walks apps/web/src/shared/** so any new literal fails CI |
| QKEY-SSE-EVENTSTREAM | apps/web/src/shared/hooks/useEventStream.ts | present | `tasksKeys\.all` | Audit M3 (2026-04-16): useEventStream's SSE invalidation handler had 9 literal `queryKey: ['...']` broadcasts. Refactored to import feature factories (tasksKeys, dashboardKeys, pathologyKeys, patientsKeys.notesAll) + sharedSseEventKeys for the 3 feature-less broadcasts (check-in, MAR, admin-overview). When a feature renames its base tuple the SSE broadcast path changes in lockstep — no silent drift |
| RISK-ROUTES-RELATIVE | apps/api/src/features/risk/risk.routes.ts | absent | `router\.(post\|get\|delete)\('/api/v1/` | Audit L3 (2026-04-16): risk.routes.ts used to declare hardcoded `/api/v1/...` path literals inside route handlers because server.ts mounted it bare via `app.use(riskRoutes)`. Non-standard vs every other feature (which uses `app.use(\`${API}/x\`, routes)` with relative handler paths). Latent trap: if someone later moved the mount to the standard pattern the paths would become `/api/v1/risk/api/v1/risk-assessments`. Refactored to two named exports — riskRoutes (mounted at /api/v1/risk-assessments) + riskPatientRoutes (mounted at /api/v1/patients) — with relative handler paths. The guard asserts no `router.(post|get|delete)('/api/v1/` literal remains |
| RISK-ROUTES-MOUNT | apps/api/src/server.ts | present | `riskRoutes, riskPatientRoutes` | Audit L3 (2026-04-16): server.ts now imports both named exports and mounts them at explicit prefixes — standard pattern that matches every other feature. The bare `app.use(riskRoutes)` form was removed |
| RISK-TEMPLATES-CATALOG | apps/api/src/features/risk/riskTemplates.ts | present | `SUICIDE_RISK_TEMPLATE` | Audit L3 follow-up (2026-04-16): static catalog of 3 clinical risk templates (suicide-risk, self-harm, harm-to-others) backing GET /api/v1/risk-assessments/templates + :id. Previously the frontend RiskAssessmentForm called these endpoints and got 404s because no backend handler existed — the form silently rendered empty and no new structured assessments could be created. Catalog is static reference data; can migrate to a `risk_templates` table if per-clinic customisation is ever needed |
| RISK-TEMPLATES-ROUTES | apps/api/src/features/risk/risk.routes.ts | present | `riskRoutes\.get\('/templates` | Audit L3 follow-up (2026-04-16): risk.routes.ts now registers GET /templates + /templates/:templateId on the root-level riskRoutes so the frontend hits real handlers. More specific /templates literal is declared before any /:id param routes to avoid greedy matching |
| RISK-SUMMARY-TAB-PATH | apps/web/src/features/patients/components/detail/tabs/SummaryTab.tsx | absent | `risk-assessments/patient/\$\{` | Audit L3 follow-up (2026-04-16): SummaryTab.tsx life-chart panel used to hit `risk-assessments/patient/:patientId` which was never a registered route and returned 404, leaving the panel empty for every patient. Fixed to use `patients/:patientId/risk-assessments` (the existing + working riskPatientRoutes path). Also dropped the `{ data: [] }` response envelope — the working endpoint returns a bare array |
| FE-URL-GUARD-SCRIPT | scripts/guards/check-frontend-calls-backend-route.ts | present | `check-frontend-calls-backend-route` | CLAUDE.md §9.4 (2026-04-16): forward-direction companion of check-mounted-routes-have-callers.sh. Walks every apiClient call in apps/web/src/**/*.{ts,tsx}, extracts the literal URL, and asserts a matching backend handler exists. Whitespace-tolerant regex captures multi-line router.post(...) declarations. Also handles bare-mounted routers with hardcoded /api/v1/... paths (allergies). Allowlist at .github/scripts/frontend-url.allowlist for dynamic URLs that can't be resolved statically. First run caught 9 pre-existing dead calls that are each TODO-annotated in the allowlist for follow-up bugfix |
| FE-URL-GUARD-CI | .github/workflows/ci.yml | present | `frontend-url-guard:` | CLAUDE.md §9.4 (2026-04-16): frontend-url-guard wired into ci.yml as a dedicated job next to the other six per-PR guards. Blocks merge on any new frontend apiClient URL that doesn't resolve to a backend handler |
| FE-URL-GUARD-ALLOWLIST | .github/scripts/frontend-url.allowlist | present | `POST auth/password-reset/request` | CLAUDE.md §9.4 (2026-04-16): allowlist for the 9 pre-existing dead calls found on the first run of the URL guard. Each entry carries a TODO rationale. Drain one entry per bugfix PR until empty |
| FIRE-AND-FORGET-GUARD | scripts/guards/check-no-fire-and-forget.ts | present | `check-no-fire-and-forget` | CLAUDE.md §9.6 (2026-04-16): regex-based guard catching `void asyncCall(...)` fire-and-forget calls and `setInterval(async () => ...)` bodies without try/catch — the exact two shapes the 2026-04-16 audit found (M1 + M2 + L2). Simpler than wiring full ESLint type-aware (which isn't installed in the repo); covers the common cases. Allowlist at .github/scripts/fire-and-forget.allowlist for intentional fire-and-forgets (cron ticks, SIGTERM shutdown hooks) with required rationale comments. First run caught apps/api/src/jobs/bootstrap.ts:160 (Whisper health monitor setInterval) — fixed in the same commit |
| FIRE-AND-FORGET-CI | .github/workflows/ci.yml | present | `fire-and-forget-guard:` | CLAUDE.md §9.6 (2026-04-16): fire-and-forget guard wired into ci.yml as a dedicated per-PR job |
| BOOTSTRAP-WHISPER-INTERVAL | apps/api/src/jobs/bootstrap.ts | present | `Whisper\] health check interval threw` | CLAUDE.md §3.2 + §9.6 (2026-04-16): Whisper health-check setInterval body now wrapped in try/catch. | CI-INTEGRATION-JOB | .github/workflows/ci.yml | present | `Postgres 17 \+ Redis 7` | CLAUDE.md §11 Layer 4 (2026-04-16): integration test job in ci.yml rewritten to use canonical env var names (DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, DB_NAME — NOT the DATABASE_URL the original stub used), pin Postgres to 17 (matching the Phase 0.5 + Phase 0.7 baseline), and provide all the secrets config.ts zod-validates at boot (JWT_ACCESS_SECRET, JWT_REFRESH_SECRET, PHI_ENCRYPTION_KEY, BLIND_INDEX_KEY, SESSION_SECRET, CALENDAR_ICAL_SECRET). Runs `migrate:dev` then `test:integration` against a real Postgres + Redis every PR |
| E2E-SMOKE-JOB | .github/workflows/ci.yml | present | `Playwright golden paths` | CLAUDE.md §11 Layer 5 (2026-04-16): e2e-smoke job in ci.yml boots the full stack (Postgres 17 + Redis 7 services + API dev server + Vite dev server via playwright.config.ts webServer) and runs the e2e/ Playwright suite on every PR. Final forcing function that catches bugs unit + integration tests can't — silent 404s on UI surfaces, query-key invalidation mismatches that only show up on a real click. Uses seed:e2e-fixtures for admin@signacare.local / Password1! matching e2e/fixtures/auth.ts USERS.admin |

| CAL-ICAL-SERVICE | apps/api/src/features/calendar/icalService.ts | present | `buildCalendarIcs` | Phase 13 PR2c (2026-04-16): VCALENDAR builder using ical-generator@7.2.0 (pinned in PR2a). Pure function — takes blocks + clinic timezone + clinician name, returns a `text/calendar` body. Maps colours to VEVENT STATUS/TRANSP/CATEGORIES (red=CONFIRMED+OPAQUE+Unavailable, yellow=TENTATIVE+TRANSPARENT+Tentative, green=CONFIRMED+TRANSPARENT+Available). Weekly blocks emit RRULE FREQ=WEEKLY BYDAY=<weekday> anchored to firstOccurrenceOnOrAfter(effectiveFrom, dayOfWeek); one-off blocks emit a single VEVENT on specific_date |
| CAL-ICAL-PUBLIC-ROUTE | apps/api/src/features/calendar/calendarIcalPublicRoutes.ts | present | `calendarIcalPublicRoutes\.get` | Phase 13 PR2c (2026-04-16): public iCal subscription endpoint. The ONE calendar route that bypasses authMiddleware — HMAC token in the query string is the credential. Rate-limited per-token (120 req/hr, keyed on token prefix not IP so a compromised URL cannot bleed legit subscribers). Looks up the clinician + clinic via dbAdmin, verifies token against the persisted issuedAt, rejects any mismatch with 401 (no oracle information). Response: text/calendar charset=utf-8 + Content-Disposition attachment |

| NO-SILENT-CATCHES-GUARD | .github/scripts/check-no-silent-catches.sh | present | `SILENT CATCH` | Phase 0.7.5 c20 (2026-04-17): CI guard that blocks `.catch(() => {})` / `.catch(() => [])` / `.catch(() => null)` / `.catch(() => undefined)` patterns in production code. | ROW-IFACE-DRIFT | scripts/guards/check-row-interface-matches-db.ts | present | `schema-drift-exempt` | Phase 0.7.5 c24 (2026-04-17): CI guard that blocks merge when a TS `export interface X(Row\|Db)` bound to a DB table declares fields that don't exist in the current schema. Companion integration test `apps/api/tests/integration/schemaDrift.test.ts` runs the same check against the live Postgres in the test-integration CI job. |
| ROW-IFACE-DRIFT-GUARD | apps/api/src/db/schema-snapshot.json | present | `"generatedAt"` | Phase 0.7.5 c24 (2026-04-17): committed schema snapshot — 190 tables, 2671 columns. | SD14-REALLOCATIONS | apps/api/migrations.archive/20260602000000_patient_team_reallocations.ts | present | `patient_team_reallocations` | Phase 0.7.5 c24 C5 (2026-04-17): Fixed SD14 — reallocation workflow was storing state (referral_status, referred_by_id, reviewed_*, rejection_reason) as ghost columns on `patient_team_assignments`. Every request/approve/reject crashed at runtime. Structural fix: split workflow from assignment state via new `patient_team_reallocations` table. ReallocationRow rewritten to match the new table. reallocationService request/approve/reject logic rewritten: request inserts a reallocation row (leaves existing assignment untouched); approve transactionally flips existing active assignment inactive, upserts the new active assignment, and stamps the reallocation status='active'; reject updates the reallocation row only. Four-eyes self-approval block preserved. Partial unique index enforces one-pending-at-a-time per (clinic, patient). |

| R1-MIGRATION-CONVENTION | scripts/guards/check-migration-convention.ts | present | `check-migration-convention` | Phase R R1 (2026-04-18): new CI guard walks every `knex.raw(...)` in `apps/api/migrations/*.ts`, extracts the SQL, and fails CI if it matches a simple-DDL pattern (CREATE TABLE, ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN, simple CREATE INDEX) without any allowed-token match (RLS, triggers, functions, views, partial/functional indexes, DML, CHECK constraints, dynamic identifiers). Structurally prevents the migration-convention deviation I made in Phase 0.7.5 c24 (4 raw-SQL ALTER TABLE statements) from recurring. Exemption: `// @migration-raw-exempt: <reason>` on the line immediately above the raw call. 19 pre-existing call sites in partitioning + ALTER COLUMN migrations exempted with honest reasons pointing at R2 consolidation. |
| R1-SNAPSHOT-FRESHNESS | scripts/guards/check-snapshot-freshness.ts | present | `check-snapshot-freshness` | Phase R R1 (2026-04-18): new CI guard validates `apps/api/src/db/schema-snapshot.json` structure (valid JSON, required keys, ≥100 tables, parseable generatedAt date), then compares the most recent git-commit time of `apps/api/migrations/` against the same for the snapshot. Fails CI if migrations committed more recently than the snapshot — the "forgot to regenerate" scenario. Fix: `npm run db:snapshot --workspace=apps/api` + commit. Ensures downstream guards (row-iface-drift, code-columns) run against current schema state. |
| R1-CODE-COLUMNS | scripts/guards/check-code-writes-real-columns.ts | present | `check-code-writes-real-columns` | Phase R R1 (2026-04-18): new CI guard — THE structural preventer for SD39-62 class bugs. AST-walks every `.insert({...})` / `.update({...})` call in `apps/api/src/**/*.ts`, resolves the bound table via backward-search for `db('<table>')` / `trx('<table>')`, and cross-checks every top-level object key against `schema-snapshot.json`. Surfaces ghost-column writes (`.update({ updated_at: ... })` on tables with no updated_at) that Knex silently drops at runtime. First run found 53 pre-existing drifts across 25 files — each exempt-annotated with honest `// @code-columns-exempt: pre-R2 drift on <table>: <cols>. Baseline 20260701000000 is the fix.` reasons. Every new ghost-column write fails CI from this commit forward. |
| R1-CLAUDE-SEC-12-1 | CLAUDE.md | present | `12\.1\. MIGRATION CONVENTION` | Phase R R1 (2026-04-18): CLAUDE.md §12.1 added — builder-first migration convention with concrete do/don't table. Paired with guard:migration-convention. Sets the house-style contract. |
| R1-CLAUDE-SEC-12-2 | CLAUDE.md | present | `CODE MUST WRITE REAL COLUMNS` | Phase R R1 (2026-04-18): CLAUDE.md §12.2 added — `.insert/.update` object literals must contain only real columns. Paired with guard:code-columns. |
| R1-CLAUDE-SEC-12-3 | CLAUDE.md | present | `SNAPSHOT MUST BE FRESH` | Phase R R1 (2026-04-18): CLAUDE.md §12.3 added — every migration PR must regenerate schema-snapshot.json. Paired with guard:snapshot-freshness. |

| R-FIX-POOL-STATEMENT-TIMEOUT | apps/api/src/db/db.ts | present | `SET statement_timeout = '30s'; SET idle_in_transaction_session_timeout = '60s'` | BUG-187: connection-level guardrails via shared appUserAfterCreate applied to BOTH appPool and rawDbRead. | R-FIX-INTEGRATION-RUNNER-RECURSIVE | apps/api/scripts/run-integration-tests.mjs | present | `readdirSync\(startDir, \{ withFileTypes: true \}\)` | BUG-033/110: integration-test runner walks subdirectories recursively. | R-FIX-REDIS-ALLKEYS-LRU | infra/redis.conf | present | `maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru` | BUG-197: bounded maxmemory (512mb) + allkeys-lru eviction policy. Pre-fix: noeviction + maxmemory=0 causes Redis to REJECT writes under memory pressure (CSRF, sessions, BullMQ, SSE pub/sub all fail). Post-fix: eviction replaces rejection as graceful-degradation mode. Conscious trade documented in docs/audit-2026-04-19/follow-up-on-cloud-deploy.md §5.5 — BullMQ queue state + jwtBlacklist entries may be evicted under sustained pressure; monitoring required. Matches deploy/azure/modules/redis.bicep:55. |
| R-FIX-TODO-TRIAGE-COMPLETE | apps/api/src/features/auth/webauthnRoutes.ts | absent | `\bTODO\(@simplewebauthn` | BUG-180: all raw TODO/FIXME/HACK markers in apps/{api,web}/src production paths replaced with // @catalogued: BUG-NNN annotations tying each to its owning bug (BUG-238 hl7Worker, BUG-239 webauthnRoutes, BUG-241 queryKey factory TODOs x14, BUG-242 nurse escalation). AU phone-mask XXX literals allowlisted narrowly in scripts/qa-agent/level-1-static.ts L1_7_ALLOWLIST. This `absent` anchor trips if any future commit reintroduces the representative TODO(@simplewebauthn marker in webauthnRoutes.ts. |
| R-FIX-WEBAUTHN-REAL-CRYPTO | apps/api/src/features/auth/webauthnRoutes.ts | present | `verifyRegistrationResponse\(` | BUG-239: WebAuthn register/verify and login/verify now perform cryptographic signature + origin + RP ID verification via @simplewebauthn/server@13.3.0 (MIT). Pre-fix: handlers accepted any client payload with a matching credential_id, storing client-echoed material and incrementing the counter without verifying the signature — silent MFA bypass. Post-fix: library-derived credential.id, credential.publicKey, and credential.counter are persisted on register; login rejects verified:false and refuses non-advancing counters (defence-in-depth on top of the library's verified flag). Regression tests: apps/api/tests/unit/webauthnVerify.test.ts (6 tests: library args, library-derived storage, tamper, replay-via-consumed-challenge, counter regression) + apps/api/tests/integration/webauthnEndToEnd.int.test.ts (2 tests: register→login round trip with RLS-scoped persistence + verified:false leaves counter unchanged). Origin/RP proxy-layer hardening: docs/audit-2026-04-19/follow-up-on-cloud-deploy.md §11. |
| R-FIX-LLM-ROUTES-PATIENT-RELATIONSHIP | apps/api/src/features/llm/llmRoutes.ts | present | `BUG-036 — patient-relationship gate` | BUG-036: 5 LLM endpoints gated with buildAuthContext + requirePatientRelationship — /clinical-ai + /agent in llmRoutes.ts + /patient-summary + /referral-letter + /search in scribeRoutes.ts. Conditional `if (patientId)` preserves no-patientId paths (classify action, clinic-wide semantic search) which don't flow patient data. 16 integration tests pin 5 endpoints × 3 scenarios (clinician-no-rel 403 / clinician-with-rel 200 / bypass-admin 200) + 1 conditional-gate-bypass test proving loadPatientContext is never called when patientId is absent. Denied-access audit automatic via forbiddenAccessAudit middleware at server.ts:341. Residual: letter/letterStructured/scribe-session → BUG-276 (S0 A-3); Ollama log verification → BUG-278 (S2 B-9); bypass-role LLM-access audit → BUG-279 (S1 A-3). |
| R-FIX-AMBIENT-NOTE-CONSENT-GATE | apps/api/src/features/llm/llmRoutes.ts | present | `verifyRecordingConsent\(` | BUG-035: /ambient-note now gates audio persistence + Whisper + note-save behind (a) Zod schema requiring patientId + consentId, (b) buildAuthContext + requirePatientRelationship (pattern copied from scribeRoutes.ts:357), and (c) verifyRecordingConsent helper which looks up scribe_consents by id + clinic_id + patient_id with TTL freshness (SCRIBE_CONSENT_TTL_MINUTES, default 15 min). All gates run BEFORE blobStorage.put (Review 2 point 1). Audit_log row AMBIENT_NOTE_RECORDING_STARTED binds record_id to consentId for forensic traceability. Pre-fix: any authenticated clinician could POST audio for any patientId and have it persisted + transcribed + saved with zero consent or relationship check. Residual: WebSocket scribe path → BUG-272; clinical_notes consent_id FK → BUG-273; revocation signal → BUG-274. |
| R-FIX-SCRIBE-NO-DIAGNOSIS-INFERENCE | apps/api/src/mcp/medicalScribe.ts | present | `NEVER infer, generate, or suggest a diagnosis` | BUG-034: three scribe prompt sites (SCRIBE_PASS3_SYSTEM Rule 9, SCRIBE_SOAP_FORMAT Assessment, SCRIBE_MSE_FORMAT Clinical Impression) rewritten to explicitly forbid diagnostic inference. Pre-fix: "Use ICD-10-AM codes where diagnosis is mentioned" / "Working diagnosis (ICD-10-AM code if stated)" / "[Working diagnosis and formulation]" — all three invited the LLM to produce diagnosis content despite the file header's TGA non-device classification saying "No Pass infers, diagnoses". Post-fix: explicit "NEVER infer" rule + permissive omit-OR-state language + synonym block ("likely", "probable", "implied", "suggestive of"). Defence-in-depth retained: extractDiagnosis regex + suggestedDiagnosis response field + hallucination-detector save-gate at llmRoutes.ts:540-561 (returns 422 and skips the insert at line 563 when fabrication detected). 7 regression tests pin absence of pre-fix strings + presence of NEW rule + synonym-drift sweep. |
| R-FIX-LOGGER-PHI-REDACT-EXPANDED | apps/api/src/utils/phiFields.ts | present | `health_fund_member_number` | BUG-216: PHI_FIELDS expanded from 16 → ~100 entries covering all PHI-flavoured schema columns (names, birth, Medicare/IHI/DVA, health fund, phone, email, address) in snake_case + camelCase variants. redact.paths expanded with 7 additional req.body.* fast paths + wildcards (*.password, *.mfaSecret). Pre-fix: logger.info({ patient }) leaked address_postcode, nok_phone, gp_email, ihi_number, health_fund_number verbatim. Post-fix: all emitted as [REDACTED]. PHI_FIELDS + redactPhi now exported for direct unit testing. Six regression tests in apps/api/tests/unit/loggerRedaction.test.ts (flat / nested / non-PHI pass-through / redact.paths fast path / realistic audit shape / end-to-end pino stream). Residual: err.message PHI leak → BUG-267 (FIXED — sanitizeErrForLogging serializer); L1.14 guard inverted → BUG-268; preventive CI guard missing → BUG-269. **BUG-267 refactor: 2026-04-21 — PHI_CATEGORY_* arrays + PHI_FIELDS set + redactPhi moved from utils/logger.ts to utils/phiFields.ts (leaf module) to break a logger↔sanitizeErrForLogging circular. logger.ts now re-exports for backward compat.** |
| R-FIX-BUG-270-REDACTPHI-COPY-ON-WRITE | apps/api/src/utils/phiFields.ts | present | `function redactPhiRecord\(` | BUG-270: recursive redaction overhead on large operational payloads. Pre-fix `redactPhi` cloned every traversed object regardless of whether PHI was present and had no cycle memoization, creating unnecessary allocation pressure for non-PHI-heavy logs and recursion risk on self-referential diagnostic payloads. Post-fix adds cycle-safe copy-on-write traversal (`redactPhiRecord` + WeakMap memo): only PHI-touched branches are cloned/redacted, untouched branches preserve reference identity, and cycles are traversed safely without mutating source objects. Runtime semantics are preserved (`[REDACTED]` by field-name taxonomy, logger formatters/redact.paths unchanged). Regression pins in `apps/api/tests/unit/loggerRedaction.test.ts`: copy-on-write fast path reference stability, touched-branch-only clone, and cycle-safe traversal proof. Validation: unit PASS (12/12), `lint:changed` PASS, `typecheck` PASS, `guard:all` PASS. |
| R-FIX-BUG-285-LLM-DISCLAIMER-ENVELOPE-GUARD | scripts/guards/check-llm-disclaimer-envelope.ts | present | `ROUTE_CONTRACTS` | BUG-285: fail-closed CI guard enforcing canonical clinical-AI disclaimer envelopes. Contract pins sanctioned response surfaces (`POST /llm/suggest`, `POST /llm/clinical-ai`, `POST /llm/agent`, `POST /scribe/patient-summary`, `POST /scribe/referral-letter`) and asserts each remains wired to `disclaimer: CLINICAL_AI_DISCLAIMER`. Guard fails on route removal/rename, handler rewiring (including `/llm/suggest` drifting away from `llmController.suggest`), or disclaimer-field deletion. Regression tests in `scripts/guards/__tests__/check-llm-disclaimer-envelope.test.ts` cover pass fixture plus two fail fixtures (`/clinical-ai` disclaimer removed, `/suggest` disclaimer removed). Wired into global guard surface via package script `guard:llm-disclaimer-envelope`, so `guard:all` now blocks disclaimer drift before merge. |
| R-FIX-BUG-308-SHUTDOWN-OBSERVABILITY-DASHBOARD | apps/api/src/shared/gracefulShutdown.ts | present | `getGracefulShutdownObservabilitySnapshot\(` | BUG-308: shutdown observability dashboard with per-hook duration + timeout metrics. Pre-fix graceful-shutdown only emitted transient logs (`hook complete`/`hook failed`) with no structured run history or queryable per-hook metrics, so operators could not trend timeout/failure behavior across restarts. Post-fix adds bounded telemetry in the canonical registry (last 50 runs): per-hook `durationMs`/`timeoutMs`/outcome (`completed`/`failed`/`timed_out`/`skipped_budget`), run-level summary counters, budget-exhausted flag, and rolling 24h aggregates. Exposed via compliance surfaces: `GET /api/v1/reports/compliance/summary` (`platformReliability` rollups) and fail-closed `GET /api/v1/reports/compliance/shutdown-observability` (Zod-validated payload). Dashboard UI now renders these metrics in a dedicated Platform Reliability section and per-hook table. Regression pins: `apps/api/tests/unit/gracefulShutdownObservability.test.ts` (`2/2`) + `apps/api/tests/integration/reportsRoutesHealth.int.test.ts` (`6/6`) + `apps/api/tests/integration/gracefulShutdown.int.test.ts` (`11/11`). Validation: `lint:changed`, `typecheck`, `guard:all` PASS. |
| R-FIX-BUG-311-SAFESCRIPT-CHECKED-CONTRACT | apps/api/src/features/prescriptions/prescriptionRepository.ts | present | `SafeScriptCheckResultSchema\.parse\(result\)` | BUG-311: SafeScript `.checked` type-level + persistence contract. Pre-fix `updateSafescriptResult(...)` was a no-op stub, so `/prescriptions/:id/safescript-check` never persisted `safescript_checked` / `safescript_checked_at` / `safescript_result`, and response state could drift from storage truth. Post-fix: shared SSoT schemas (`packages/shared/src/safeScript.schemas.ts`) now govern request + result types end-to-end; service returns parsed `SafeScriptCheckResultSchema` payloads; repository parses and persists result to canonical columns with not-found fail-closed behavior; response mapper normalizes persisted payload via safe-parse and drops malformed legacy data with structured warning signal (`BUG-311_SAFE_SCRIPT_RESULT_CONTRACT_DRIFT`). Regression pins: `apps/api/tests/integration/bug311SafeScriptCheckedContract.int.test.ts` (`2/2`) covering success persistence and malformed payload fail-closed `422` with no DB mutation; compatibility suite `tests/integration/prescriptionsDisciplineBarrier.int.test.ts` (`9/9`) PASS. Validation: `lint:changed`, `typecheck`, `guard:all` PASS. |
| R-FIX-BUG-314-SCRIBE-WS-HEARTBEAT | apps/api/src/mcp/scribeStreaming.ts | present | `new ScribeWebSocketHeartbeatController<ScribeWebSocketLike>\(` | BUG-314: dead-client detection for `/ws/scribe` WebSocket sessions. Pre-fix the server had no active ping/pong liveness loop, so half-open sockets could leave stale sessions resident in memory and continue occupying scheduler/streaming state. Post-fix introduces a dedicated heartbeat controller (`apps/api/src/mcp/scribeWebSocketHeartbeat.ts`) with explicit fail-closed semantics: register socket on connect, refresh liveness on `pong`, periodic ping tick, and terminate stale/dead sockets with close code `4410` + reason `HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT`. `scribeStreaming.ts` now wires lifecycle cleanup to remove timed-out sessions from both `sessions` and `wsSessionIndex`, preventing ghost-session accumulation. Regression pins: `apps/api/tests/unit/scribeWebSocketHeartbeat.test.ts` (`4/4`) covers healthy ping path, stale timeout termination, pong refresh, and ping-write failure; `apps/api/tests/integration/scribeWebSocketConsent.int.test.ts` (`10/10`) re-validated no consent/auth regressions on the same WS surface. Validation: `lint:changed`, `typecheck`, `guard:all` PASS. |

| R-FIX-E2E-STORAGE-STATE-LOGIN | e2e/fixtures/global-setup.ts | present | `context\.storageState\(\{ path: ` | BUG-032: Playwright probes previously re-ran loginAs in beforeEach ~50 times per suite, saturating MAX_SESSIONS (5) + auth rate-limiter bucket and causing the 8th login to hang with UI stuck at /login. Probe specs declare `test.use({ storageState, authPersona })` — session reuse via cookies + localStorage from storageState AND sessionStorage reseeded via context.addInitScript (Playwright storageState does NOT persist sessionStorage per types.d.ts:9287-9329). Frontend authStore persists isAuthenticated to sessionStorage; without the sidecar, restored contexts see isAuthenticated=false and AuthGuard redirects to /login. 01-auth.spec.ts kept using direct loginAs to preserve real-login regression coverage. Regression test: e2e/probes/storage-state-smoke.spec.ts (3 chromium tests — no /login redirect, no POST /auth/login, welcome header identity). |

''` partial-config drift), escript/npdsClient.ts (stub-mode WARN not ERROR). Clinicians believed real eRx / SMS / push was flowing. CLAUDE.md §6.2 violation. L3-style pre-exec reviews absorbed 6 items (feature-flag-first code shape; server.ts try/catch + process.exit(1); CrashLoopBackoff rationale; secret-safety of error output; isXxxConfigured() scope-limit; evidence/eclipse skip tags). Rejected Review 1 #2 (SafeScript throw) as misattribution. Residual: per-clinic config drift → BUG-310 (S2 B-11); SafeScript .checked type contract → BUG-311 (S3 B-11). |
| R-FIX-SIGTERM-GRACEFUL-SHUTDOWN | apps/api/src/shared/gracefulShutdown.ts | present | `export async function runGracefulShutdown` | BUG-042: server.ts had a minimal SIGTERM handler duplicated across HTTPS + HTTP branches that did NOT drain BullMQ workers (in-flight HL7 / outreach / Outlook / AI / OCR jobs killed mid-execution → duplicate enqueue), did NOT close WebSocket connections (`server.close()` hung on WS upgrades), did NOT flip /ready to 503 (LB kept routing), was not re-entrant-safe, did NOT cancel node-cron schedulers (ticks fired against destroyed DB pool), and did NOT stop the workflow engine. New `shared/gracefulShutdown.ts` registry: priority-based hooks (100 readiness flip → 90 WebSocket close with 1001 going-away + 1s grace + terminate → 85 schedulers (matview/backup/audio-retention/appointment-reminder + featureFlags cleanup timer) → 80 HTTP close (closeIdleConnections + server.close) → 70 Whisper → 60 BullMQ workers (per-worker timeoutMs: ai=20s, ocr=15s, others=5s default) → 50 workflow engine → 20 DB → 10 Redis → 5 OTEL SDK flush). Overall 25s budget with deadline tracking + per-hook default 5s timeout with override for long-running workers; re-entrant via isShuttingDown flag (second invocation returns immediately). Both SIGTERM + SIGINT invoke same path. /ready route (routes/health.ts) returns 503 reason='graceful_shutdown_in_progress' during drain. Duplicate /ready in server.ts consolidated away. 10/10 integration tests PASS including T7 (WS-before-HTTP priority pinned), T8 (custom timeoutMs honoured), T9 (scheduler-before-DB ordering), T10 (OTEL-after-DB/Redis ordering). L3 first-pass REJECTED with 8 findings, all absorbed. L5 REQUEST_CHANGES identified apps/api/src/observability/otel.ts parallel `process.on('SIGTERM', () => void shutdownOtel())` handler — absorbed by folding into the canonical registry at priority 5 (spans outlive DB/Redis teardown to capture infra exit in traces). Non-goals explicit: does not guarantee exactly-once execution (BUG-202), log durability on hard SIGKILL (BUG-306 Pino sync flush), WS client seamless reconnect, queued-but-not-started job drain. Residual: BUG-306 (S2 B-9, Pino sync flush); BUG-308 (S3 B-11, shutdown observability dashboard). |
| R-FIX-PINO-SYNC-FLUSH | apps/api/src/server.ts | present | `flushLoggerSync\(\)` | BUG-306: add priority-5 shutdown hook `pino-sync-flush` that executes synchronous destination flush before process exit. `utils/logger.ts` now owns the destination handle (`pino.destination({ sync: false })`) and exports `flushLoggerSync()` for deterministic hook use. This closes the prior "buffer may be lost on exit" gap from BUG-042 residuals and keeps shutdown durability mechanics explicit in the registry path (not hidden in ad-hoc signal handlers). Regression coverage: `apps/api/tests/unit/pinoFlushSync.test.ts` (helper semantics) + `apps/api/tests/integration/gracefulShutdown.int.test.ts` T11 (same-priority order pin for OTEL-before-pino flush). |

| R-FIX-PRESCRIBER-DISCIPLINE-BARRIER | apps/api/migrations/20260421000003_prescriber_discipline_barrier.ts | present | `is_prescribing_eligible_discipline` | BUG-040: AHPRA-compliant two-layer defence prevents a psychologist (or any non-prescribing discipline) from being recorded as the prescriber on patient_medications. Layer A (app): `requirePrescribingDiscipline(auth)` helper in apps/api/src/shared/authGuards.ts — called by medicationService.create + update (when `prescribedByStaffId` is touched). Fails fast with HTTP 403 `PRESCRIBING_DISCIPLINE_REQUIRED`. Layer B (DB): `is_prescribing_eligible_discipline(slug text)` SQL function (SSoT for allow-list — both layers call it, zero drift possible) + `patient_medications_prescriber_discipline_check()` PL/pgSQL trigger function + BEFORE INSERT + BEFORE UPDATE OF prescribed_by_staff_id triggers raising `'prescriber discipline "%" not authorised to prescribe (BUG-040)'`. Fires for ALL roles including dbAdmin — defence-in-depth against compromised owner role or direct SQL bypass. Allows NULL `prescribed_by_staff_id` (legacy/transient paths). Allow-list: psychiatry, general-practice, nurse-practitioner. Deny: all psychology variants, RN/EN, social work, pharmacy, allied health (dietetics/physio/speech-path/peer/counselling/art-therapy/exercise), Aboriginal health work. Regression: apps/api/tests/integration/prescriberDisciplineBarrier.int.test.ts (8 tests — psychiatrist/NP/GP happy paths + psychologist/RN raise + UPDATE swap raises + NULL allowed + app-layer 403). Residual: midwives/dentists/podiatrists/optometrists/vaccinating-pharmacists → BUG-289 when EMR scope expands; future prescription_orders table → BUG-290; pre-fix data-quality survey → BUG-291; **L4 absorption** — existing `prescriptions` table (eScript) lacks equivalent guard+trigger → BUG-292 (S0 A-3); clozapine repository paths lack guard → BUG-293 (S0 A-3). medicationRepository.create now persists `prescribed_by_staff_id` + `recorded_by_staff_id` from service-supplied auth.staffId (pre-fix the columns were silently dropped, making the trigger decorative). Test count updated from 8 → 10 (T9 NULL-discipline fail-closed, T10 column round-trip). |

Migration replaces `is_prescribing_eligible_discipline(slug)` with an expanded, punctuation-tolerant normalization pipeline (`lower+trim` -> collapse non-alphanumeric runs to `-` -> trim edge dashes) and extends allow-list to include `internal-medicine`, `general-medicine`, `endocrinology`, `paediatrics`, `obstetrics-gynaecology`, `general-surgery`, and `medical-oncology` while preserving existing `psychiatry`, `general-practice`, `nurse-practitioner`. This preserves one policy source consumed by app guard + all prescribing triggers. Regression suite `apps/api/tests/integration/prescriberDisciplineBarrier.int.test.ts` expanded to 17 assertions across new-allowed specialists, blocked disciplines, fail-closed NULL-discipline path, and app-layer parity (`requirePrescribingDiscipline`). Change class: risky (SSoT function replacement affects patient_medications + prescriptions + clozapine trigger surfaces simultaneously). |
| R-FIX-AUDIT-LOG-IMMUTABILITY | apps/api/migrations/20260421000002_audit_log_immutability.ts | present | `audit_log_prevent_mutation` | BUG-039: audit_log is now tamper-evident via two-layer defence (HIPAA 164.312(b); APP 11.1 security). Layer A: `REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE, TRUNCATE ON audit_log FROM app_user` (grant-level block — INSERT + SELECT retained). Layer B: `audit_log_prevent_mutation()` PL/pgSQL function + BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE triggers that `RAISE EXCEPTION 'audit_log is append-only (BUG-039 tamper-evident)'` — fires for ALL roles including dbAdmin (owner), so a future `GRANT ALL` regression or a compromised owner role is still blocked. Pre-fix: v2 baseline granted all privileges to app_user by default (Postgres inherits owner privileges on CREATE TABLE); auditLogImmutability.test.ts Layer 2 failing `has_table_privilege(app_user, 'audit_log', UPDATE/DELETE) === false`. Post-fix: Layer 2 PASS + 2 new defence-in-depth assertions verify the trigger raises for dbAdmin. writeAuditLog (utils/audit.ts) unchanged — it only INSERTs via dbAdmin; zero legitimate mutation paths on audit_log exist in apps/api/src. 7-year retention is planned via DROP PARTITION (DDL, not DML — triggers don't fire). Regression: apps/api/tests/integration/auditLogImmutability.test.ts (16 tests). Residual: llm_interactions lacks equivalent REVOKE+trigger → BUG-286 (S1 A-3); SHA-256 hash chain dropped in v2 squash → BUG-287 (S2 B-9); partitioning dropped in v2 squash → BUG-288 (S2 B-9). |

| R-FIX-REMOVE-VOID-REQUIRE-ENV-REDUNDANT | apps/api/src/integrations/medicare/eclipseClient.ts | absent | `void requireEnv\(` | BUG-008: 10 `void requireEnv('ENV_NAME', 'context')` call-sites removed across 4 skeleton integration clients (eclipse 3 / mhr 3 / radiology 2 / healthlink 2). All were dead code — `requireEnv()` at apps/api/src/shared/requireEnv.ts:26-38 is **synchronous** and throws AppError eagerly; each call sat inside an async handler after a `!isConfigured()` gate and was immediately followed by an unconditional `throw new Error('XXX_NOT_IMPLEMENTED')`, so the `void` did nothing and the trailing throw always executed. Secondary issue: the `void <asyncCall>()` shape matched the `check-no-fire-and-forget` guard regex, producing 10 false-positive guard failures. Fix: deleted the 10 lines + the now-unused `requireEnv` import from each of the 4 files (only `optionalEnv` still imported). BUG-043's boot-time `assertProductionIntegrationsConfigured()` already covers MHR/radiology/healthlink via feature-flag-first checks (FEATURE_FLAGGED_INTEGRATIONS array in apps/api/src/shared/assertProductionIntegrationsConfigured.ts). Eclipse remains a documented skeleton with no production path (feature-flag OFF). Registry anchor is `absent` (pattern must NOT be present in the file) — any re-introduction of `void requireEnv(` fails the guard. BUG-008 was tracked in legacy docs/audit-2026-04-19/bug-catalogue.md at S4 but missing from v2 yaml until this fix. |

| R-FIX-ERX-CONFORMANCE-TEST-SUITE | apps/api/tests/conformance/cts-v3-0-1-mvp/erxConformanceMvp.test.ts | present | `BUG-299 CTS v3.0.1 MVP` | BUG-299 (S0): ADHA CTS v3.0.1 MVP conformance test harness. Prior state: zero test files referenced ETP1/ETP2/CTS/ADHA/NPDS/ASLR conformance — any change to the prescribing stack could silently break conformance, and eRx Access Agreement acceptance test could not run. Scope LOCKED to 5 MVP test vectors per 3-reviewer consensus on 2026-04-22 (full CTS v3.0.1 has ~200 vectors and would blow A-4 budget + starve BUG-294 S0); deferred to BUG-344 in A-5 for remaining ~55 automated + Observed-Conformance witnesses. Fix: new test tree apps/api/tests/conformance/cts-v3-0-1-mvp/ with erxConformanceMvp.test.ts exercising: T1 ETP2 happy path — FHIR MedicationRequest POST against nock-mocked NPDS returns server-assigned npdsId + erxToken + expiresAt (extensions parsed correctly); T2 token validation failures — 400 (malformed SCID) + 401 (expired token) + 404 (non-existent) each surface as {success:false, error:'NPDS <status>…'} without crashing; T3 NASH mTLS rejection — missing ADHA_CERT_PATH env short-circuits at isNpdsConfigured() BEFORE any network attempt (defence-in-depth from BUG-043 boot assertion); T4 HPI-O format rejection — buildErx001Xml throws err with status=503 + code=ERX_NOT_CONFIGURED when clinic.hpio is missing OR malformed (regression shield for BUG-295); T5 duplicate submission — 409 Conflict from NPDS surfaces as {success:false, error:matches /NPDS 409/ AND /duplicate|SCID already exists/}. NEW dev dep: nock 14.0 for HTTPS interception. nock.disableNetConnect() prevents accidental real requests; pendingMocks() assertion on T3 proves no outbound request was attempted. 5/5 green. Fixtures at file top: mkPayload() for ErxPrescriptionPayload construction with overrides, sampleFhirResource for the FHIR submit body. NPDS_URL defaults to `https://api.digitalhealth.gov.au/npds/v1` and the submit path is `/MedicationRequest` (absolute, replacing base path) — nock intercepts at host root. CI gate: MVP suite runs under the default vitest pass (no special include needed — standard `tests/**/*.test.ts` glob picks up conformance/). Follow-ups: BUG-344 (A-5) — remaining ~55 CTS vectors + weekly production-NPDS test-account run for Observed Conformance + coverage map at docs/audit-2026-04-19/bug-plans/BUG-299-cts-coverage.md (map file creation tracked with BUG-344 scope). Registry anchor pins `BUG-299 CTS v3.0.1 MVP` string (header comment of the test file) — deleting the MVP suite fails the guard. Change class: risky (new conformance harness + new dev dep + ADHA accreditation dependency). |
| R-FIX-PBS-PAPER-PDF-GENERATOR | apps/api/src/shared/pbsPrescriptionPdf.ts | present | `generatePbsPrescriptionPdf` | BUG-294: PBS paper prescription PDF generator. Prior state: prescriptions DB schema had every PBS field (prescriber_number, provider_number, Medicare number, IHI, PBS item code, is_authority, authority_code, quantity, repeats, S8 flag, generic/brand name, dose, route, frequency, directions) but no PDF renderer existed — apps/api/src/features/documents/ generates clinical notes + letters, NOT PBS scripts. Blocks ETP1 paper go-live + eRx Access Agreement acceptance test. Fix: pdfkit-based A4 three-up template (Prescriber Copy / Dispensing Copy / Patient Copy) with copy cut-lines, S8 red-header marker, Authority line with code, PBS item code (or "Private"), prescriber+provider+HPI-I IDs, patient demographics + IHI + Medicare + concession, clinic letterhead + HPI-O, Rx medication block (generic + brand + dose/route/frequency + directions), date+signature line, footer with prescription ID. NEW dep: bwip-js 4.10 (pure-JS barcode generator, no native addons) — loaded via createRequire(import.meta.url) because the ESM bundle in 4.10 has a dual-package hazard where bwipp_jabcode is imported but not re-exported from bundled bwipp.mjs (crashes vitest's ESM loader); the CJS build is clean. GS1 DataMatrix barcode encodes the eRx token at 60x60pt top-right of each panel; absence of token (paper-only flow) drops the barcode but keeps the printed token ID. pdfkit constructor takes compress:false so content streams are uncompressed — unit tests can grep rendered text from the hex-encoded TJ operators. Tests: apps/api/tests/pbsPrescriptionPdf.test.ts (9 unit tests — T1 non-empty buffer; T2 %PDF header; T3 A4 MediaBox; T4 three-up rendering ≥3 "PBS PRESCRIPTION" markers; T5/T6 S8 flag toggles SCHEDULE 8 marker; T7 Authority + code; T8 eRx token in footer; T9 tolerates missing optional fields). extractTextFromPdf helper joins hex-encoded TJ blocks with '' (no separator) because pdfkit splits words across blocks when inserting kerning adjustments. Puppeteer rejected (too-heavy Chromium dep); pdfkit is direct vector rendering. Registry anchor pins `generatePbsPrescriptionPdf` — removing the exported generator fails the guard. Follow-ups: BUG-344 state-specific variations + S8-pad crypto fingerprint + pharmacy-submission QR code; wiring at medicationController Print-Script action is a separate UI task. Change class: risky (new PHI-bearing PDF artefact + new barcode dep + legal-compliance artefact). Standards: CLAUDE.md §3.1 (Promise-based with error handler), no-hardcoded-secrets (barcode encodes caller-supplied token only). |
| R-FIX-MHR-DOCUMENT-PUSH-CLIENT | apps/api/src/integrations/mhr/mhrDocumentClient.ts | present | `buildDocumentReference` | BUG-298: MHR FHIR document-push client code-complete. Prior state: pushDocument threw 'MHR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED: NASH-signed FHIR client awaits ADHA registration'. Now: (1) buildDocumentReference constructs a FHIR R4 DocumentReference with subject (IHI via http://ns.electronichealth.net.au/id/hi/ihi/1.0), author (HPI-I), custodian (HPI-O), type.coding (LOINC — 11488-4 consult / 18842-5 discharge / 57133-1 referral / 60591-5 summary), content.attachment.data (base64-encoded CDA XML), masterIdentifier (urn:uuid:<letterId> for MHR-side dedup). (2) buildBundle wraps in FHIR transaction with POST DocumentReference. (3) pushDocument flow: isMhrDocumentApiConfigured check → createMtlsAgent (BUG-332 shared helper, integrationName='MHR', cert env MHR_NASH_CERT_PATH + passphrase MHR_NASH_CERT_PASSPHRASE) → httpsPostJson with X-MHR-Conformance-Id header → parseBundleResponse extracts externalDocId from entry[0].response.location (DocumentReference/<id>/_history/<ver>). (4) Retry with jittered exponential backoff on 5xx (200ms + 800ms, ±25% jitter; budget = 3 attempts); hard-fail on 4xx (permanent, no retry, operator-alertable logger.error with bodySnippet truncated to 400 chars — no PHI). Shared helper (BUG-332) is the 3rd caller — N=3 rule-of-three satisfied. (5) Parse errors on successful 2xx responses fall into the retry loop as network-equivalent failures (conservative; malformed MHR response suggests a transient MHR issue). Tests: apps/api/tests/mhrDocumentClient.test.ts (9 unit tests — T1 LOINC codes for all 4 documentType variants; T2 base64 round-trip of cdaXml; T3/T4/T5 identifier system URIs for IHI/HPI-I/HPI-O; T6 bundle transaction shape; T7 externalDocId extraction from location; T8 malformed JSON throws; T9 missing location throws). Full E2E blocked until ADHA partner registration + NASH cert; code-complete per BUG-298 accepted_pattern. Caller letterDeliveryService.ts already routes to pushDocument under feature-flag 'integration-mhr-docref'. Registry anchor pins `buildDocumentReference` function — removing the payload builder fails the guard. Change class: risky (new integration + PHI transport via NASH mTLS). Standards: CLAUDE.md §6.2 (no hardcoded secrets — env-only), §3.1 (try/catch with retry budget), CLAUDE.md §13 (pattern for future: caller letterDeliveryService should receive AuthContext when next touched). |
| R-FIX-SHARED-MTLS-HELPER | apps/api/src/shared/mtls.ts | present | `export function createMtlsAgent` | BUG-332: shared NASH / mTLS https.Agent factory extracted at N=2 in preparation for BUG-298 (N=3). Prior state: apps/api/src/integrations/escript/npdsClient.ts:getHttpsAgent and apps/api/src/integrations/hiService/hiServiceClient.ts:getHttpsAgent were structurally-identical 30-LOC blocks — read optionalEnv(certPath), existsSync, requireEnv(passphrase), construct https.Agent({pfx,passphrase,rejectUnauthorized,keepAlive}), cache module-scope. BUG-297 L5 review correctly deferred extraction until rule-of-three. Extracting NOW (Wave A-4 Sub-cluster B opening commit) means BUG-298's MHR FHIR document-push client consumes the shipped helper directly rather than a half-drafted co-commit. New apps/api/src/shared/mtls.ts exports createMtlsAgent({certPathEnv, passphraseEnv, integrationName, passphraseDescription}) + resetMtlsAgentCacheForTests(name?). Module-level Map cache keyed by integrationName so a test that swaps one integration's cert doesn't invalidate another, and two integrations sharing an env shape don't collide. Return shape preserved exactly: undefined in stub mode (cert env absent OR file missing) with production ERROR log + dev WARN log; throws via requireEnv when cert exists but passphrase is missing (fail-fast — would guaranteed-fail the TLS handshake). Refactor: hiServiceClient.getHttpsAgent shrinks from 33 LOC to 7 LOC (delegation only); npdsClient.getHttpsAgent same shape. __resetHttpsAgentForTests delegates to resetMtlsAgentCacheForTests('HI Service') — test hooks preserved. Tests: apps/api/tests/mtlsHelper.test.ts (5 unit tests — T1 undefined when env absent; T2 undefined when file missing; T3 two calls with same name both return undefined in stub mode; T4 cache scoped by integrationName; T5 resetMtlsAgentCacheForTests with + without name argument). BUG-297 hiServiceMtls integration tests (8) remain green post-refactor — the test hook swap is transparent. Registry anchor pins `export function createMtlsAgent` — removing the helper or renaming the export fails the guard. Change class: standard (pure refactor; same production behaviour). Follow-up: BUG-298 next in Sub-cluster B will be the helper's first greenfield caller. |

| R-FIX-LLM-CALLER-MIGRATION-TO-PROMPTS-OUTPUTS | apps/api/src/shared/recordLlmInteraction.ts | present | `FORBIDDEN_METADATA_RAW_TEXT_KEYS` | BUG-342: caller migration populating promptText+outputText+consentId on recordLlmInteraction (unblocked by BUG-282 c7984e7).
