Migrated: (1) features/llm/llmRoutes.ts:843 /agent handler — promptText=query, outputText=result.answer, consentId=null (not recording-bound); (2) mcp/ambientProcessor.ts:559 — promptText=diarizedTranscript||transcript, outputText=formattedNote, consentId threaded through new ProcessOptions.consentId field from the /ambient-note HTTP handler (dto.consentId) and the WS scribe stop handler (session.consentId bound at session-open per BUG-272); (3) mcp/trainingPipeline.ts:75 saveFeedback — promptText=feedback.inputText, outputText=feedback.aiOutput, consentId=null (post-hoc feedback not recording-bound); (4) features/documents/documentService.ts:259 — promptText=systemPrompt+userMessage, outputText=content, consentId=null. ProcessOptions interface in ambientProcessor.ts extended with consentId?: string | null field; two processAmbientAudio callers (llmRoutes /ambient-note + scribeStreaming stop handler) thread it through. Additionally: assertMetadataPhiSafe in recordLlmInteraction extended with FORBIDDEN_METADATA_RAW_TEXT_KEYS runtime guard — keys {inputText, outputText, aiOutput, prompt, output, rawText, transcript, answer, promptText} are rejected with '[BUG-342] metadata key \\'X\\' is forbidden — raw LLM prompt/output text must be passed via promptText + outputText args' error. This is the regression shield preventing a future PR from reintroducing raw text to metadata JSONB. Length-only keys (inputTextLen, aiOutputLen) are still accepted — aggregate markers allowed per BUG-037. Tests: apps/api/tests/integration/llmCallerMigration.int.test.ts (6 tests — T1 promptText+outputText writes llm_prompts_outputs row; T2/T3/T4 forbidden keys inputText/aiOutput/transcript rejected; T5 legacy length-only metadata still works; T6 ProcessOptions.consentId runtime-accessible). BUG-282 12-test regression suite remains green. Follow-up BUG-317 retention still owns the async hard-purge path. Anchor pins FORBIDDEN_METADATA_RAW_TEXT_KEYS set name — rename fails the guard. Change class: risky (4 production call-site changes + 1 interface extension + 1 runtime guard). |
| R-FIX-LLM-PROMPTS-OUTPUTS | apps/api/migrations/20260701000036_llm_prompts_outputs.ts | present | `llm_prompts_outputs_mark_revoked` | BUG-282 (A-4 re-attempt): encrypted PHI-isolation table for LLM prompt+output text with tamper-evident triggers, revocation soft-mark, machine-checkable encryption_status, and RLS. A-3 first attempt drew L3 REJECT + L4 BLOCK + L5 PASS; all blocking items absorbed into this spec per A-4 plan. NEW migration 20260701000036 creates llm_prompts_outputs (id uuid PK, llm_interaction_id uuid NOT NULL FK CASCADE, prompt_encrypted text NULL, output_encrypted text NULL, encryption_status varchar(16) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ENCRYPTED' CHECK IN ('ENCRYPTED','FAILED','REVOKED'), consent_id uuid NULL FK scribe_consents SET NULL, created_at timestamptz NOT NULL) + 3 indexes (interaction, consent, encryption_status) + CHECK constraint. Immutability via BUG-343's applyImmutability helper; then CREATE OR REPLACE the trigger function body with a carve-out that permits exactly ONE UPDATE shape: revocation soft-mark (encryption_status NOT REVOKED → 'REVOKED' + both ciphertext columns → NULL + llm_interaction_id unchanged + consent_id unchanged). Every other UPDATE + every DELETE still raises 'llm_prompts_outputs is append-only (BUG-282 tamper-evident)'. NEW SECURITY DEFINER function llm_prompts_outputs_mark_revoked(p_consent_id uuid) RETURNS integer (row-count affected) does the soft-mark UPDATE — called from the consent revoke endpoint. Idempotent: re-running skips already-REVOKED rows via WHERE clause + trigger carve-out's OLD.encryption_status IS DISTINCT FROM 'REVOKED'. RLS policy joins via EXISTS on llm_interactions.clinic_id (no direct clinic_id column — parent row is SSoT; avoids drift potential). GRANT EXECUTE on mark_revoked to app_user. Recorder extension: apps/api/src/shared/recordLlmInteraction.ts adds optional promptText/outputText/consentId args; when promptText+outputText supplied, INSERTs land in a single dbAdmin.transaction (R2 invariant: either both or neither — FK-violation rollback test covers non-encryption failure path). Encryption-failure policy (single invariant, no OR): on encryptPhi failure OR PHI_ENCRYPTION_KEY absent at runtime, write NULL ciphertext (NEVER plaintext — Rule 3/6 invariant), set encryption_status='FAILED', emit structured logger.error with {recordId, feature, reason} — no PHI. Parent llm_interactions INSERT always succeeds (clinical-flow continuity wins over PHI-companion availability). Ciphertext-shape detection: successful AES-256-GCM output has 3 colon-separated parts and differs from plaintext; failure modes (missing-key passthrough, unexpected-shape, cipher throw) all route to FAILED path. Boot assertion: apps/api/src/shared/assertProductionIntegrationsConfigured.ts new Check 7.7 rejects production boot when PHI_ENCRYPTION_KEY is missing OR length <64 hex chars (AES-256 = 32 bytes key material = 64 hex). Adds to missing[] → CrashLoopBackoff. Non-prod WARN+continue (consistent with BUG-295 STRICT pattern). Revocation hook: apps/api/src/features/llm/scribeRoutes.ts consent revoke endpoint now calls llm_prompts_outputs_mark_revoked(consentId) synchronously between the cache invalidation and the audit_log write. Soft-mark failure is logged but doesn't block the audit row (R1 forensic-durability invariant). Row count logged (without PHI — counts only) so ops can spot bulk-revocation events. Async hard-purge owned by BUG-317 retention job (out of BUG-282 scope). Tests: apps/api/tests/integration/llmPromptsOutputs.int.test.ts (12 tests — T1 legacy single-INSERT; T2 both-tables atomic; T3 transaction rollback on FK violation — neither row lands; T4 ENCRYPTED status + iv:tag:ciphertext shape; T5 FAILED + NULL when PHI_ENCRYPTION_KEY absent; T6 UPDATE raises on non-carve-out shapes; T7 DELETE raises; T8 FK CASCADE + SET NULL via information_schema; T9 RLS cross-tenant SELECT returns zero; T10 export filter WHERE encryption_status='ENCRYPTED' AND consent_id IS NOT NULL excludes FAILED + REVOKED + NULL-consent; T11 production boot refuses without PHI_ENCRYPTION_KEY; T12 mark_revoked flips status + trigger carve-out permits this exact shape + rejects all others). 39 regression tests (BUG-039 + BUG-286 + BUG-274 + BUG-343) remain green. Shipped-code surfaces extended: shared/recordLlmInteraction (new transactional path + FAILED fallback + args), shared/assertProductionIntegrationsConfigured (Check 7.7), features/llm/scribeRoutes consent revoke (mark_revoked call). Follow-ups: BUG-342 caller migration (drains llm_interactions.metadata of raw text by populating new args across ~15-20 call sites), BUG-317 async hard-purge retention job. Registry anchor pins `llm_prompts_outputs_mark_revoked` function name — rename fails the guard. Change class: risky (schema + trigger carve-out + encryption-at-rest + cross-cutting to shipped code). |
| R-FIX-MAKE-IMMUTABLE-HELPER | apps/api/src/db/migrations-helpers/makeImmutable.ts | present | `export async function applyImmutability` | BUG-343: extract shared makeImmutable() migration helper at rule-of-three. Two existing immutable-class tables (audit_log via BUG-039, llm_interactions via BUG-286) ship with near-identical inline SQL for append-only triggers + REVOKE. BUG-282's llm_prompts_outputs is the 3rd immutable table (rule-of-three reached), earning the extraction. NEW apps/api/src/db/migrations-helpers/makeImmutable.ts exports applyImmutability(knex, opts) + dropImmutability(knex, opts) + SAFE_PG_IDENTIFIER regex + assertSafeIdentifier() + escapeSqlString() helpers. MakeImmutableOptions: {tableName, appUserRole='app_user', errorMessage='<tableName> is append-only', fnNameSuffix='prevent_mutation'}. applyImmutability emits (in order): CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION <tableName>_prevent_mutation() → DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS + CREATE TRIGGER <tableName>_no_update BEFORE UPDATE → DROP + CREATE <tableName>_no_delete BEFORE DELETE → guarded DO-block REVOKE UPDATE/DELETE/TRUNCATE from app_user. Layer B (triggers) before Layer A (revoke) so in-flight UPDATE/DELETE during migration window hits exception not silent-succeed. Identifier safety: tableName + appUserRole + fnNameSuffix all validated against /^[a-z_][a-z0-9_]*$/ regex before SQL interpolation (Postgres doesn't parameterise identifiers); errorMessage single-quote-escaped. Per-table function name (not shared) preserves ops-debug MTTI rationale from BUG-286. SQL emission shape byte-identical to BUG-286's inline migration when called with tableName='llm_interactions' + matching errorMessage. Idempotent: CREATE OR REPLACE + DROP IF EXISTS + guarded REVOKE — safe to re-run. CI guard extension: scripts/guards/check-migration-convention.ts now also scans apps/api/src/db/migrations-helpers/**/*.ts so helper's raw() calls carry §12.4 taxonomy annotations (preserves coverage invariant across extraction). Tests: apps/api/tests/integration/makeImmutableHelper.int.test.ts (9 tests: T0/T0b identifier-validator accepts valid + rejects SQL-injection-style/uppercase/empty; T1 INSERT succeeds post-apply; T2 UPDATE raises with default message; T3 DELETE raises; T4 has_table_privilege asserts UPDATE/DELETE/TRUNCATE false + SELECT/INSERT true for app_user; T5 custom errorMessage propagates verbatim; T6 idempotent re-apply; T7 dropImmutability reverses — UPDATE/DELETE succeed on fresh probe). BUG-286 + BUG-039 regression suites (22 tests) remain green — helper extraction does not disturb the existing two immutable tables (they don't call the helper; the helper is for new immutable tables going forward). Deliberately NOT refactoring shipped migrations: rewriting inline SQL to call the helper is cosmetic + carries non-zero SQL-shape-drift risk; helper consumed by BUG-282 as first call site. Anchor pins `export async function applyImmutability` — a rename of the exported function fails the guard. Change class: standard (new helper + integration tests + 5-LOC guard extension; no migration, no schema change). |
| R-FIX-NPDS-CONFORMANCE-ID-PER-CLINIC | apps/api/src/integrations/escript/npdsClient.ts | present | `resolveNpdsConformanceId` | BUG-302: NPDS conformance ID per-clinic binding for multi-tenant eRx. Pre-fix NPDS_CONFORMANCE_ID was a single env var at npdsClient.ts:87; every clinic on a multi-tenant Signacare instance submitted eScripts under the same identity. ADHA conformance requires per-clinic attribution: each clinic participating in eRx has its own conformance ID issued by eRx + its own ETP1 site ID for the Adapter pathway. Fix has 5 parts: (1) Migration 20260701000034 adds clinics.npds_conformance_id varchar(64) NULLABLE + clinics.erx_etp1_site_id varchar(64) NULLABLE. Preflight COUNT(*) logged so ops tracks backfill progress. (2) NEW exported resolveNpdsConformanceId(clinicId) reads clinics.npds_conformance_id first (canonical for multi-tenant prod); falls back to NPDS_CONFORMANCE_ID env var with WARN log if column is NULL or whitespace-only (transitional period); throws HttpError 503 ERX_NOT_CONFIGURED when BOTH absent. (3) mtlsRequest(method, path, conformanceId, body?) — conformance ID now REQUIRED parameter; caller resolves per-clinic before invoking. Pre-fix inline requireEnv lookup removed. (4) submitToNpds(fhirResource, clinicId) + queryActiveScriptList(patientIhi, clinicId) + cancelOnNpds(npdsId, reason, clinicId) — all require clinicId. (5) escriptService callers pass auth.clinicId through. (6) isNpdsConfigured() relaxed to check NPDS_API_URL + ADHA_CERT_PATH only (NPDS_CONFORMANCE_ID is no longer required at boot time since it's per-clinic). (7) New boot-assertion Check 7.6 in assertProductionIntegrationsConfigured.ts with STRICT_NPDS_CONFORMANCE env-gated WARN-before-FAIL rollout (mirrors BUG-295 STRICT_ERX_HPIO pattern). Default WARN for ≥30 days post-deploy so ops backfills per-clinic via admin UI (tracked as BUG-339 follow-up). Tests: apps/api/tests/integration/npdsConformancePerClinic.int.test.ts (7 tests: T1 column NULLABLE; T2 per-clinic value returned; T3 NULL clinic falls back to env; T4 both absent throws ERX_NOT_CONFIGURED; T5 per-clinic precedence over env; T6 NPDS_API_URL absent returns NOT_CONFIGURED; T7 whitespace-only per-clinic value falls through to env). Migration snapshot regenerated. Registry anchor pins resolveNpdsConformanceId function name. Change class: risky (schema change + client-signature change + boot-assertion + live eRx transport). |
| R-FIX-WF81-NPDS-PAYLOAD-SECURITY-MODE | apps/api/src/integrations/escript/npdsClient.ts | present | `buildSecureSubmitPayload` | BUG-WF81-NPDS-PAYLOAD-ENCRYPTION: NPDS submit path now supports explicit payload security modes (`off` / `sign` / `encrypt_sign`). `sign` attaches detached RSA-SHA256 digest/signature headers over the FHIR body; `encrypt_sign` wraps the payload in an AES-256-GCM envelope plus PKI signature metadata. Runtime mode is controlled by env (`NPDS_PAYLOAD_SECURITY_MODE`) with supporting secrets (`NPDS_PAYLOAD_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY_PEM`, `NPDS_PAYLOAD_ENCRYPTION_KEY_HEX`) and boot-time production assertions in `assertProductionIntegrationsConfigured.ts` (including optional `STRICT_NPDS_PAYLOAD_SECURITY=true`). Conformance regression evidence added in `erxConformanceMvp.test.ts` T7/T8, including signature verification and encrypted-envelope assertions. |
| R-FIX-HPII-VALIDATION | apps/api/src/shared/hiNumbers.ts | present | `validateHiNumber` | BUG-296 / BUG-WF81-HPII-MISSING: HPI-I structural validation + prescriber-path gate. SSoT validators live in `shared/hiNumbers.ts` and are consumed via `hiServiceClient` helpers. Prescribing flows now enforce strict HPI-I validation through `requireValidHpii(auth)` with no warn-mode and no role bypass; missing/malformed HPI-I always throws `403 PRESCRIBER_HPII_INVALID`. Wired on medication create/update-prescriber and prescription create/submit. Regression coverage updated in `apps/api/tests/integration/hpiiValidation.int.test.ts` (11 tests; strict-only posture including admin no-bypass case). Change class: risky (clinical-blocking gate on prescribing surfaces). Follow-up BUG-336 remains for prescriber-profile data backfill and HI-service-assisted verification UX. |
| R-FIX-ERX-HPIO-HARD-ERROR | apps/api/src/integrations/escript/erxRestPayloads.ts | present | `ERX_NOT_CONFIGURED` | BUG-295: clinics.hpio column + eRx payload hard-error on missing/malformed HPI-O. Pre-fix, clinics table had no hpio column; apps/api/src/integrations/escript/erxRestPayloads.ts:258 serialised `<PrescriberHPIO>${c.hpio || ''}</PrescriberHPIO>` emitting an EMPTY STRING for every ETP1+ETP2 payload (an instant eRx accreditation failure — HPI-O is mandatory under HI Service policy). Compounding bug: escriptService.ts:248-257 didn't even propagate payload.prescriberHpio onto the clinician object it passed to erxRestPayloads, so even populated clinic.hpio would have been dropped. 3-layer fix: (1) Migration 20260701000033 adds clinics.hpio varchar(16) NULLABLE + CHECK constraint clinics_hpio_format_check (hpio IS NULL OR hpio ~ '^800362[0-9]{10}$'). NULLABLE because 7 existing clinic rows are all NULL post-migration; tightening to NOT NULL after ops backfill = BUG-316 follow-up. Luhn checksum enforcement lives at the app layer (BUG-296 validateHiNumber) rather than a PL/pgSQL function — keeps migration self-contained. Preflight audit: migration logs SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE hpio IS NULL so ops tracks backfill progress without manual query. (2) erxRestPayloads.buildFullPrescriptionXml hard-throws when c.hpio missing/malformed. Error shape mirrors HttpError duck-typed fields (.status=503 + .code='ERX_NOT_CONFIGURED' + .details.{field,reason}) so global errorHandler renders as 503. Per-clinic graceful degrade: NULL-hpio tenants get 503 at request time; other clinics continue prescribing normally — no wave-wide outage. (3) escriptService.ts:248-257 now propagates payload.prescriberHpio → clinician.hpio. (4) assertProductionIntegrationsConfigured.ts new Check 7.5: WARN-before-FAIL mode via STRICT_ERX_HPIO env var (default false). WARN mode logs for every clinic with NULL hpio at boot + continues. STRICT mode adds to missing list → production CrashLoopBackoff. Default WARN for ≥30 days post-deploy so ops backfills without downtime. Tests: apps/api/tests/integration/erxHpioValidation.int.test.ts (9 tests — T1 valid HPI-O passes + XML contains it; T2/T3/T4/T5/T6 missing/empty/wrong-prefix/wrong-length/non-numeric all throw ERX_NOT_CONFIGURED; T7 CHECK accepts valid INSERT; T8 CHECK rejects malformed INSERT; T9 CHECK permits NULL). Migration snapshot regenerated. Registry anchor pins `ERX_NOT_CONFIGURED` error code — a rename fails the guard. Change class: risky (new DB column + CHECK + payload hard-error + boot-assertion). |
| R-FIX-HI-SERVICE-MTLS | apps/api/src/integrations/hiService/hiServiceClient.ts | present | `getHttpsAgent` | BUG-297: HI Service NASH mTLS was not actually used on outbound SOAP requests. Pre-fix hiServiceClient.ts called fetch() WITHOUT passing an httpsAgent. The env var HI_SERVICE_CERT_PATH was checked for "is configured" gating (isHiServiceConfigured) but the PFX cert was NEVER loaded or attached to the request — an in-line comment at line 78-80 acknowledged this as placeholder. Production HI Service calls would have failed TLS handshake even when NASH cert was installed. The pattern was already correct in npdsClient.ts + erxAdapterClient.ts (native https.request + https.Agent cache); just never copied into hiServiceClient. Fix mirrors npdsClient.ts exactly: (1) module-scope `let httpsAgent: https.Agent | undefined` cache; (2) `getHttpsAgent()` lazy-loads HI_SERVICE_CERT_PATH via fs.readFileSync + constructs https.Agent({pfx, passphrase, rejectUnauthorized: true, keepAlive: true}); NASH .p12 and .pfx both handled natively (PKCS#12, no .pem conversion needed — operationally important since clinic IT receives NASH certs as .p12); (3) new `mtlsRequest(path, soapAction, body)` helper using native https.request() with the agent option, because Node 18-20 global fetch() does NOT support custom https agents (documented pre-existing limitation); (4) searchIhi + verifyIhi both route through mtlsRequest instead of fetch; (5) dev-mode graceful-degrade: offline behaviour unchanged (isHiServiceConfigured() false → returns {found:false, error:'HI Service not configured. Set HI_SERVICE_URL and HI_SERVICE_CERT_PATH.'}); (6) production-without-cert logs ERROR so ops dashboards surface it (BUG-043 boot-assertion should already have blocked boot; this is last-line-of-defence); (7) NEW env var HI_SERVICE_CERT_PASSPHRASE required when HI_SERVICE_CERT_PATH is set — requireEnv throws structured ENV_MISSING if cert path present but passphrase absent; added to config/secrets.ts SENSITIVE_KEYS allow-list (ADHA_CERT_PASSPHRASE added too for npdsClient parity — was missing pre-BUG-297); (8) NEW __resetHttpsAgentForTests() hook for test isolation. Tests: apps/api/tests/integration/hiServiceMtls.int.test.ts (8 tests — T1 searchIhi offline returns NOT_CONFIGURED; T2 verifyIhi offline returns unverified; T3-T6 validateIhiFormat accepts Luhn-valid 800360 prefix / rejects non-800360 / wrong length / bad Luhn; T7 isHiServiceConfigured false absent; T8 isHiServiceConfigured true when URL + cert file exist). Tests use ephemeral /tmp stub cert (no live NASH endpoint required). Registry anchor pins `getHttpsAgent` function name — a rename or removal fails the guard. Change class: risky (live cert transport + new env var + SOAP transport method swapped from fetch to native https.request — behavior-preserving at the API surface, but a production-live wire-level change). |
| R-FIX-LETTER-LLM-PATIENT-RELATIONSHIP | apps/api/src/features/llm/letterService.ts | present | `requirePatientRelationship\(auth, letter\.patient_id\)` | BUG-276 (L4-absorbed): per-endpoint patient-relationship gate audit of letter + scribe-session mutating endpoints. Pre-fix, PATCH /letters/:id/sections/:sectionKey and PATCH /scribe/session/:id accepted any authenticated clinician in the right clinic; the letter path enforced only tenant scope (clinic_id = auth.clinicId on the letter row); the scribe-session path had an ownership check (clinician_id = req.user.id OR admin/superadmin) but not a care-relationship check, so a clinician who started a session against a patient they no longer treat could continue mutating the session's state transitions (pause/resume/end/abandon) indefinitely. Endpoint inventory frozen pre-kickoff per plan Sub-cluster D: 26 total mutating endpoints across letterRoutes + letterStructuredRoutes + scribeRoutes; decision-tree narrowed to 2 that need new gates + 8 already-gated (regression-only) + 16 correctly skipped (state transitions on letters already-created-under-gate; session-scoped metadata where session was already gated on create; clinic-config/admin-triage non-patient surfaces). Fix: requirePatientRelationship(auth, letter.patient_id) inside letterService.regenerateSection + editSection (applied in the service, not the controller, so ALL callers get the gate — not just the PATCH /sections/:sectionKey route). requirePatientRelationship(auth, existing.patient_id) in PATCH /scribe/session/:id route handler right after the session lookup, BEFORE the originating-clinician ownership check (layered defence: relationship check + ownership check). Tests: apps/api/tests/integration/letterScribeSessionPatientRelationship.int.test.ts (6 tests — BUG-036 3-scenario pattern × 2 endpoints. T1 clinician-no-rel PATCH letters/:id/sections/:key 403; T2 admin BYPASS_ROLES 200; T3 clinician-with-rel via episode 200; T4/T5/T6 mirror for PATCH scribe/session/:id). Scope discipline: ~20 LOC diff (down from original ~40 LOC estimate) per pre-kickoff inventory freeze. Stop-rule observed: if any endpoint reveals a module-level refactor need, file S2 B-11 follow-up and do NOT let BUG-276 become a letters-module rewrite. Change class: risky (patient-safety surface; service-layer gate affects all callers of regenerateSection/editSection; potentially changes 403 response shape for non-relationship cases). |
| R-FIX-SCRIBE-CONSENT-REVOCATION | apps/api/migrations/20260701000032_scribe_consents_revocation.ts | present | `revoke_reason` | BUG-274: patient-revocation signal for active scribe session. Pre-fix, BUG-035 gate ran at session open only; a patient who revoked consent mid-session (typical scenario: clinical deterioration, patient says "stop recording") could not halt the active scribe — the WebSocket chunk handler continued pushing audio into session.chunks, transcribePartial continued sending to Whisper, and processAmbientAudio (on session stop) fed the full recording including post-revoke content to the LLM. llm_interactions rows were written with content captured AFTER consent was withdrawn. This is an APP 12 violation (consent withdrawal must be respected in-flight, not just at next session) and an ACHS Standard 4 failure (patient partnership — patient said stop, system kept recording). Fix has 3 layers: (1) DB migration 20260701000032 adds scribe_consents.revoked_at (timestamptz NULL) + revoked_by (uuid NULL FK staff) + revoke_reason (text NULL) columns + partial index scribe_consents_active_idx ON (id) WHERE revoked_at IS NULL for O(1) hot-path lookup. (2) New POST /api/v1/scribe/consent/:id/revoke endpoint — idempotent (re-calling on already-revoked consent returns 200 with no state change, no double-audit), patient-relationship gated, cross-tenant protected (404 not 403 to avoid leakage), writes audit_log AMBIENT_NOTE_RECORDING_REVOKED synchronously BEFORE returning (R1 invariant: forensic record precedes any failable downstream work). (3) verifyRecordingConsent blocks on revoked_at IS NOT NULL with new HttpError 403 CONSENT_REVOKED + new shared helper isConsentRevoked with 2-second in-process cache (SCRIBE_CONSENT_REVOKE_CACHE_TTL_MS) + explicit markConsentRevokedInCache invalidation from the revoke endpoint so the next chunk check picks up the new state without waiting for TTL. (4) scribeStreaming.ts per-chunk revocation enforcement via enforceRevokedConsent helper called at: binary-frame message handler BEFORE pushing to session.chunks (so post-revoke audio never accumulates); start of transcribePartial (catches race between chunk handler scheduling and transcribe execution); stop handler (race resolution — revoke wins; client sees {type:'revoked'} not {type:'result'}). On-revoke invariants (all hold atomically per session): state → STOPPED BEFORE further processing; session.chunks cleared; session.partialTranscript cleared; ws.send({type:'revoked', reason:'RECORDING_REVOKED'}) then ws.close(4403, 'RECORDING_REVOKED'); sessions.delete(sessionId). isConsentRevoked fail-closed for non-existent rows (safer interpretation: "row gone" = "no consent"). New SCRIBE_WS_CLOSE.RECORDING_REVOKED = 4403 (shares HTTP-family code with FORBIDDEN; reason string disambiguates). StreamingSession.consentId field added — bound at session-open from validated StartMessage. New AuditAction union member 'AMBIENT_NOTE_RECORDING_REVOKED'. Tests: apps/api/tests/integration/scribeConsentRevocation.int.test.ts (8 tests — T1 revoke endpoint writes columns + audit; T2 verifyRecordingConsent throws CONSENT_REVOKED after revoke; T3 idempotent second revoke = 200 + no duplicate audit; T4 cross-tenant revoke = 404, no leak; T5 non-existent = 404; T6 isConsentRevoked reflects cache invalidation; T7 non-existent consent fail-closed = revoked; T8 audit row written BEFORE any downstream cleanup). Migration snapshot regenerated. Registry anchor is `present`-type on the revoke_reason column — renaming the column fails the guard. Change class: risky (migration + new endpoint + new shared helper + per-chunk polling + WebSocket close-code + AuditAction extension; patient-revocation is patient-safety-critical surface). |
| R-FIX-LLM-BYPASS-ROLE-AUDIT | apps/api/src/shared/writeLlmAccessBypassAudit.ts | present | `LLM_ACCESS_BYPASS_ROLE` | BUG-279: bypass-role visibility for LLM endpoints. Pre-fix, when admin/superadmin used /clinical-ai, /agent, /patient-summary, /referral-letter, or /search, the BUG-036 requirePatientRelationship gate short-circuited for BYPASS_ROLES (authGuards.ts:26) so the 403 path was never taken; forbiddenAccessAudit (middleware that fires on 403 responses) never wrote a row; bypass-role usage of LLM endpoints — the highest-impact misuse vector — left no forensic trail. Fix: dedicated helper writeLlmAccessBypassAudit(params) in shared/ that writes audit_log.action='LLM_ACCESS_BYPASS_ROLE' when caller role is in BYPASS_ROLES (no-op otherwise). Each of the 5 gated endpoints invokes the helper EXPLICITLY (not as generic middleware — per user-feedback rule, hidden middleware magic is rejected; explicit call-sites at each handler make the audit visible at the source). recordId=patientId for patient-scoped calls (nil-UUID substituted via audit.ts:128 fallback when patientId is null, original preserved in new_data._recordRef); new_data carries {endpoint, feature, role, patientId}. New AuditAction union member 'LLM_ACCESS_BYPASS_ROLE' added to utils/audit.ts type. Tests: apps/api/tests/integration/llmAccessBypassAudit.int.test.ts (6 tests — T1/T2 superadmin + admin unit-level helper tests write rows; T3 clinician role is no-op; T4 missing staffId emits WARN + no row; T5 end-to-end POST /scribe/search as admin produces audit row with endpoint='/scribe/search' + feature='scribe-search' + recordId=patientId; T6 regular clinician /scribe/search 403 from BUG-036 gate → no LLM_ACCESS_BYPASS_ROLE row). Wired at 5 call sites: /llm/clinical-ai (enhanced + direct paths) + /llm/agent + /scribe/patient-summary + /scribe/referral-letter + /scribe/search. Audit volume: bypass-role LLM calls are infrequent (admin/superadmin rarely invoke LLM surfaces); existing audit_log indexing + retention unchanged. Non-blocking from the request's perspective — `await`ed on success path so the audit row is durable when client receives 200; inner try/catch logs write failure via Pino error without throwing (same pattern as forbiddenAccessAudit). Change class: standard (new shared helper + 5 explicit call-site edits + 1 enum addition; no schema, no migration). |
| R-FIX-LLM-INTERACTIONS-IMMUTABILITY | apps/api/migrations/20260701000031_llm_interactions_immutability.ts | present | `llm_interactions_prevent_mutation` | BUG-286: llm_interactions tamper-evidence (two-layer defence mirroring BUG-039's audit_log pattern). Pre-fix, app_user had UPDATE+DELETE+TRUNCATE grants on llm_interactions — BUG-037 made it append-only at the APP layer (recordLlmInteraction only INSERTs, no UPDATE/DELETE paths in src/) but DB grants still permitted mutation. A compromised app process or a future regression introducing an UPDATE call could rewrite or delete AI-interaction history, destroying training-data provenance + forensic trail. Layer A: migration 20260701000031 REVOKES UPDATE/DELETE/TRUNCATE on llm_interactions from app_user (INSERT + SELECT preserved — INSERT for recordLlmInteraction, SELECT for BUG-280 contamination scan + training-export audit). Layer B: BEFORE UPDATE + BEFORE DELETE triggers invoking llm_interactions_prevent_mutation() PL/pgSQL function which unconditionally raises 'llm_interactions is append-only (BUG-286 tamper-evident)'. Triggers fire for ALL roles including dbAdmin (no SECURITY DEFINER) — a compromised owner role or future DDL tool issuing UPDATE via owner is still blocked. Separate function name (NOT reusing audit_log_prevent_mutation) so ops debugging an llm_interactions RAISE sees the correct table name in the error — reduces MTTI. Idempotent: CREATE OR REPLACE + DROP IF EXISTS + guarded REVOKE. Down path drops triggers + function + re-grants (CAB required for prod rollback; forward-fix preferred). Tests: apps/api/tests/integration/llmInteractionsImmutability.int.test.ts (6 tests: T1/T2 app_user retains SELECT + INSERT; T3/T4 app_user blocked from UPDATE + DELETE; T5/T6 dbAdmin UPDATE + DELETE raise canonical error). Pre-existing `trg_llm_interactions_updated_at` BEFORE UPDATE trigger becomes dead code (never fires because BUG-286's _no_update trigger alphabetically precedes it + raises); non-blocking — cleanup deferred as operational debt, not a live bug. Standard: HIPAA 164.312(b) integrity; APP 11.1; AI Ethics — immutable AI interaction history supports post-hoc accountability + training-data provenance. Registry anchor pins the function name `llm_interactions_prevent_mutation` so a future rename fails the guard. Change class: risky (migration + GRANT changes + new trigger functions). |
| R-FIX-CLOZAPINE-DISCIPLINE-BARRIER | apps/api/migrations/20260701000030_clozapine_prescriber_discipline_barrier.ts | present | `clozapine_titration_days_prescriber_discipline_check` | BUG-293: AHPRA prescriber-discipline barrier (BUG-040 → BUG-292) extended to the clozapine tables. Clozapine is the highest-safety-risk psychotropic in Signacare's prescribing surface (agranulocytosis / FBC weekly monitoring for the first 18 weeks) — a non-prescribing discipline attributed as clozapine prescriber is a worse failure than BUG-040 because the drug risk profile is worse. Two columns carry prescriber attribution: (1) `clozapine_titration_days.prescribed_by_staff_id` (canonical BUG-040 column name, NULLABLE) — written when a specific titration day's mg is entered; (2) `clozapine_registrations.prescriber_staff_id` (clozapine-specific column name, NULLABLE) — written when a patient is enrolled on clozapine. Registration IS the prescribing-initiation moment so the column is semantically equivalent to prescribed_by_staff_id and gets the same barrier. Layer A (app): clozapineService migrated from raw (clinicId, actorId) to AuthContext-first signatures (CLAUDE.md §13); requirePrescribingDiscipline(auth) at top of createRegistration, updateRegistration, and NEW upsertTitrationDay service method (previous controller went direct to repository; now routes through service). recordBloodResult is DELIBERATELY NOT gated — recording an ANC/WBC blood result is a monitoring action, not a prescribing decision; nurses and allied-health legitimately record them. Any treatment decision triggered by the result (dose change, clozapine cessation) flows through updateRegistration / upsertTitrationDay which ARE gated. Layer B (DB) new migration 20260701000030 creates TWO PL/pgSQL functions (one per table — keeps future per-table customisation decoupled): `clozapine_titration_days_prescriber_discipline_check()` and `clozapine_registrations_prescriber_discipline_check()`. Both call the SSoT `is_prescribing_eligible_discipline(TEXT)` from BUG-040's migration — no allow-list duplication across three tables (patient_medications, prescriptions, clozapine_*). Both raise `'prescriber discipline "%" not authorised to prescribe (BUG-040)'` verbatim for consistent operator-facing error text. Triggers: BEFORE INSERT + BEFORE UPDATE OF <col> on each table. Fires for ALL roles including dbAdmin (no SECURITY DEFINER) — defence against compromised owner-role or direct SQL. Both columns NULLABLE so trigger short-circuits on NULL (legacy / transient paths; app-layer is authoritative for real attribution). Controller migration: 7 call sites updated (listClozapineRegistrations, getClozapineRegistration, createClozapineRegistration, updateClozapineRegistration, listBloodResults, recordBloodResult, upsertTitrationDay) — each constructs buildAuthContext(req[, patientId]) and passes through to the service. upsertTitrationDay controller previously bypassed service → direct repository call; this fix routes it through the service so Layer A gate runs before the repository write (was Layer B only pre-fix). Tests: apps/api/tests/integration/clozapineDisciplineBarrier.int.test.ts (11 tests — T1/T2/T3 allowed disciplines INSERT titration_days, T4/T5 blocked disciplines INSERT raise canonical error, T6 UPDATE-swap raises, T7 NULL legacy path succeeds, T8/T9 registrations INSERT allow/block on prescriber_staff_id column, T10 app-layer upsertTitrationDay 403, T11 app-layer createRegistration 403). Migration snapshot regenerated and committed. Fix-registry anchor pins the titration_days function name so a future rename fails the guard. Complements BUG-292's trigger + CLAUDE.md §7.3.1 documented in BUG-290. Change class: risky (schema migration + service-signature migration + 2 tables touched + controller routing change). |
| R-FIX-PRESCRIPTIONS-DISCIPLINE-BARRIER | apps/api/migrations/20260701000029_prescriptions_prescriber_discipline_barrier.ts | present | `prescriptions_prescriber_discipline_check` | BUG-292: AHPRA prescriber-discipline barrier (BUG-040) extended to the prescriptions (eScript) table — the legally-submitted record integrated with NPDS. Pre-fix, prescriptionService had NO requirePrescribingDiscipline call and NO trigger on the prescriptions table; a compromised psychologist account with prescription:create permission could submit an eScript to NPDS with zero barrier. Fix applies two-layer defence mirroring BUG-040 exactly: Layer A (app) prescriptionService migrated from raw (clinicId, actorId) to AuthContext-first signature (CLAUDE.md §13 mandates new service code use AuthContext); requirePrescribingDiscipline(auth) called at top of create + runSafeScriptCheck + submitErx + cancel (any function that writes or state-transitions a prescription). Layer B (DB) new migration 20260701000029 creates prescriptions_prescriber_discipline_check() PL/pgSQL function (mirrors BUG-040's patient_medications_ variant; separate function per CLAUDE.md §7.3.1 naming rule `<table>_prescriber_discipline_check`) and attaches BEFORE INSERT + BEFORE UPDATE OF prescribed_by_staff_id triggers calling the SSoT is_prescribing_eligible_discipline(TEXT) function from BUG-040's migration (no allow-list duplication — one function serves both tables). Trigger raises 'prescriber discipline "%" not authorised to prescribe (BUG-040)' verbatim for consistent operator-facing error text across prescribing surfaces. Fires for ALL roles including dbAdmin (defence against compromised owner role). Scope discipline: AuthContext migration cascaded from prescriptionService (6 call sites: listByPatient/getById/create/runSafeScriptCheck/submitErx/cancel) to endocrinology (insulinService.createNewVersion + 1 endocrinologyRoutes call site) because insulinService internally calls prescriptionService.create and .cancel — clean end-to-end AuthContext path, no half-migration. Total 9 call sites updated. Schema difference vs BUG-040: prescriptions.prescribed_by_staff_id is NOT NULL (unlike patient_medications which allows NULL); trigger still short-circuits on NULL but column constraint makes that path unreachable for prescriptions — integration test T7 verifies NOT NULL blocks rather than the trigger. Tests: apps/api/tests/integration/prescriptionsDisciplineBarrier.int.test.ts (9 tests). Migration snapshot regenerated and committed. Fix-registry anchor pins the function name so a future rename fails the guard. Complements CLAUDE.md §7.3.1 documented in BUG-290. Change class: risky (schema migration + service-signature migration + 2-feature touch). |
| R-FIX-PRESCRIBING-TABLE-CHECKLIST | CLAUDE.md | present | `### 7\.3\.1 Prescribing-tables checklist` | BUG-290: preventive documentation to prevent regression class where future tables with `prescribed_by_staff_id` land without the BUG-040 discipline-barrier trigger. §7.3.1 in CLAUDE.md codifies: (1) BEFORE INSERT + BEFORE UPDATE OF prescribed_by_staff_id triggers are mandatory; (2) must call the shared SSoT function `is_prescribing_eligible_discipline(slug)` defined in migration 20260421000003; (3) naming convention `<table>_prescriber_discipline_check()`; (4) reject message matches BUG-040's verbatim text so operators see consistent errors; (5) allow NULL prescribed_by_staff_id (legacy/transient); (6) trigger fires for dbAdmin too (defence against compromised owner role); (7) service-layer `requirePrescribingDiscipline(auth)` guard at every write path (Layer A + Layer B defence in depth); (8) integration-test pattern mirrors BUG-040's 10-test shape; (9) discipline allow-list pinned to psychiatry / general-practice / nurse-practitioner with explicit CAB-decision requirement for future additions. Canonical trigger-creation SQL skeleton included. Registry anchor is `present`-type (the section must exist in CLAUDE.md); any reviewer can grep-cite the rule when rejecting a migration. Change class: non-risky (documentation only — no code/schema/migration). Complements BUG-292 + BUG-293 landing in the same Wave A-3 cluster. |
| R-FIX-SCRIBE-DISCLAIMER-ENVELOPE | apps/api/src/features/llm/scribeDraftSurface.ts | present | `disclaimer: CLINICAL_AI_DISCLAIMER` | BUG-284: /scribe/patient-summary + /scribe/referral-letter response envelopes now carry the canonical `disclaimer: CLINICAL_AI_DISCLAIMER` field alongside the existing `isAiDraft: true` flag. Pre-fix both endpoints returned only the boolean flag, breaking parity with /suggest + /clinical-ai + /agent (BUG-038 shipped the canonical disclaimer there). Frontend had to hard-code the warning string; auditors couldn't cross-reference AI-source signals across the scribe + non-scribe LLM surfaces. Import added from shared/llmDisclaimer.ts (single source of truth — any future wording change flows through both surfaces atomically). Tests: apps/api/tests/integration/scribeDisclaimerEnvelope.int.test.ts (3 tests: T1 /patient-summary envelope carries disclaimer; T2 /referral-letter envelope carries disclaimer; T3 verbatim-string assertion prevents future inline-literal drift). Router ownership later moved from scribeRoutes.ts into scribeDraftSurface.ts during clinical-context extraction; this row now tracks the new owning file so the verified fix remains truthful. BUG-038 existing 4-test regression suite re-verified green. Change class: standard — additive field; no breaking change; no shared infra. |
| R-FIX-LLM-INTERACTIONS-RETROSPECTIVE-AUDIT | apps/api/scripts/audit-llm-interactions-contamination.ts | present | `export \{ hasRelationship \}` | BUG-280: before BUG-036, 5 LLM endpoints accepted patientId without requirePatientRelationship. Any clinician could have triggered LLM calls against any patient — producing llm_interactions rows where user_id lacked a sanctioned care-relationship to patient_id at created_at. This fix is a CAPABILITY delivery, not a data mutation: a read-only TypeScript audit script that identifies CANDIDATE contamination rows for OAIC Notifiable Data Breach assessment. The audit does NOT establish reportability — separate human + legal review determines whether any candidate event constitutes an 'eligible data breach likely to result in serious harm' per the OAIC NDB scheme (R3 absorption: wording precision prevents "BUG-280 fixed" being misread as "no contamination occurred"). Script uses dbAdmin (bypasses RLS for cross-tenant reconciliation) + SET statement_timeout='10min'. Relationship model mirrors requirePatientRelationship exactly (R3 absorption — original 3-check plan would have falsely flagged BYPASS_ROLES + break-glass paths as contamination): (1) BYPASS_ROLES scalar match on staff.role ∈ {superadmin, admin}; (2) active approved break-glass session (staff_id, clinic_id matched, approved_at <= T, expires_at > T, revoked_at NULL-or-after-T) — session-wide, not patient-bound; (3) open/active/admitted episode (non-deleted, created_at <= T) with staff as primary_clinician_id OR key_worker_id; (4) active team assignment (patient_team_assignments × staff_team_assignments via org_unit_id, both is_active, staff.end_date NULL-or-after-T); (5) appointment attendance (attendance_status != 'removed', non-deleted appointment, created_at <= T). Every check uses EXISTS() for planner short-circuit (R1 absorption — essential against appointment_attendees cardinality). Output appended (not overwritten) to docs/audit-2026-04-19/findings/BUG-280-llm-interactions-contamination.md: per-clinic / per-staff / per-patient breakdowns + CSV of candidate rows. Dry-run by default; AUDIT_WRITE_REPORT=true enables findings append. Tests: apps/api/tests/integration/auditLlmContamination.int.test.ts (7 integration tests seeding real tables — A1 open episode → PASS; A2 no relationship → FAIL; A5 episode created AFTER T → FAIL time-boundary; A6 team end_date before T → FAIL; A7 active break-glass → PASS session-wide; A8 expired break-glass → FAIL; A9 appointment attendance → PASS). require.main === module guard so `import` from tests doesn't accidentally call main() + process.exit. Residual limitations documented at top of script: staff.role CURRENT state (no role_history), break_glass clinic_id match assumption, is_active snapshot. State: fixed with resolution_note="audit_capability_shipped; findings_pending_ops_run; NDB_assessment_pending". |
| R-FIX-CLINICAL-NOTES-CONSENT-FK | apps/api/migrations/20260701000028_clinical_notes_consent_fk.ts | present | `clinical_notes_consent_id_fk` | BUG-273: the link between a clinical_notes row and the scribe_consents row authorising its recording lived only in audit_log (AMBIENT_NOTE_RECORDING_STARTED with record_id=consentId + new_data->>'patientId' matching the note's patient_id) — fragile multi-column temporal JOIN, unenforceable at the DB layer. This fix adds `clinical_notes.consent_id uuid REFERENCES scribe_consents(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT` via a zero-downtime 5-step pattern (R3 pre-exec absorption): (1) ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN nullable; (2) CREATE INDEX clinical_notes_consent_id_idx partial WHERE consent_id IS NOT NULL; (3) ADD CONSTRAINT ... NOT VALID (no existing-rows scan); (4) ops-run chunked backfill at apps/api/scripts/backfill-clinical-notes-consent-id.ts OUTSIDE the migration transaction (statement_timeout=5min, work_mem=64MB, 1000-row chunks, DISTINCT ON tie-break on most recent audit row, preflight ABORT if any note has >1 candidate in 10-minute window); (5) ops runs ALTER TABLE ... VALIDATE CONSTRAINT in maintenance window (brief ShareUpdateExclusiveLock, uses FK index, no table scan). Backfill report appended to docs/audit-2026-04-19/findings/BUG-273-backfill-report.md (counts: resolved / unresolved_ai_draft / legacy_not_ai_draft / total) converting "best effort" to "measured effort" (R2). Schema reality correction during execution: original plan's audio_storage_key-based backfill join abandoned — that column doesn't exist on clinical_notes; switched to temporal-proximity on (clinic_id, patient_id, created_at) with audit_log.new_data->>'patientId' as the match. Application write path: apps/api/src/features/llm/llmRoutes.ts /ambient-note INSERT now carries consent_id: dto.consentId directly (no audit-log join needed). Zod validates dto.consentId as UUID at route entry; verifyRecordingConsent has passed before the INSERT; FK NOT VALID still enforces on new writes (tests F2/F3 prove). Four-layer defence: Zod + verifyRecordingConsent + INSERT + FK. 5 integration tests (F1 column+FK; F2 bogus consent → 23503; F3 DELETE referenced consent → 23503 RESTRICT; F4 /ambient-note e2e → consent_id direct on saved note; F5 preflight ambiguity SQL flags synthesised >1-match scenarios). Regressions green: BUG-035 (9 tests) + BUG-275 (3 tests). NOT NULL enforcement deferred to BUG-315 (S2 B-11) until legacy rows cleaned up. Change class: risky (migration + FK + production write-path + 24/7 compatibility). Registry anchor `present`-type pins the FK name — any rename fails merge. |
| R-FIX-PHI-LOG-GUARD | scripts/guards/check-log-no-phi.ts | present | `check-log-no-phi` | BUG-269: PHI_FIELDS in apps/api/src/utils/phiFields.ts is a static allow-list consumed by runtime redactPhi + pino.redact.paths. Any key NOT in the set passes through unredacted. BUG-216's checkSchemaPhiDrift WARNs on boot but runs only per process start and only surveys DB schema, not actual logger call sites. New CI guard scripts/guards/check-log-no-phi.ts walks the TypeScript AST of every apps/api/src/**/*.ts file and, for each logger.(info/warn/error/debug/fatal/trace/child) call, inspects inline object-literal arguments (arg[0] and arg[1] for pino's two-arg shape). Each key is checked against the same PHI regex as checkSchemaPhiDrift — `/phone|email|address|medicare|ihi|hpii|dva|ndis|prescriber|dob|given|family|preferred|nok|pbs|narrative|complaint|diagnosis|lookup|blind_?index/i`. A key that MATCHES the regex AND is NOT in PHI_FIELDS AND is NOT in scripts/guards/log-phi.allowlist fails the guard. Spread elements + identifier arguments + nested identifiers under PHI-shaped keys emit WARNs (informational — the guard can't prove safety statically; runtime redactPhi is the last line of defence). Nested inline object literals are scanned recursively. Guard loader strips single-line // comments from phiFields.ts before regex extraction (an apostrophe in a comment like "They're" was initially breaking the paired-quote extractor). Allowlist (3 entries with rationale): `suspects` (column label in drift log), `familyId` (session/refresh-token family ID, not patient family), `emailSent` (boolean status flag, not email value). Baseline at commit time: 0 failures, 34 WARNs across 467 files. Implementation caught 4 real findings: `familyId` (classified allowlist), `emailSent` (classified allowlist), `adminEmail` × 2 sites (classified as PHI — added `adminEmail` + `admin_email` to PHI_CATEGORY_EMAIL since admin email is OAIC personal info same as any email). Tests: apps/api/tests/unit/logPhiGuard.test.ts (5 tests covering novel-PHI-key violation, known-PHI pass-through, allowlist bypass, non-PHI non-match, adminEmail classification). BUG-216 regression suite (loggerRedaction.test.ts, 8 tests) still PASS. Limitations documented in guard header: static-analysis only (Object.assign + computed keys invisible); first-level keys only for identifier-valued properties; regex coupling with checkSchemaPhiDrift (change in one = change in both); pino two-argument metadata scanned but only when literal. Change class: standard (CI guard only + 2 PHI_FIELDS additions). |
| R-FIX-AMBIENT-CATCH-PASSTHROUGH | apps/api/src/features/llm/llmRoutes.ts | absent | `next\(new Error\(msg\)\)` | BUG-275: the /ambient-note handler's fallback catch branch wrapped all non-matched errors in `new Error(msg)` before calling `next()`, destroying class identity, stack, cause chain, and custom fields (`.code` / `.status` / `.details`) that the global errorHandler's duck-typed branch (shared/errors.ts:100-114) relies on. Pre-fix, a downstream `processAmbientAudio` throw of a custom error with `.status=418` + `.code='X'` rendered as generic 500 + 'INTERNAL_ERROR' instead of 418 + 'X'. Fix: replace `next(new Error(msg))` with `next(err)` so the original instance passes through unchanged. Scope expanded during implementation to 2 additional sites found by the new CI guard: (a) `apps/api/src/features/llm/llmTrainingRoutes.ts:259` same pattern in model-create handler — same 1-line fix; (b) `apps/api/src/features/patients/zitaviSyncRoutes.ts:110` synthesised bad-gateway condition — upgraded to `new HttpError(502, 'ZITAVI_BAD_GATEWAY', ...)` since the condition is genuinely new (not an unwrapped caught error). CI guard `scripts/guards/check-no-next-new-error.ts` rejects the exact `next\(new Error\(` literal shape in `apps/api/src/features/**`, comment-aware so prose mentions don't false-positive. Registry anchor is `absent`-type (the pattern must NOT exist in this file) — any re-introduction fails the merge gate. Guard scope documented as a narrow safety rail, not a comprehensive ban: indirect wrapping (`const e = new Error(x); next(e)`) + typed-class wrapping are NOT caught (both legitimate in many cases). Tests: apps/api/tests/integration/ambientNoteErrorPassthrough.int.test.ts (3 — T1 HttpError regression, T2 unknown Error falls to next(err), T3 duck-typed error with .status+.code renders original 418 not generic 500 — T3 is the load-bearing structural assertion). Pre-exec reviews absorbed: guard scope note in header; T2 rewritten to assert passthrough not response shape. |
| R-FIX-AMBIENT-CATCH-PASSTHROUGH-TRAINING | apps/api/src/features/llm/llmTrainingRoutes.ts | absent | `next\(new Error\(msg\)\)` | BUG-275: mirror of R-FIX-AMBIENT-CATCH-PASSTHROUGH for the llmTrainingRoutes model-create handler. |
| R-FIX-AMBIENT-CATCH-PASSTHROUGH-ZITAVI | apps/api/src/features/patients/zitaviSyncRoutes.ts | absent | `next\(new Error\(` | BUG-275: the zitaviSyncRoutes bad-gateway path used `next(new Error('Invalid response from Zitavi gateway'))`. Upgraded to `new HttpError(502, 'ZITAVI_BAD_GATEWAY', ...)` so the global errorHandler maps it to a specific 502 response instead of generic 500. |
| R-FIX-SCRIBE-WEBSOCKET-CONSENT-GATE | apps/api/src/mcp/scribeStreaming.ts | present | `verifyRecordingConsent\(auth\.clinicId` | BUG-272: WebSocket /ws/scribe accepted any upgrade, trusted client-supplied staffId/clinicId from the first 'start' message, and ran processAmbientAudio (Whisper + LLM) on unconsented audio. OAIC Privacy APP 3.1 breach waiting for any future persistence wiring; until then, `processAmbientAudio` still runs on unconsented recordings. Fix mirrors BUG-035 (HTTP /ambient-note) on the WS surface via TWO gates + state machine. **Gate 1 (upgrade-time auth)**: server.on('upgrade') intercepts handshake, parses Authorization: Bearer or signacare_access cookie, jwt.verify via config.jwt.accessSecret; on failure writes raw 'HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized\\r\\n\\r\\n' to socket and destroys before handshake completes. AuthContext attached to the ws instance so handleControlMessage reads it directly — client-supplied staffId / clinicId are IGNORED. **Gate 2 (session-open)**: first message must be {type:'start', patientId, consentId} per StartMessageSchema Zod. Zod fail → ws.close(4422). Then requirePatientRelationship + verifyRecordingConsent + writeAuditLog all run inside withTenantContext(auth.clinicId, ..., auth.staffId) so app.clinic_id is set and RLS-enforced queries work. On throw → ws.close(4403, errCode). On success → writeAuditLog AMBIENT_NOTE_RECORDING_STARTED with recordId=consentId and newData.transport='websocket' (forensic replay distinguishes WS from HTTP). **State machine** PENDING_START → ACTIVE → STOPPED. Audio frames in PENDING_START are dropped silently at frame handler (invariant documented: no audio reaches Whisper before consent is verified). Second 'start' on ACTIVE → close 4409 SESSION_ALREADY_OPEN. **PENDING_START idle timeout** SCRIBE_WS_SESSION_OPEN_TIMEOUT_MS=5000ms (absorbed from pre-exec Review 1) — socket that doesn't send start → close 4408 so resources aren't leaked. Close codes defined as SCRIBE_WS_CLOSE constants block at module top (absorbed from Review 2): 4401/4403/4408/4409/4422 chosen to parallel HTTP status for cross-layer observability. Reused primitives (SSoT, no duplication): verifyRecordingConsent (shared/recordingConsent.ts), requirePatientRelationship (shared/authGuards.ts), writeAuditLog (utils/audit.ts), withTenantContext (shared/tenantContext.ts), jwt.verify (via config.jwt.accessSecret). Tests: apps/api/tests/integration/scribeWebSocketConsent.int.test.ts (8 — W1/W2 upgrade-auth 401; W3 malformed Zod 4422; W4/W5 consent gate 4403 CONSENT_REQUIRED / CONSENT_EXPIRED; W6 relationship gate 4403 NO_PATIENT_RELATIONSHIP; W7 happy path session_started + audit row transport:websocket; W8 pending-start timeout 4408). BUG-035 regression suite (9 tests) re-verified green. Pre-execution reviews: 2 critique reviews confirmed both tactical calls (server.on('upgrade') over verifyClient; HTTP-parallel close codes) and added 3 substantive absorptions + 2 clarifications. Non-goals: Whisper+LLM pipeline unchanged; DB persistence not wired (gate in place for future); per-frame consent re-verification not needed (TTL covers session); Ping/Pong heartbeats filed as BUG-314 (S3 B-11). Residual: browser-side MediaRecorder captures audio before WS opens (frontend scope); session-hijack post-upgrade (global token-revocation bug in backlog); per-clinic TTL drift → BUG-310. Change class: risky (shared WS surface + patient-safety + OAIC NDB-reportable). |
| R-FIX-LOGGER-ERR-SERIALIZER-PHI | apps/api/src/utils/sanitizeErrForLogging.ts | present | `export function sanitizeErrForLogging` | BUG-267: PostgreSQL constraint violations embed column values into err.message — `Key (medicare_number)=(2123456789)`, `Key (clinic_id, medicare_number_lookup)=(uuid, <hash>)` (composite blind-index — REAL prod shape), `Failing row contains (...)` (check constraints). pino.stdSerializers.err passed these through verbatim; any logger.error({ err }) on a DB-constraint path leaked PHI to journald (OAIC Notifiable Data Breach reportable). Fix: replace stdSerializers.err with custom sanitizeErrForLogging that calls stdSerializers.err internally for shape preservation, then runs sanitizeString() over .message / .stack / .detail / .hint / .table / .constraint. Three regex anchors: (1) `/Key \\(([^)]+)\\)=\\((.*?)\\)/g` — split captured column list on comma, redact entire value block if ANY column ∈ PHI_FIELDS (composite-aware; Pre-exec Review 1 absorbed — single-column `[a-z_]+` would have missed `Key (c1, c2)=(v1, v2)`). (2) `/Failing row contains \\((.*?)\\)/g` → unconditional redact (fail-closed; can't map positional values to columns without query introspection). (3) `/column "[a-z_]+"/g` → preserved (ops-useful, no value). All regexes non-greedy + bounded by `)` → ReDoS-safe. Runs on error paths only (no hot-path impact). Returns a sanitised COPY; err is never mutated (catch-blocks higher up see the original). **L4 clinical-safety absorbed** — added PHI_CATEGORY_BLIND_INDEX (medicare_number_lookup / ihi_number_lookup / dva_number_lookup + camelCase) to cover the ACTUAL composite unique constraint shapes (patients_medicare_lookup_uniq on (clinic_id, medicare_number_lookup) etc.); SHA-256 HMAC is a deanonymisation vector per OAIC. Extended checkSchemaPhiDrift regex to include `lookup|blind_?index` tokens so future blind-index columns surface. Fixed 3 prod console.error leaks that bypassed pino: server.ts uncaughtException + unhandledRejection handlers now call sanitizeErrForLogging before console.error; ocr/worker.ts bootstrap catch → logger.fatal({ err }); correspondence/correspondenceController.ts createLetter catch → logger.error({ err }). **L5 architecture absorbed** — extracted PHI_CATEGORY_* + PHI_FIELDS + redactPhi from utils/logger.ts into new leaf module utils/phiFields.ts to break the logger↔sanitizeErrForLogging circular (depcruise `no-circular` at error severity would have rejected). Renamed shared/phiEncryption.ts `PHI_FIELDS` → `ENCRYPTED_PHI_COLUMNS` to end the name collision (different sets, different purposes: encryption-at-rest vs logger redaction). logger.ts re-exports for backward compat. madge --circular reports 0 new cycles (3 pre-existing in integrations/escript unchanged). Layer 2 of defence in depth: Layer 1 per-handler err.code catches, Layer 3 redact.paths + redactPhi() structured-field redaction (BUG-216). Tests: apps/api/tests/unit/sanitizeErrForLogging.test.ts (13 — U1 medicare_number; U2 given_name; U3 clinic_id NON-PHI preserved; U4 Failing row parens replaced; U5 column "family_name" preserved; U6 stack frame structure preserved; U7 non-PG unchanged; U8 detail+hint redacted; U9 composite clinic_id+medicare_number; U10 REAL composite blind-index `Key (clinic_id, medicare_number_lookup)=(...)`; U11 ihi_number_lookup composite; U12 dva_number_lookup composite; extra mutation-guard + code preservation). apps/api/tests/integration/loggerErrSerializer.int.test.ts (2 — I1 real PG 23505 on patients_medicare_lookup_uniq composite blind-index path → hash REDACTED; I2 FK violation on non-PHI UUID → value SURVIVES). Residual: BUG-312 (S3 B-11 — downgraded from S2 after fixing prod paths; remaining seed/observability sites); BUG-313 (S3 B-11 — third-party loggers); novel PG error-message formats conservative-by-design residual. Change class: risky (shared infra + reportable-privacy-incident layer). |
| R-FIX-CSRF-SLIDING-REFRESH | apps/api/src/middleware/csrfMiddleware.ts | present | `redis\.expire\(\`csrf:\$\{token\}\`, CSRF_TOKEN_TTL_SECONDS\)` | USER-A.2 (A-5-USER Sub-cluster A): sliding TTL refresh for CSRF tokens. Pre-fix the `/auth/csrf` endpoint issued a token with a fixed 60-min Redis TTL (`redis.set('csrf:<token>', '1', 'EX', 3600)` at `authRoutes.ts:28`); every subsequent mutation validated the token via `redis.get` but did NOT extend the TTL. A clinician actively working past the 60-min mark hit `CSRF_INVALID` on the next write even though the auth session was still alive — user item #6 "Appointment create — CSRF token expired" repro. Post-fix: after a successful `redis.get` returns a non-null value, call `redis.expire('csrf:<token>', 3600)` to reset the TTL. Token lifetime now tracks active use, not issue time; the real idle-out guard is the auth session timeout (sessionIdleMiddleware). No schema change, no route signature change, no security downgrade — an XSS that steals a live token still can't extend beyond 60 min of inactivity. Graceful-degradation on Redis outage unchanged (fall through to Custom Header Check). Change class: standard (one-line behaviour change inside an existing guarded try/catch; no new dependency, no new surface). Guard anchor pins the `redis.expire` call — removal fails the merge gate. |
| R-FIX-NOTE-SNIPPETS-EPISODE-SCOPED | apps/api/src/features/clinical-notes/noteSnippets.ts | present | `if \(episodeId\) q\.where\(\{ episode_id: episodeId \}\)` | USER-A.3 (A-5-USER Sub-cluster A — S0 PHI-isolation): `buildOutcomesSnippet` previously queried `outcome_measures` WHERE only `clinic_id` + `patient_id`, returning the latest 5 scores across ALL episodes. When a clinician composed a clinical note inside Episode B and invoked Alt+Shift+O to insert outcome measures, Episode A's scores leaked into the draft — APP 11 segmentation concern / cross-episode PHI into the note-in-progress. Fix: `buildOutcomesSnippet(clinicId, patientId, episodeId)` accepts `episodeId: string | null`; when present applies `.where('episode_id', episodeId)` so the query is scoped to the active episode. `buildNoteSnippets` accepts a 4th optional `episodeId` param; the clinical-notes snippets route `/patient/:patientId/snippets` reads `req.query.episodeId` and threads it through. Frontend: `useSnippetMacros` options extended with `episodeId?: string | null`; `NoteEditor` accepts + forwards `episodeId`; `ClinicalNotesPanel` passes the panel's `episodeId` through. Header text toggles from "latest 5" → "latest 5, this episode" when scoped so the clinician sees the scope they're getting. Backward compatible: omit `episodeId` and behaviour matches pre-fix (pre-episode intake flows still work). No schema change. Change class: risky (PHI-isolation fix — spans route signature + hook + editor + panel; the composed snippet surface is a new per-episode contract). Guard anchor pins the `.where({ episode_id: episodeId })` line. Follow-up: audit every other query on `outcome_measures` / `rating_scales` for the same class of bug (tracked as A-5-CERT.δ episode-scoping guard script). |

| R-FIX-BUG-351-ACCESS-ADMIN-STAFF-JOIN | apps/api/src/shared/authGuards.ts | present | `whereNotIn\('s\.role', Array\.from\(OPERATIONAL_ONLY_ROLES\)\)` | BUG-351 (S1 PHI-isolation): close post-facto drift on the clinic-scoped nominated/delegated admin bypass. Pre-fix `requirePatientRelationship` Check 0 matched `clinics.{nominated,delegated}_admin_staff_id` by staff_id alone — a demoted / deactivated / soft-deleted admin retained clinic-wide PHI bypass until a superadmin re-nominated. Discovered by the L4 clinical-safety review on 0.5.C. Fix: Check 0 JOINs `staff as s` on `(s.id = c.nominated_admin_staff_id OR s.id = c.delegated_admin_staff_id)`, then requires `s.id = auth.staffId AND s.is_active = true AND s.deleted_at IS NULL AND s.role NOT IN OPERATIONAL_ONLY_ROLES`. Three drift vectors closed: (1) demotion to receptionist/readonly, (2) deactivation (is_active=false), (3) soft-delete (L3-absorb-1: the canonical offboarding path per staffRepository SSoT). Knex nested `on` + `.orOn` verified to emit `INNER JOIN "staff" "s" ON ("s"."id" = "c"."nominated_admin_staff_id" or "s"."id" = "c"."delegated_admin_staff_id")` via `.toString()`; NULL columns fall out of INNER JOIN naturally. Per-request cost: one extra PK-indexed JOIN, <1ms. Tests: 3 new scenarios appended to clinicalAccessRbac.int.test.ts (T15 demote, T16 deactivate, T17 soft-delete — L3-absorb-1); 17/17 suite total PASS on live Postgres; T15/T16/T17 FAILED on pre-fix baseline (TDD confirmed). Gate: L1 tsc 0 errors, eslint clean on touched files, 14/16 CI guards PASS, L3 REJECT (missing soft-delete filter) → absorbed → PASS, L4 PASS (2 follow-ups registered: BUG-352 clinic_id drift + BUG-353 force-logout on demotion), L5 PASS (DB cascade trigger registered as BUG-354 follow-up). Change class: risky (auth guard rewrite + SQL JOIN shape change + drift-class contract expansion). Anchor pins the `whereNotIn` + Array.from idiom — rename of OPERATIONAL_ONLY_ROLES or removal of the role check fails the merge gate. Follow-ups: BUG-352 (S2, clinic_id drift on staff transfer), BUG-353 (S3, force-logout on demotion), BUG-354 (S2, DB cascade trigger for belt-and-braces Layer B). |
| R-FIX-POWER-SETTINGS-ACCESS-ADMINS | apps/api/src/features/power-settings/powerSettingsRoutes.ts | present | `PowerAccessAdminsSchema` | Phase 0.5.C: Power Settings backend + UI for per-clinic nominated + delegated access administrators. Two new endpoints on `/power-settings`: GET `/clinics/:clinicId/access-admins` (any admin/superadmin may view — returns `{ nominatedAdmin, delegatedAdmin }` with staff details) + PUT `/clinics/:clinicId/access-admins` (superadmin ONLY — Zod body `{ nominatedAdminStaffId: uuid \| null, delegatedAdminStaffId: uuid \| null }`). PUT validates: (a) actor identity resolvable (401 UNAUTHENTICATED fallback — L4-absorb-1 unattributable-audit guard), (b) both slots distinct when both set (400 VALIDATION_ERROR), (c) both candidates are active staff members of THIS clinic (400 — pre-DB check matching the Layer-A/B defence-in-depth pattern from 0.5.A), (d) neither is operational-only (role NOT IN OPERATIONAL_ONLY from `@signacare/shared`). On success: persists new values on `clinics`, writes audit_log row with full `{nominated,delegated}_admin_staff_id` diff (oldValues from pre-write snapshot). Frontend: new `AccessAdminsPanel` tab on PowerSettingsPage (superadmin-only tab) with clinic picker + two staff Selects + distinct-validation banner + save button; uses `powerSettingsKeys.accessAdmins(clinicId)` + `powerSettingsKeys.clinicStaff(clinicId)` factory keys. `ModuleAccessMatrix` gains a read-only fallback banner driven by `readOnlyReason` state which is set when saveMut.onError catches 403 `ACCESS_SETTINGS_READ_ONLY`. Regression test: apps/api/tests/integration/clinicAccessAdminsPowerSettings.int.test.ts — 5 scenarios (P1 superadmin GET returns current state + identities; P2 superadmin PUT writes + audit row verified in audit_log; P3 same-staff-both-slots → 400 VALIDATION_ERROR; P4 operational-only staff → 400 VALIDATION_ERROR with /operational\|receptionist/ regex; P5 non-superadmin PUT → 403 via direct-minted JWT + Redis idle bootstrap). 5/5 PASS on live Postgres; 0 new regressions across the wider integration suite; two previously-flaky suites (letterScribeSessionPatientRelationship, llmDisclaimerEnvelope) now GREEN. Gate: L1 tsc 0 errors (api + web); eslint clean on new files (1 pre-existing `any` in clinic_modules createTable unchanged); 15/16 CI guards PASS (2 pre-existing FAILs on naming-conventions + silent-catches — outside touched files); L3 + L4 + L5 agents. Anchor pins the Zod schema name — rename fails the merge gate. |
| R-FIX-REQUIRE-PATIENT-RELATIONSHIP-TWO-RAIL | apps/api/src/shared/authGuards.ts | present | `WITH RECURSIVE ancestor_units` | Phase 0.5.B: two-rail access-model rewrite of `requirePatientRelationship`. Pre-0.5.B the guard role-bypassed for `{superadmin, admin}` via BYPASS_ROLES + only matched plain `staff_team_assignments` at the exact team (no role-assignments + no hierarchy). Per PART 12 of the master plan, superadmin + generic admin must NO LONGER auto-pass clinical-data access (superadmin = cross-clinic settings operator, admin = clinic-level role; only explicit nominated/delegated admins bypass). Post-0.5.B the guard (a) removed BYPASS_ROLES; (b) added a clinic-scoped bypass via `clinics.nominated_admin_staff_id` + `delegated_admin_staff_id` joined on the patient's clinic_id (can't leak across tenants); (c) replaced the single-team JOIN with a WITH RECURSIVE CTE (`ancestor_units`) that walks UP `org_units.parent_id` from the patient's team, matching the caller via either `staff_team_assignments` or `staff_role_assignments` at any ancestor level — captures team leaders with role-only attachments AND facility/region-level executives with ancestor assignments. Episode + appointment branches unchanged. Added two new guards: `requireClinicalAccessRole(auth)` (sync, blocks OPERATIONAL_ONLY_ROLES = {receptionist, readonly}) and `requireAccessSettingsAuthority(auth, clinicId)` (async, passes for superadmin OR nominated/delegated admin of target clinic; otherwise 403 ACCESS_SETTINGS_READ_ONLY). `staffSettingsRoutes.ts` PUT/POST/DELETE on module-access / role-assignments / team-assignments swap `requireRole('admin','superadmin')` for an `adminWrite` composite middleware that chains admin role + requireAccessSettingsAuthority. GET routes keep `admin` (any admin can view). `patientRoutes.ts` requireClinicalRole swapped from inline string check to the shared guard. `packages/shared/src/permissions.ts` NEW — `hasClinicalAccess` / `isOperationalOnly` / `isCrossClinicOperator` helpers for FE/BE parity. `Sidebar.tsx` hides Dashboard/Patients/Tasks/Clinical-Lists/Workspace for superadmin (settings-only) and strips Clinical-Lists for operational-only roles. Test-bootstrap: `_helpers.ts` sets seeded admin as `nominated_admin_staff_id` on `loginAsAdmin` so 7 existing adjacent test suites that relied on the old admin bypass (ambientNoteConsentGate, ambientNoteErrorPassthrough, clinicalNotesConsentFK, letterScribeSessionPatientRelationship, llmAccessBypassAudit, llmDisclaimerEnvelope, llmRoutesPatientRelationshipGate) remain green. Tests: apps/api/tests/integration/clinicalAccessRbac.int.test.ts (14 scenarios — T1 clinician+plain-team → pass; T2 team-leader+role-only → pass NEW; T3 facility-executive cascade → pass NEW; T4 superadmin → 403 NEW formerly bypassed; T5 generic admin → 403 NEW formerly bypassed; T6/T7 nominated/delegated → pass NEW; T8 receptionist → CLINICAL_ACCESS_DENIED; T9 wrong-team clinician → 403; T10/T11 nominated/delegated write → pass; T12 generic admin write → ACCESS_SETTINGS_READ_ONLY NEW; T13 generic admin view → pass; T14 superadmin cross-clinic write → pass). 14/14 PASS; adjacent clinicAccessAdminsSchema + 7 recovered suites all green; zero new regressions. Gate: L1 tsc 0 errors (api+web+shared); eslint 0 errors on my new files (authGuards/authConstants/permissions/clinicalAccessRbac — pre-existing `any`s in other files in staffSettingsRoutes / patientRoutes remain as BUG-347-adjacent debt); 14/16 CI guards PASS (2 pre-existing FAILs outside touched files); L3 code-reviewer-general / L4 clinical-safety-reviewer / L5 architecture-reviewer parallel review. Change class: risky (auth/ rewrite + two new guards + schema-dependent bypass + 7 route handlers re-gated). BUG-348 catalogue entry pending in 0.5.D. Anchor pins the recursive CTE — removal or rename fails the merge gate. |
| R-FIX-CLINIC-ACCESS-ADMIN-COLUMNS | apps/api/migrations/20260423000001_clinic_access_admins.ts | present | `clinics_access_admin_same_clinic_check` | Phase 0.5.A (PART 12 foundation commit): migration adds `clinics.nominated_admin_staff_id` + `clinics.delegated_admin_staff_id` as nullable UUID FK to staff(id) ON DELETE SET NULL, with indexes, CHECK constraint `clinics_access_admin_distinct` (rejects identical non-NULL values in both slots), and cross-clinic containment trigger `clinics_access_admin_same_clinic_check` (L3-absorb-1: raises ERRCODE 23514 if either admin FK references a staff member whose clinic_id != this clinic's id — Layer-B defence, paired with Layer-A Zod in 0.5.C). Preflight introspection log emits the count of clinics awaiting nomination so ops knows how many need superadmin attention before non-superadmin admins regain access-settings write capability (strict transition per plan PART 12 clarification #3). ON DELETE SET NULL rationale: staff deletion must not cascade to tenant; superadmin re-nominates before ambient authority returns. Taxonomy annotations per §12.4: check_constraint (CHECK), function_create (plpgsql trigger fn), trigger_create × 2 (BEFORE INSERT + BEFORE UPDATE OF nominated/delegated), introspection (preflight), drop_* symmetric in down(). Tests: apps/api/tests/integration/clinicAccessAdminsSchema.int.test.ts (5 scenarios — T1 column shape + FK metadata + indexes, T2 CHECK constraint accepts/rejects combinations, T3 snapshot-freshness pinning, T4 L3-absorb-1 behavioural ON DELETE SET NULL proof, T5 L3-absorb-1 cross-clinic containment rejection). 5/5 PASS on live Postgres; 0/5 on pre-migration baseline (TDD confirmed). Gate: L1 tsc + eslint clean; 14/16 CI guards PASS (2 pre-existing FAILs outside touched files); L3 REJECT→absorb→PASS; L4 PASS (RLS preserved, strict-NULL default is clinically safe); L5 PASS (5 standards met; bidirectional clinics↔staff FK not a cycle). Anchor pins the trigger function name — rename or removal fails the merge gate. Change class: risky (schema + triggers + foundation for PART 12 access model). | R-FIX-NOTE-GENERATE-LETTER-BUTTON | apps/web/src/features/clinical-notes/components/ClinicalNotesPanel.tsx | present | `Generate Letter` | USER-E.1 (A-5-USER Sub-cluster E): Generate-Letter button on the saved-note view. Pre-fix the "generate letter from note" feature existed (correspondenceRoutes `/generate-from-note` endpoint + GenerateLetterFromNoteButton component in the LetterComposer) but required the clinician to navigate to /correspondence first, then pick the note back out. User reported "No ability to generate letters from saved notes" — the feature was undiscoverable from the note surface. The CorrespondenceTab + LetterComposer consumption of `fromNoteId` to prefill composer body is USER-E.2 follow-up (spec at docs/specs/user-e-letters-from-notes.md). | R-FIX-NOTE-SNIPPETS-EPISODE-COMPLETE | apps/api/src/features/clinical-notes/noteSnippets.ts | present | `buildRiskSnippet\([^)]*episodeId` | USER-A.3 absorb-1 (Wave A-5-USER Sub-cluster A): complete the episode-scoping fix across ALL episode-bearing snippet builders + schema-drift fix on risk builder. Pre-absorb (commit 3d3bac1): only buildOutcomesSnippet was episode-scoped; L3+L4+L5 review panel returned REJECT/BLOCK with "same S0 PHI class remains on buildRiskSnippet + buildMedicationsSnippet". risk_assessments and patient_medications both carry `episode_id` (verified via schema-snapshot + psql). Post-absorb: (1) buildRiskSnippet accepts `episodeId: string \| null` and applies `.where({ episode_id: episodeId })` when set; (2) buildMedicationsSnippet same shape; (3) buildNoteSnippets threads episodeId to both. Additionally, buildRiskSnippet had PRE-EXISTING schema drift — it selected `severity` (doesn't exist; real column is `overall_risk_level`) and `domains` (doesn't exist at all). The outer `.catch(() => emptySnippet(t))` silently swallowed the pg column-does-not-exist error so every Alt+Shift+R returned `**risk:** _unable to load — see logs_`. Fixed: builder now selects overall_risk_level + the 5 boolean risk-class flags (suicide_risk / self_harm_risk / harm_to_others_risk / absconding_risk / vulnerability_risk) and joins the flipped ones into a comma-separated domains string. pathology / vitals / allergies explicitly NOT scoped — their tables lack `episode_id` (patient-level clinical reality: labs / vitals / allergies span episodes). Inline comments at the buildNoteSnippets switch document why each snippet type is or isn't scoped. Regression test: apps/api/tests/integration/noteSnippetsEpisodeScoping.int.test.ts (5 HTTP-level tests — S1 outcomes B-only, S2 risk B-only + column-drift proof, S3 meds B-only, S4 legacy omit returns both, S5 boundary 400 on malformed UUID). 5/5 PASS; adjacent clinicalNotesConsentFK suite remains green. Change class: risky (5 files + defence-in-depth across HTTP route + hook + editor + panel + amend modal). Anchor pins the risk-builder's episodeId param. |

| R-FIX-BUG-281-LLM-AUTHCONTEXT | apps/api/src/mcp/aiEnhancer.ts | present | `async function loadPatientContext\(auth: AuthContext, patientId: string\)` | BUG-281 (S1) — LLM service-layer AuthContext §13 migration. Post-fix: all three take AuthContext as first param; each calls `requirePatientRelationship(auth, patientId)` before any patient-data read. `handleMcpRequest` + `handleToolCall` take AuthContext; generic `if (a?.patientId) requirePatientRelationship(auth, a.patientId)` gate at top of handleToolCall covers every patient-scoped tool uniformly. `runAgent` uses Node AsyncLocalStorage (`agentAuthStore`) to propagate auth through DIRECT_QUERIES + callAndFormat + runLlmAgentLoop without cascading 40+ handler signatures; `currentAuth()` helper throws hard if called outside `runAgent` — regression guard. Removed the silent-degrade catch in `enhancedGenerate` (previously `/* continue without enrichment */`) — relationship rejection now bubbles to the caller as 403. Tests: 4/4 HTTP-level (T1 cross-clinic MCP → rejection, T2 same-clinic MCP → positive, T3 agent cross-clinic → 403, T4 AsyncLocalStorage regression proof). Flake ×3 zero. Adjacent 47/50 green (3 pre-existing failures on letterScribeSessionPatientRelationship verified unrelated). L4 non-blocking note: transient Redis/PG failure now surfaces as 403 (previously silent-degrade) — separate 503-vs-403 distinction tracked as BUG-281b follow-up. Anchor pins the migrated function signature. |

| R-FIX-BUG-358-TRIGGER-AUDIT-GUARD | .github/scripts/check-trigger-has-audit-row.sh | present | `check-trigger-has-audit-row \(BUG-358 invariant\)` | BUG-358 — mechanical CI enforcement of the "every state-mutating DB trigger function must emit audit_log or fail-closed RAISE" invariant that BUG-354 retroactive L4/L5 (2026-04-23) flagged as convention-only. The guard scans `apps/api/migrations/*.ts` (excluding the §12.4.6 squashed baseline), extracts every `CREATE [OR REPLACE] FUNCTION ... RETURNS TRIGGER` with dollar-quote-tag-aware body tracking (handles `$$`, `$fn$`, `$do$`), selects the LATEST up() definition per function name (CREATE OR REPLACE-aware — critical because migration 20260423000005 defines `clinics_access_admin_slot_integrity` without audit and 20260423000007 replaces it WITH audit; the guard checks the 007 definition). Asserts each body contains either `INSERT INTO audit_log` OR `RAISE EXCEPTION/NOTICE/WARNING`. Allowlist: `audit_trigger_fn` (the canonical audit writer — self-audit recursion hazard), `set_updated_at` (BEFORE UPDATE timestamp helper, no security state), `staff_can_see_specialty` (SQL RLS helper, returns boolean). 8 trigger functions scanned on current repo; 0 violations. TDD fixture: synthetic `fixture_silent_mutator` with UPDATE-without-audit body correctly flagged with file:line + 3-option remediation hint (add audit INSERT / add RAISE / add to allowlist). Fail-closed gates (RAISE) are treated as audit-equivalent — BUG-322 tracks adding audit_log rows for rejection triggers as a separate concern (autonomous-transaction pattern). Anchor pins the script's self-announcement line — file rename/removal fails the merge gate. |

| R-FIX-BUG-360-LLM-TRAINING-AUDIT-OBSERVABLE | apps/api/src/features/llm/llmRoutes.ts | present | `kind: 'tier_5_9_audit_write_failed'` | BUG-360 absorb (L4 BLOCK) — Tier 5.9 two-person training-export approval audit writes are regulator-facing (TRAINING_EXPORT_REQUESTED/APPROVED/REJECTED/DOWNLOADED). Pre-fix: `.catch(() => {})` silently discarded failures. Post-fix: REQUESTED/APPROVED/REJECTED log at `level: error` with structured `kind: 'tier_5_9_audit_write_failed'` tag + actorId + recordId + decision context so on-call gets paged on sustained failures. DOWNLOADED is stricter — it `await`s the audit write inside a try/catch, and on failure returns 503 `AUDIT_WRITE_FAILED` and DOES NOT stream the PHI-bearing JSONL (ISO 14971 risk-control: the export cannot proceed without a regulator-facing audit attestation). Anchor pins the structured-tag literal — any rename or removal fails the merge gate. |
