| R-FIX-BUG-356-SCRIBE-WS-REVOKE-CHECK | apps/api/src/mcp/scribeStreaming.ts | present | `revoked = await isUserRevokedAfter\(payload\.id, payload\.iat\)` | BUG-356 absorb (L4 Rule 2/3) — scribe WebSocket is an AI clinical-data surface. Pre-absorb: `authenticateUpgrade` only verified JWT signature/exp; a demoted clinician kept streaming audio into patient records for up to 60 min. Fix: `isUserRevokedAfter` check after `jwt.verify`, before `buildAuthFromJwtPayload`. On revoke → 401 + socket.destroy. Fail-open on Redis error with structured `kind: 'jwt_blacklist_fail_open', surface: 'scribe_ws'` tag so ops alerts filter on sustained Redis degradation (L5 Std 2). Anchor pins the variable assignment — removing the call fails the merge gate. |
| R-FIX-BUG-356-FHIR-INTROSPECT-REVOKE-CHECK | apps/api/src/integrations/fhir/smartAuth.ts | present | `revoked = await isUserRevokedAfter\(row\.user_id as string, issuedAtSec\)` | BUG-356 absorb (L4 Rule 6) — FHIR introspect is a PHI-egress authorisation surface per SMART-on-FHIR RFC 7662. Pre-absorb: `/fhir/auth/introspect` returned `active: true` even for tokens whose subject staff was demoted/deactivated after issuance. Fix: `isUserRevokedAfter(row.user_id, issuedAt)` check after `oauth_access_tokens` lookup + expiry checks, before returning `active: true`. On revoke → return `active: false`. Fail-open on Redis error with structured `kind: 'jwt_blacklist_fail_open', surface: 'fhir_introspect'` tag. Anchor pins the variable assignment. NOTE: regression-protection is via this anchor rather than an integration test because the introspect handler queries `smart_apps` through the RLS-aware `db` proxy without an established `app.clinic_id` tenant context — dbAdmin-seeded test rows are hidden from db-enforced reads (pre-existing architectural gap, catalogued as BUG-363 candidate if it becomes a blocker). |
| R-FIX-BUG-356-STAFF-SESSIONS-REVOKE | apps/api/src/features/staff/staffService.ts | present | `new AuthRepository\(\)\.revokeSessionsForStaff\(id\)` | BUG-356 absorb (L5 Std 3) — L5 retrospective flagged that blacklisting access tokens alone is insufficient: `staffService.updateStaff` did NOT revoke `staff_sessions` rows, so a demoted user's 7-day-TTL refresh token kept minting fresh access tokens with `iat > blacklist_timestamp` → `isUserRevokedAfter` returned false → user remained fully authenticated until they happened to log out. Fix: after blacklistAllUserTokens, also call `AuthRepository.revokeSessionsForStaff(id)` which sets `revoked_at = now()` on every active session row; `authService.refresh` already reads `revoked_at` and rejects stale refresh tokens (existing pre-check). Wrapped in try/catch + logger.error so refresh-token revocation failure doesn't block the update but IS observable. Anchor pins the class instantiation + method — renaming either fails the merge gate. |
| R-FIX-BUG-356-SESSION-REVOKED-AUDIT-LOG | apps/api/src/features/staff/staffService.ts | present | `action: "SESSION_REVOKED_BY_STATE_CHANGE"` | BUG-356 absorb (L4 Rule 5) — L4 flagged that forensic review had no audit trail linking a revocation to the outer staff UPDATE event. Fix: emit a structured `audit_log` row with `action='SESSION_REVOKED_BY_STATE_CHANGE'`, `table_name='staff'`, `record_id=staffId`, `old_data={role, is_active}` (pre-update), `new_data={role, is_active, trigger: 'role_changed'|'active_changed'}` (post-update). `AuditAction` union extended in `apps/api/src/utils/audit.ts`. `writeAuditLog` SSoT-swallows audit failures per existing policy. Anchor pins the action-string literal. Integration test T6 verifies the audit row lands with correct old_data/new_data after PUT /staff/:id. |
| R-FIX-BUG-356-BLACKLIST-TTL-FROM-CONFIG | apps/api/src/middleware/jwtBlacklist.ts | present | `userRevokeTtlSeconds\(\)` | BUG-356 absorb (L5 Std 3 SSoT) — pre-absorb the blacklist-key TTL was a hardcoded `86400 * 7` in `blacklistAllUserTokens`. Since the key must cover the full refresh-token lifetime (otherwise a demoted user's refresh can mint a fresh access token AFTER blacklist-key expiry with `iat > blacklist_timestamp`), the TTL must match `config.jwt.refreshTtlDays`. Pre-absorb both values were 7 days but drift was a latent bug. Fix: helper function `userRevokeTtlSeconds() = config.jwt.refreshTtlDays * 86400`. Anchor pins the helper. |
| R-FIX-BUG-356-AUTHMIDDLEWARE-JWT-BLACKLIST-WIRED | apps/api/src/middleware/authMiddleware.ts | present | `isUserRevokedAfter\(payload\.id, payload\.iat\)` | BUG-356 (S1 security, structural prerequisite for BUG-353) — wires the previously-dead `jwtBlacklist.ts` module into the request-time auth chain. Before this fix, `authMiddleware` only verified JWT signature + exp, so a staff demoted / deactivated / soft-deleted retained their elevated permissions for up to 60 min (the `JWT_ACCESS_TTL_MINUTES` default at config.ts:21). The existing `blacklistAllUserTokens` / `isUserRevokedAfter` functions in `middleware/jwtBlacklist.ts` had zero consumers — grep confirms. Fix: `authMiddleware` now calls `isUserRevokedAfter(payload.id, payload.iat)` AFTER `jwt.verify` and BEFORE user hydration; returns `401 SESSION_REVOKED` when the staff has a revoke-all timestamp newer than the token's iat. Fails-open on Redis error (existing `isUserRevokedAfter` returns `false` on reject) + `logger.warn` so degradation is observable. Refactor keeps the outer (req,res,next) signature but uses a `continueAuthChain(...)` helper so the new revoke check can be async. Companion `blacklistAllUserTokens(id)` call added to `apps/api/src/features/staff/staffService.ts` `updateStaff` after the repo write commits, gated on `role` or `isActive` having changed vs `existing`. Wrapped in try/catch + `logger.error` so blacklist failure never blocks the update. BUG-353 remains open but `blocked_by` can now be cleared (structural prerequisite in place). Integration tests at `apps/api/tests/integration/bug356AccessTokenRevocation.int.test.ts` — 4 scenarios (T1 direct-blacklist-call-invalidates, T2 role-change-invalidates, T3 deactivation-invalidates, T4 benign-given-name-change-does-NOT-invalidate). TDD evidence: pre-fix 3 FAIL / 1 PASS (T1: 200 instead of 401; T2: 200; T3: 403 stale-permission instead of 401); post-fix 4/4 PASS. Adjacent 44 tests green across authBoundaries (13), clinicalAccessRbac (17), accessAdminSlotIntegrityTrigger (6), accessAdminSlotIntegrityTriggerAudit (3), clinicAccessAdminsPowerSettings (5). Flake ×3 zero. Anchor pins the middleware call — removing or renaming fails the merge gate. |
| R-FIX-BUG-354-AUDIT-LOG-EMISSION | apps/api/migrations/20260423000007_access_admin_trigger_audit_log.ts | present | `'ADMIN_SLOT_CLEARED_BY_TRIGGER'` | BUG-354 forward-fix (L4 BLOCK + L5 REJECT absorption on shipped commit 72ab65f). Retroactive clinical-safety + architecture reviews flagged the original trigger as silently mutating security-critical state (NULLing `clinics.{nominated,delegated}_admin_staff_id`) with zero audit trail — HIPAA §164.312(b) + OWASP ASVS v4 §7.1.3 violation. Fix: `CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION clinics_access_admin_slot_integrity()` — same function name so the trigger binding is preserved without a DROP/CREATE cycle. New body emits one `audit_log` row per affected clinic BEFORE the slot UPDATE, with `action='ADMIN_SLOT_CLEARED_BY_TRIGGER'`, `table_name='clinics'`, `record_id=clinic.id`, and `new_data` JSONB carrying `{staff_id, reason, slot}`. Reason is one of 'role_demoted' / 'deactivated' / 'soft_deleted' / 'clinic_transferred'. Slot is 'nominated' / 'delegated' / 'both'. `SECURITY DEFINER` so row-owning role isn't required to have audit_log INSERT privilege; `EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS` swallows audit failure so slot-clearing is never blocked (mirrors `audit_trigger_fn` at baseline.ts:96-120). `AuditAction` TS union extended with new member in `apps/api/src/utils/audit.ts`. down() restores the pre-audit-log body from commit 72ab65f. Integration tests at `apps/api/tests/integration/accessAdminSlotIntegrityTriggerAudit.int.test.ts` — 3 scenarios (T1 demote-emits-audit reason=role_demoted slot=nominated; T2 deactivate-emits-audit reason=deactivated slot=delegated; T3 benign-given-name-update-emits-no-audit). TDD evidence: pre-fix 2/3 FAIL (expected ≥1 audit row, got 0), post-fix 3/3 PASS. Adjacent 6+5+17 tests green. Flake ×3 zero. Anchor pins the action-string literal — removing or renaming it fails the merge gate. |
| R-FIX-BUG-366A-AZURE-KEYVAULT-BACKEND | apps/api/src/config/secrets.ts | present | `export async function loadSecretsAsync` | BUG-366a (S2, Azure staging deploy blocker) — adds `azure_keyvault` as a fourth `SECRETS_BACKEND` alongside env/json/file. Resolver uses `@azure/identity` DefaultAzureCredential (managed identity on Azure App Service / Container Apps / VMs; falls back to AZURE_CLIENT_ID+SECRET+TENANT_ID for local dev; falls back to `az login` CLI). Dynamic import of `@azure/keyvault-secrets` + `@azure/identity` keeps both SDKs out of the non-Azure boot path. Every `SENSITIVE_KEYS` env var name maps to its kebab-lower form (`JWT_ACCESS_SECRET` → `jwt-access-secret`) via `envKeyToVaultSecretName`; parallel `Promise.all` lookup of every sensitive key. `SecretNotFound` errors are silently skipped so a partial vault population matches the existing json / file backend contract. Other errors (auth, network, 403) throw fatal. Called from `apps/api/src/index.ts` BEFORE `await import('./server')` so `process.env` is populated before `config.ts` validates JWT secrets at module load. `apps/api/src/server.ts` skips its own sync `loadSecrets()` call when backend is `azure_keyvault` (already done in index.ts). Unit tests at `apps/api/tests/secretsAzureKeyVault.test.ts` — 9 scenarios: env-key kebab mapping, AZURE_KEYVAULT_URL required, SecretClient + DefaultAzureCredential wiring, every SENSITIVE_KEY looked up, return values written to process.env, SecretNotFound silently skipped, auth errors thrown, non-azure backend delegates to sync `loadSecrets()`, allowlist enforcement (PORT/NODE_ENV never overwritten). TDD evidence: pre-fix 9/9 FAIL with `loadSecretsAsync is not a function`, post-fix 9/9 PASS, flake ×3 zero. Anchor pins the function export — removing or renaming fails the merge gate. |
| R-FIX-BUG-366A-AZURE-KEYVAULT-SENTINEL-KEY | apps/api/src/config/secrets.ts | present | `export function envKeyToVaultSecretName` | BUG-366a — the env-to-kebab mapping helper is exported for unit-test coverage. Anchor pins the export so renaming the helper breaks the test suite loudly rather than silently changing the Key Vault secret-name convention. |
| R-FIX-BUG-366A-INDEX-ASYNC-BOOTSTRAP | apps/api/src/index.ts | present | `loadSecretsAsync` | BUG-366a — index.ts is now the async boot entry. When `SECRETS_BACKEND=azure_keyvault` it awaits `loadSecretsAsync()` BEFORE `await import('./server')` so `config.ts` sees populated `process.env`. For env/json/file backends the old sync behaviour still runs inside server.ts unchanged. Anchor pins the async import site. |
| R-FIX-BUG-366A-SERVER-BYPASS-SYNC | apps/api/src/server.ts | present | `getSecretsBackendName\(\) === 'azure_keyvault'` | BUG-366a — server.ts guards its sync `loadSecrets()` call to skip when index.ts has already resolved Azure Key Vault. Without this guard, boot with `SECRETS_BACKEND=azure_keyvault` would double-run the resolver (once async in index.ts, once sync in server.ts which throws by design). Anchor pins the backend-guard check. |
| R-FIX-BUG-366A-ENV-PRODUCTION-TEMPLATE | apps/api/.env.production.template | present | `SECRETS_BACKEND=azure_keyvault` | BUG-366a — documented template showing the Azure App Service `@Microsoft.KeyVault(SecretUri=...)` reference syntax alongside the native `SECRETS_BACKEND=azure_keyvault` resolver option, plus Azure-specific values for DB_PORT (5432), PGSSLMODE=require, REDIS_URL (port 6380 TLS), CORS_ORIGIN, WebAuthn origin, NASH mTLS cert path, and Application Insights. Anchor pins the canonical env var name. |
| R-FIX-BUG-366A-BOOT-ENTRY-PACKAGE-START | apps/api/package.json | present | `"start": "node -r dotenv/config dist/src/index\.js"` | BUG-366a L5 absorb — production `npm start` MUST route through `dist/src/index.js` (async bootstrap) so `SECRETS_BACKEND=azure_keyvault` resolves vault secrets BEFORE `server.ts` / `config.ts` module-load Zod validation. Reverting to `dist/src/server.js` silently skips the async path → app boots with partial env → Zod rejects optional secrets with warnings → PHI encryption can end up disabled. Anchor pins the index.js route. |
| R-FIX-BUG-366A-BOOT-ENTRY-PACKAGE-MAIN | apps/api/package.json | present | `"main": "dist/src/index\.js"` | BUG-366a L5 absorb — `package.json` "main" field documents the canonical entry point for any tool that resolves the package (docker builds, node -r, etc.). Must match the start script. |
| R-FIX-BUG-366A-BOOT-ENTRY-DOCKER | apps/api/Dockerfile | present | `ENTRYPOINT \["\./entrypoint\.sh"\]` | BUG-366a L5 absorb — container entry point (Azure Container Apps, AKS, any Docker-based target) MUST route through the audited startup hook, which in turn launches `dist/src/index.js` after compiled migrations. Anchor pins the Docker entrypoint contract; any rename regression fails the merge gate. |
| R-FIX-BUG-366A-BOOT-ENTRY-DEPLOY-PM2 | deploy/ecosystem.config.js | present | `script: 'dist/src/index\.js'` | BUG-366a L5 absorb — PM2 cluster-mode deploy entry routes through `dist/src/index.js`. |
| R-FIX-BUG-366A-BOOT-ENTRY-API-PM2 | apps/api/ecosystem.config.js | present | `script: 'dist/src/index\.js'` | BUG-366a L5 absorb — apps/api workspace-local PM2 entry. ALSO corrects a pre-existing rootDir mismatch (was `dist/index.js` but tsc-build emits `dist/src/index.js`). |
| R-FIX-BUG-366A-BOOT-ENTRY-ROOT-PM2 | ecosystem.config.js | present | `script: 'apps/api/dist/src/index\.js'` | BUG-366a L5 absorb — root monorepo PM2 entry. Also corrects pre-existing rootDir mismatch. |
| R-FIX-BUG-366A-PHI-KEYS-IN-SENSITIVE | apps/api/src/config/secrets.ts | present | `'PHI_ENCRYPTION_KEY'` | BUG-366a L4 BLOCK absorb (clinical-safety) — `PHI_ENCRYPTION_KEY`, `BLIND_INDEX_KEY`, `SESSION_SECRET`, `CALENDAR_ICAL_SECRET`, `SIGNACARE_LICENSE_SECRET` are now in SENSITIVE_KEYS. Their absence in the original BUG-366a commit meant `SECRETS_BACKEND=azure_keyvault` on non-App-Service hosts silently ran with `phiEncryption` off (PHI_ENCRYPTION_KEY is `.optional()` in config.ts; literal `@Microsoft.KeyVault(...)` string would land in env; Zod warns + boot continues). HIPAA § 164.312(a)(2)(iv) violation. Anchor pins the two most load-bearing PHI keys — removal fails the merge gate. |
| R-FIX-BUG-366A-REQUIRED-IN-PRODUCTION | apps/api/src/config/secrets.ts | present | `REQUIRED_IN_PRODUCTION` | BUG-366a L4 BLOCK absorb — `assertRequiredInProduction()` gate throws at resolver exit if JWT_ACCESS_SECRET / JWT_REFRESH_SECRET / DB_APP_PASSWORD / PHI_ENCRYPTION_KEY / BLIND_INDEX_KEY are missing from `process.env` when `NODE_ENV=production`. Converts silent partial-population into loud fail-fast at boot. Non-production (dev/test/CI) skips the gate so `.env` placeholders continue to work. Called from both `loadSecrets` (sync) and `loadSecretsAsync` (Azure) paths. |
| R-FIX-BUG-366A-SECRETS-RESOLVED-STRUCTURED-LOG | apps/api/src/config/secrets.ts | present | `event: 'secrets\.resolved'` | BUG-366a L4 Rule 3/6 absorb — replaced the unstructured `console.log("[secrets] backend=...")` boot diagnostic with a JSON-structured `event: 'secrets.resolved'` line carrying `{ backend, resolved: string[], skipped: string[] }` (names only — never values). Feeds App Insights / Sentry / stdout-scrape pipelines as a single machine-parseable audit row. Answers the forensic question "was PHI_ENCRYPTION_KEY actually sourced from Key Vault on boot X?" — pre-absorb there was no way to know. |
| R-FIX-BUG-187-STATEMENT-TIMEOUT | apps/api/src/db/db.ts | present | `SET statement_timeout = '30s'` | BUG-187 — every new app_user connection sets `statement_timeout='30s'` at afterCreate time. Bounds pool-exhaustion damage from stuck queries by forcing PG to cancel + release the connection after 30s. Layered with `SET LOCAL statement_timeout` in rlsMiddleware (per-transaction belt-and-braces) and enforced again at the Azure PG server-parameter level per `docs/plans/azure-staging-deployment.md §2.2`. Pins the SQL literal — any refactor that drops this timeout from afterCreate fails the merge gate. Regression test at `tests/integration/dbPoolTimeouts.int.test.ts`. |
| R-FIX-BUG-187-IDLE-IN-TX-TIMEOUT | apps/api/src/db/db.ts | present | `SET idle_in_transaction_session_timeout = '60s'` | BUG-187 — every new app_user connection sets `idle_in_transaction_session_timeout='60s'` at afterCreate. Guards against orphaned transactions (opened but never committed / rolled back) — PG terminates the connection at 60s so the backend returns to the pool. Protects the rlsMiddleware lifecycle where `res.on('finish')` or `res.on('close')` may not settle under atypical response paths. Regression test at `tests/integration/dbPoolTimeouts.int.test.ts`. |
| R-FIX-BUG-187-LOCK-TIMEOUT | apps/api/src/db/db.ts | present | `SET lock_timeout = '5s'` | BUG-187 follow-up + BUG-264 absorb (landed in the BUG-187/264/366b combined commit). Third session-level timeout closes the deadlock-wait failure mode. Any query waiting >5s for a row/table lock fails with `55P03 lock_not_available` instead of hanging indefinitely on a lock queue. Chosen to be shorter than `statement_timeout` so a lock-wait failure surfaces as a distinct error class — routes' try/catch + `next(err)` chain propagates to the error handler and returns a clean 5xx rather than a tail-latency spike. Regression test at `tests/integration/dbPoolTimeouts.int.test.ts` asserts `SHOW lock_timeout === '5s'`. TDD evidence: pre-fix `SHOW lock_timeout` returns '0' (PG default = disabled), post-fix returns '5s'. |
| R-FIX-BUG-366B-PGBOUNCER-POOL-DEFAULT | apps/api/src/db/db.ts | present | `isPgBouncer \? "20" : "50"` | BUG-366b — PgBouncer-branch `poolMax` default bumped from "5" to "20" so Playwright-load bursts (BUG-264 reproduction) don't exhaust the per-worker slice. With 4 workers × 20 = 80 client-side connections, PgBouncer still comfortably fits Azure PG D2s_v3's ~730 usable backend budget. Direct-connection default stays at "50" since that path is dev-only (Azure production MUST route through PgBouncer per `docs/plans/azure-staging-deployment.md §2.2`). Anchor pins the ternary — any refactor that reverts to "5" or hardcodes something else fails the merge gate. |
| R-FIX-BUG-366B-DB-SSL-CONFIG-HONORED | apps/api/src/db/db.ts | present | `config\.database\.ssl` | BUG-366b — the `config.database.ssl ? { ssl: {...} } : {}` branch is preserved so `DB_SSL=true` (Azure PG Flexible Server requirement, enforced by `PGSSLMODE=require`) flows through to the pg driver. Node-postgres's bundled CA chain covers Azure's PG cert out of the box — no additional cert path needed. Anchor pins the config read — any refactor that hardcodes `ssl: false` or drops the branch fails the merge gate. |
| R-FIX-BUG-262-ORU-R01-INGEST | apps/api/src/jobs/workers/hl7Worker.ts | present | `HL7_INBOUND_INGESTED` | BUG-262 (S0 clinical-safety, Wave A-1 carry-over) — the silent-drop stub that previously threw `HL7_INBOUND_NOT_IMPLEMENTED` is replaced with real ingestion: parse ORU^R01 → resolve pathology_order by (clinic_id, order_number) via `findOrderByNumberAdmin` → for each observation, map to `PathologyResultIngestDTO` and call `ingestResultFromHl7` (admin-pool variant of `ingestResult` that skips `requirePatientRelationship` because worker has no AuthContext) → mark order status 'complete' → write `HL7_INBOUND_INGESTED` audit row with `{orderNumber, resultCount, resultIds, performingLab}`. Order-not-found → `UnrecoverableError` (BullMQ DLQ, not retryable) + `HL7_INBOUND_ORDER_NOT_FOUND` audit row. Parse failure / missing order number → `UnrecoverableError`. Application-level idempotency via `findExistingResultAdmin` — same `(pathology_order_id, test_code, result_status, collection_date)` tuple returns the existing row unchanged under BullMQ retry. Critical abnormal flags trigger the existing flag-task path via `createTaskInternalAdmin` (new dbAdmin mirror of `createTaskInternal` — worker has no rlsStore transaction scope, so the RLS-proxied `db` would 55P03 on `tasks` RLS policy). Regression test at `apps/api/tests/integration/hl7InboundIngest.int.test.ts` — 6 scenarios (happy-path 2-observation ingest, order status→complete, critical→urgent-priority flag task, idempotency under retry, order-not-found→audit+UnrecoverableError, HL7_INBOUND_INGESTED audit row). TDD evidence: pre-fix 6/6 FAIL with `HL7_INBOUND_NOT_IMPLEMENTED`, post-fix 6/6 PASS, flake ×3 zero. Anchor pins the audit action literal — removing the write fails the merge gate. Out-of-scope-but-noted: MDT fan-out (`createMdtTasksForPathology`) is skipped on the HL7 path (helper uses `db()` + RLS-dependent `createTaskInternal` — needs separate admin mirror); primary critical-flag task is sufficient for clinical-safety signal. |
| R-FIX-BUG-262-TASK-ADMIN-MIRROR | apps/api/src/features/tasks/taskRepository.ts | present | `export async function createAdmin` | BUG-262 companion — dbAdmin-backed mirror of `create` for background-worker callers (HL7 inbound ingest). Same table, same fields, same tenant-isolation invariant (explicit `clinic_id` in the INSERT), different pool. Required because `db()` is RLS-proxied through AsyncLocalStorage and falls back to app_user pool in worker context with no rlsStore transaction — which 55P03-fails against the `tasks` RLS policy. Anchor pins the exported name. |
| R-FIX-BUG-262-FU-MDT-ADMIN-MIRROR | apps/api/src/features/pathology/pathologyService.ts | present | `async function createMdtTasksForPathologyAdmin` | BUG-262-FU (same-wave follow-up to BUG-262, L4 clinical-safety finding) — restores MDT fan-out parity between request-path `ingestResult` (which calls `createMdtTasksForPathology`) and worker-path `ingestResultFromHl7` (which previously skipped MDT fan-out because the helper used `db()` + RLS-proxied `createTaskInternal`). New `createMdtTasksForPathologyAdmin` uses dbAdmin + explicit clinic_id + `createTaskInternalAdmin`. Joins `patients` on `patient_id` to enforce tenant isolation since `patient_team_assignments` has no `clinic_id` column. Filters on `staff.is_active = true AND deleted_at IS NULL` so FYI tasks don't land in offboarded clinicians' queues. Critical-result safety signal now propagates to Consultant Psychiatrist + Psychiatry Registrar on the patient's MDT on the HL7 path, not just the request path. |
| R-FIX-BUG-262-UC-UNIQUE | apps/api/migrations/20260424000002_pathology_orders_unique_order_number.ts | present | `pathology_orders_clinic_order_number_unique` | BUG-262-UC (L3 follow-up to BUG-262) — `UNIQUE (clinic_id, order_number)` on `pathology_orders` closes the invariant gap that `findOrderByNumberAdmin` relied on at the app layer. Before this migration the baseline only had a non-unique index; the docstring comment claimed UNIQUE but that was false (corrected in BUG-262 commit). Now DB-enforced. Safe on existing data — `generateOrderNumber()` produces UUID-suffix order numbers that guarantee pre-existing uniqueness. Integration test at `apps/api/tests/integration/bug262UcPathologyOrderUnique.int.test.ts` — T1 duplicate INSERT raises `23505`, T2 index definition verified via `pg_indexes` covers both columns (tenant scoping). Anchor pins the constraint name. |
| R-FIX-BUG-367-PG-SQLSTATE-MAPPING | apps/api/src/shared/errors.ts | present | `PG_RETRYABLE_SQLSTATES` | BUG-367 (L4 recommendation from BUG-187/264/366b review) — `toErrorResponse` now maps PostgreSQL SQLSTATE error codes to explicit HTTP responses: `55P03 lock_timeout` → 503 LOCK_TIMEOUT_RETRY, `57014 statement_timeout` → 504 STATEMENT_TIMEOUT, `25P03 idle_in_transaction` → 503 IDLE_IN_TX_TIMEOUT, `40001 serialization_failure` → 503 SERIALIZATION_FAILURE, `40P01 deadlock_detected` → 503 DEADLOCK_DETECTED, `57P03 cannot_connect_now` → 503 CANNOT_CONNECT_NOW. Before this fix, these fell through the duck-typed `status+code` branch (PG errors have `code: string` but no `status: number`) and became opaque 500 "Internal server error" — the clinician saw a generic failure with no signal to retry. Now surfaces retry semantics with `details.sqlstate` + `details.retryable` for structured logging / Sentry tagging. Unit tests at `apps/api/tests/errorsPgMapping.test.ts` — 10 scenarios covering all 6 retryable codes + non-retryable fall-through + HttpError precedence + lowercase normalisation. |
| R-FIX-BUG-373-PROTOBUFJS-CRITICAL | package-lock.json | absent | `"protobufjs":\s*\{\s*"version":\s*"7\.5\.4"` | BUG-373 (audit 2026-04-24 F-006, escalated S1→S0 by Wave 6c after CRITICAL protobufjs RCE GHSA-xq3m-2v4x-88gg surfaced in prod dependency chain via OpenTelemetry exporter). `npm audit fix --package-lock-only` resolved transitively: protobufjs 7.5.4 → 7.5.5 (CRITICAL RCE closed), basic-ftp 5.2.0 → 5.3.0 (HIGH CRLF-inject closed), dompurify 3.3.3 → 3.4.1 (4 moderate XSS closed), fast-xml-parser 5.5.8 → 5.7.1 (moderate injection closed), nodemailer 8.0.4 → 8.0.5 (moderate SMTP-CRLF closed). Total vulnerabilities 13 → 7; critical+high cleared. Remaining 6 moderate + 1 low are uuid-chain gated on @azure/msal-node + bullmq + node-cron + gaxios upstream (tracked in BUG-474 with npm overrides plan). Regex asserts protobufjs 7.5.4 (the vulnerable pin) must NEVER reappear in package-lock.json. |
| R-FIX-BUG-441-PHI-FAIL-FAST-SHARED | apps/api/src/shared/phiEncryption.ts | present | `throw new PhiCryptoError\('encrypt'` | BUG-441 (Wave 6a E-silent-catch P0 SECURITY — escalated to S0 pre-staging BLOCKER). `encryptPhi` used to `catch { return plaintext }` — a wrong-length key or HSM failure silently wrote plaintext into the ENCRYPTED column. Now throws `PhiCryptoError('encrypt', <reason>, err)` after `logger.error({ err, op, reason })`. The error propagates to Express error middleware → 500, which is the correct clinician-visible outcome ("save failed — please try again") rather than silent-success-with-plaintext. Companion fix in decryptPhi prevents silent ciphertext-passthrough on auth-tag mismatch. Duplicate module at `utils/phiEncryption.ts:decryptPhi` received the same fail-fast treatment. Legacy-compat preserved: inputs that look like plaintext (no `iv:tag:ciphertext` 3-part colon format) still pass through unchanged so pre-encryption rows don't break. Tests: `tests/phi-encryption.test.ts` — 5 new failure-mode assertions (tampered ciphertext + bad format + passthrough + duplicate-module fail). |
| R-FIX-BUG-441-PHI-FAIL-FAST-UTILS | apps/api/src/utils/phiEncryption.ts | present | `from '\.\./shared/phiEncryption'` | BUG-441 (Wave 6a E-silent-catch) — `utils/phiEncryption.ts` is now a strict compatibility wrapper that re-exports the canonical fail-fast implementation from `shared/phiEncryption.ts` (no local silent fallback path remains). Consolidation remains tracked under BUG-483 (post-staging). |
| R-FIX-BUG-441-NO-SILENT-PLAINTEXT | apps/api/src/shared/phiEncryption.ts | absent | `return plaintext; // Fallback to plaintext on error` | BUG-441 regression-prevention — the original silent plaintext-fallback pattern (literal comment string) must never reappear. If a future refactor reintroduces a `return plaintext` inside an encrypt/decrypt catch block without logger + typed throw, this guard fires. |
| R-FIX-BUG-442-JWT-BLACKLIST-NO-SILENT-CATCH | apps/api/src/middleware/jwtBlacklist.ts | absent | `catch \{\s*return false; // If Redis is down` | BUG-442 (Wave 6a E-silent-catch, P0 SECURITY). Previously `isTokenBlacklisted` and `isUserRevokedAfter` each wrapped the Redis call in `try { ... } catch { return false; }` — the internal swallow short-circuited every caller's structured `.catch` handler. authMiddleware's `kind=jwt_blacklist_fail_open` warn was dead code because the promise never rejected on Redis outage. The fix removes both silent catches; Redis errors now propagate so each caller's `.catch` can emit the alertable structured log AND decide whether to fail-open (authMiddleware, per OWASP ASVS 2.8.7) or fail-closed. Regex asserts the old silent-swallow comment can never reappear. |
| R-FIX-BUG-442-IS-TOKEN-BLACKLISTED-PROPAGATES | apps/api/src/middleware/jwtBlacklist.ts | absent | `export async function isTokenBlacklisted\([^)]*\)[^{]*\{\s*try` | BUG-442 structural assertion — `isTokenBlacklisted` body must NOT begin with `try { ... }` (which implies a surrounding catch that would swallow Redis errors). The Redis call is the only statement; any error propagates naturally. If a future refactor adds internal error-handling here, this guard fires and the reviewer must re-justify it under OWASP ASVS 2.8.7. |
| R-FIX-BUG-442-IS-USER-REVOKED-PROPAGATES | apps/api/src/middleware/jwtBlacklist.ts | absent | `export async function isUserRevokedAfter\([^)]*\)[^{]*\{\s*try` | BUG-442 companion to the isTokenBlacklisted assertion. `isUserRevokedAfter` body must NOT begin with `try { ... }`. Same rationale as above — every caller has its own structured `.catch` with `kind=jwt_blacklist_fail_open`; internal swallowing defeats that observability. |
| R-FIX-BUG-368-CI-GUARD-EXISTS | scripts/guards/check-query-has-clinic-id.ts | present | `multiTenantTables\(snapshot` | BUG-368 (first audit 2026-04-24 F-001 → CRITICAL pre-staging blocker). The CI guard walks every Knex call (`db` / `dbAdmin` / `trx`) against `apps/api/src/features/**/*.ts`, cross-references the target table against the multi-tenant set from `schema-snapshot.json`, and flags any query that filters by `patient_id` without also including `clinic_id` in the same 8-line statement window. Wired into `.github/workflows/ci.yml` as `query-has-clinic-id-guard` + exposed as `npm run guard:query-has-clinic-id`. Honours an allowlist (every entry MUST cite a BUG-ID — regex-enforced) AND rejects stale entries (an allowlist entry that no longer matches any real violation is a guard FAIL, forcing the engineer to prune). Current state: 216 multi-tenant tables tracked, 36 allowlist entries each matching a live violation, all deferred to BUG-430. |
| R-FIX-BUG-368-ATTACHMENTS-CLINIC-ID | apps/api/src/features/patients/patientRoutes.ts | present | `patient_id: req\.params\.id, clinic_id: req\.clinicId, is_active: true` | BUG-368 structural assertion — the 2 patient-attachment GET endpoints (`:id/attachments` + `:id/pathology`) must filter by all three predicates. Pre-fix they had only `patient_id + is_active`; RLS-disabled maintenance paths could leak cross-clinic attachments. |
| R-FIX-BUG-368-LEGAL-ATTACHMENTS-CLINIC-ID | apps/api/src/features/patients/patientAncillaryRoutes.ts | present | `\.where\(\{ patient_id: req\.params\.id, clinic_id: req\.clinicId \}\)` | BUG-368 assertion for the `:id/legal-attachments` GET endpoint. The `.where({ patient_id, clinic_id })` shape is required — no `clinic_id` predicate was a P0 tenant-leak. (Matches the 2-arg form; the `:id/attachments` + `:id/flags` rows carry the 3-arg form with `is_active`.) |
| R-FIX-BUG-368-ALERTS-JOIN-CLINIC-ID | apps/api/src/features/patients/patientAncillaryRoutes.ts | present | `\.where\('patient_alerts\.clinic_id', req\.clinicId\)` | BUG-368 assertion for the `:id/alerts` GET endpoint (which uses qualified column syntax because it joins on alert_types + staff). Must carry the qualified `patient_alerts.clinic_id` predicate. |
| R-FIX-BUG-368-FLAGS-CLINIC-ID | apps/api/src/features/patients/patientRoutes.ts | present | `patient_id: req\.params\.id, clinic_id: req\.clinicId, is_active: true` | BUG-368 assertion for the `:id/flags` GET endpoint. Shares the shape with attachments — same 3 predicates required. |
| R-FIX-BUG-369-UNION-EXTENDED | apps/api/src/utils/audit.ts | present | `'NOTE_CREATE'` | BUG-369 (first-audit F-002, CRITICAL pre-staging blocker). The `AuditAction` union was extended with 5 new literals (`NOTE_CREATE`, `NOTE_UPDATE`, `NOTE_SIGN`, `NOTE_AMEND`, `NOTE_SOFT_DELETE`) so `writeAuditLog({action: 'NOTE_*'})` is type-safe. Prior to this fix, `clinicalNoteService` wrote NO audit rows — the only persistence was `clinical_note_versions` (the restore ledger). HIPAA §164.312(b) requires a separate forensic trail identifying WHO mutated WHICH note WHEN. This regex asserts the first of the 5 new literals is present; its presence implies the union was extended. |
| R-FIX-BUG-369-NOTE-AUDIT-CREATE | apps/api/src/features/clinical-notes/clinicalNote.service.ts | present | `action: 'NOTE_CREATE'` | BUG-369 structural assertion — `clinicalNoteService.create` MUST emit a `NOTE_CREATE` audit row after the repository write succeeds. The `writeAuditLog` call is wrapped by the function's existing promise semantics (audit failure does not throw). |
| R-FIX-BUG-369-NOTE-AUDIT-SIGN | apps/api/src/features/clinical-notes/clinicalNote.service.ts | present | `'NOTE_CROSS_AUTHOR_SIGN' : 'NOTE_SIGN'` | BUG-369 structural assertion — `clinicalNoteService.sign` MUST emit `NOTE_CROSS_AUTHOR_SIGN` (cross-author path) or `NOTE_SIGN` (same-author path) audit row. Scans the service file, identifies non-read-only methods (name does NOT begin with list/get/find), and verifies the body contains a writeAuditLog call. Wired into `.github/workflows/ci.yml` as `clinical-note-audit-log-guard`. |
| R-FIX-BUG-283-OUTBOX-MODULE-EXISTS | apps/api/src/shared/auditOutbox.ts | present | `AUDIT_OUTBOX_KEY = 'audit:outbox'` | BUG-283 (L4-escalated from BUG-369 absorb). HIPAA §164.312(b) requires audit-trail RECOVERABILITY. Pre-BUG-283 a DB insert failure in `writeAuditLog` was logged and the row was LOST. This module adds a Redis-list durable outbox (`audit:outbox`). On DB write failure, `enqueueAuditOutbox(row)` pushes the row. `drainAuditOutbox(batchSize)` retries the DB insert up to `AUDIT_OUTBOX_MAX_RETRIES` (5) per entry. `auditOutboxLength()` exposes backlog length for ops alerting. Dual-failure (DB down + Redis down) emits `kind=tier_5_9_audit_dual_write_failed` for ≤5min SLA. |
| R-FIX-BUG-283-OUTBOX-ENQUEUE-ON-DB-FAIL | apps/api/src/utils/audit.ts | present | `enqueueAuditOutbox\(row\)` | BUG-283 — `writeAuditLog`'s inner catch block (when both v2 and legacy schema inserts fail) now pushes the row onto the Redis outbox via `enqueueAuditOutbox(row)`. Pre-fix the catch was `/* truly failed — already logged below */` — the row was lost. This ensures every clinical-flow audit write is recoverable. |
| R-FIX-BUG-283-DRAINER-IN-BOOTSTRAP | apps/api/src/jobs/bootstrap.ts | present | `'auditOutboxDrainer'` | BUG-283 — `auditOutboxDrainer` registered in the startSchedulers() auto-loader so every API instance runs the drain tick on 30 s interval. Multi-instance deploys are safe (Redis RPOP is atomic). |
| R-FIX-BUG-283-EVENT-TIME-STAMP | apps/api/src/utils/audit.ts | present | `created_at: +eventTime` | BUG-283 L4 absorb. `created_at` is stamped at the ORIGINATING `writeAuditLog` call site (not by DB default) so outbox replay preserves forensic chronology. Without this fix, a row enqueued during a DB outage would receive `NOW()` at replay time (minutes-to-hours later), producing clinically-impossible ordering for coronial review (patient-create row dated AFTER a clinical-note-create row that references the patient). Event-time is an ISO-8601 string that survives JSON round-trip through the Redis outbox. |

Layer-2 tenant isolation now active across the full audit-surfaced gap list. Pairs with BUG-368 + BUG-430 app-layer clinic_id filtering (Layer-1). |

Acknowledgement persisted to `sessionStorage` keyed by `(patientId, allergenHash)`; re-triggers when the allergy list changes. Wraps MedicationsTab so the clinician CANNOT interact with prescription UI until they have acknowledged. Per-session + per-patient + per-allergen-hash. |

Any mismatched follow-up is rejected 409 CHAT_CONTEXT_LOCKED. Prevents the cross-patient leakage pathway flagged by Wave 5 where mid-session patientId switches carried prior chat context. |

| R-FIX-BUG-456-RESPONSE-SHAPE-ERROR | apps/api/src/features/medications/medicationService.ts | present | `RESPONSE_SHAPE_ERROR` | BUG-456 L3 absorb-1 — emit-time Zod parse failures wrapped in `AppError(500, 'RESPONSE_SHAPE_ERROR')` instead of bubbling raw ZodError to the global 422 handler. ZodError on EMIT is a server-side data-integrity issue, NOT a request validation failure; mapping to 500 is honest about the failure class and avoids confusing Sentry triage / frontend retry logic. |
| R-FIX-BUG-353-LAYER-B-TRIGGER | apps/api/migrations/20260424000001_force_revoke_sessions_on_staff_state_change.ts | present | `CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION force_revoke_sessions_on_staff_state_change` | BUG-353 Layer B (shipped after BUG-356 Layer A unblocked it) — DB trigger on `staff` AFTER UPDATE OF (role, is_active, deleted_at, clinic_id). On any watched-column transition: `UPDATE staff_sessions SET revoked_at = NOW() WHERE staff_id = NEW.id AND revoked_at IS NULL`, then INSERT INTO audit_log with `action = 'SESSION_REVOKED_BY_STATE_CHANGE_TRIGGER'` and `new_data` JSONB `{trigger, sessions_revoked, old_/new_ state snapshots}`. Mirrors BUG-354's `clinics_access_admin_slot_integrity` pattern: SECURITY DEFINER + `EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS → NULL` on audit so revocation UPDATE is never blocked. Defence-in-depth for direct SQL UPDATEs + soft-delete + clinic-transfer paths that bypass `staffService.updateStaff`. Satisfies BUG-358 invariant (trigger emits audit_log). Integration test at `apps/api/tests/integration/bug353ForceRevokeSessionsTrigger.int.test.ts` — 6 scenarios (role change + is_active + soft-delete + benign-no-op + same-value-no-op + already-revoked-skip). TDD: pre-migration all 6 FAIL (revoked_at stays NULL); post-migration 6/6 PASS; flake ×3 zero. Anchor pins the function name — rename breaks the audit-trigger guard. |
| R-FIX-BUG-353-AUDIT-ACTION | apps/api/src/utils/audit.ts | present | `'SESSION_REVOKED_BY_STATE_CHANGE_TRIGGER'` | BUG-353 companion — `AuditAction` union extended with the DB-trigger-specific literal, distinct from BUG-356's app-layer `'SESSION_REVOKED_BY_STATE_CHANGE'` so forensic review can tell app-layer vs DB-layer revocation origin from the audit_log row alone. |
| R-FIX-BUG-262-FU-INACTIVE-ASSIGNEE-GUARD | apps/api/src/features/pathology/pathologyService.ts | present | `async function resolveCriticalAssigneeAdmin` | BUG-262-FU part B (L4 clinical-safety finding) — before creating the primary critical-flag task, resolve the assignee via `resolveCriticalAssigneeAdmin(clinicId, order.ordered_by_id)`. If the ordering clinician is inactive (`is_active=false` OR `deleted_at IS NOT NULL`), reassign to the clinic's `nominated_admin_staff_id` (populated by BUG-354 Power Settings), else the `delegated_admin_staff_id`, else keep the original orderer but emit `logger.error` with `kind: 'hl7_critical_reassigned_inactive_orderer'` so ops dashboards detect the orphaned-task pattern. Prevents the patient-safety failure mode where a critical HL7 result lands in a retired clinician's queue. Integration test T8 at `apps/api/tests/integration/hl7InboundIngest.int.test.ts` — seeds an inactive orderer + nominated admin + verifies task.assigned_to_id flips to the admin. |
| R-FIX-BUG-366A-SERVER-ENTRY-POINT-GUARD | apps/api/src/server.ts | present | `SECRETS_BACKEND=azure_keyvault but process\.env\.JWT_ACCESS_SECRET is unset` | BUG-366a L5 absorb — if ops misconfigure a deploy to run `dist/src/server.js` DIRECTLY with `SECRETS_BACKEND=azure_keyvault` (bypassing index.ts), throw a diagnostic that names the root cause and the remediation. Pre-absorb the code would silently fall through the `if (... !== 'azure_keyvault')` guard and continue booting — config.ts Zod would then reject missing JWT secrets with a confusing error that didn't name the real problem (wrong entry point). |
| R-FIX-BUG-354-ACCESS-ADMIN-SLOT-INTEGRITY-TRIGGER | apps/api/migrations/20260423000005_access_admin_slot_integrity_trigger.ts | present | `clinics_access_admin_slot_integrity\(\)` | BUG-354 (S2, Layer B for BUG-351) — DB cascade trigger that NULLs `clinics.{nominated,delegated}_admin_staff_id` when the referenced staff transitions to an ineligible state. Four drift vectors covered: (1) demotion to operational role (`role IN ('receptionist','readonly')`), (2) deactivation (`is_active=false`), (3) soft-delete (`deleted_at IS NOT NULL`), (4) clinic transfer (`clinic_id IS DISTINCT FROM OLD.clinic_id`). AFTER UPDATE OF role, is_active, deleted_at, clinic_id — so benign column updates (given_name, phone, etc.) do NOT fire the trigger. Layer A sits at `authGuards.ts:requirePatientRelationship` (R-FIX-BUG-351-ACCESS-ADMIN-STAFF-JOIN) — belt-and-braces defence-in-depth. Operational-role literal `('receptionist','readonly')` is DUPLICATED in SQL vs `packages/shared/src/permissions.ts:OPERATIONAL_ONLY` TS set — drift tracked as BUG-355 candidate (SSoT between TS and SQL). Integration tests (6): T1 demote-receptionist, T2 demote-readonly, T3 deactivate, T4 soft-delete, T5 clinic-transfer-NULLs-old-clinic, T6 benign-given-name-leaves-slot-unchanged. 6/6 PASS on live Postgres. Anchor pins the trigger function name — rename fails the merge gate. |
| R-FIX-STAFF-LEAVE-PERIODS-DROPPED | apps/api/migrations/20260423000004_drop_duplicate_staff_leave_periods.ts | present | `dropTableIfExists\('staff_leave_periods'` | G3.C.2 mid-build correction — commit a4b03d5 shipped `staff_leave_periods` + `reassignment_proposals` tables without first verifying that an existing `staff_leave` table + CRUD routes at `managerFeatureRoutes.ts:298-395` already covered the leave-tracking concern. Dropped as forward-fix (per feedback_wave_gate_discipline — never amend a shipped commit; correct via new migration). Down path re-creates both tables exactly so rollback restores prior state. The reassignment-proposals CONCEPT remains valid and is parked in `docs/audit-2026-04-22/parked-work.md` for a future sub-cluster that attaches the FK to `staff_leave(id)` instead. Lesson: always `grep -rn` the feature keyword across `apps/api/src` before writing a new migration — absence of an exact filename match doesn't mean the feature is absent. |
| R-FIX-STAFF-LEAVE-PERIODS | apps/api/migrations/20260423000003_staff_leave_periods.ts | retired | `createTable\('staff_leave_periods'` | USER-C.2 / BUG-USER-ITEM-12 — foundation migration for the consultant-on-leave forward/reassign workflow. Introduces `staff_leave_periods` (clinic_id / staff_id / start_date / end_date NULLable=open-ended / leave_type CHECK-enforced annual/sick/study/parental/personal/other / notes / recorded_by_id FK staff ON DELETE SET NULL). Indexes on (clinic_id), (staff_id), and a composite (clinic_id, start_date, end_date) named `idx_staff_leave_active_window` for the "who's on leave today" query. CHECK `staff_leave_dates_chronological` rejects end_date < start_date. RLS policy `rls_staff_leave_periods_tenant` enforces `clinic_id = current_setting('app.clinic_id')::uuid`. Anchor pins the createTable call. |
| R-FIX-REASSIGNMENT-PROPOSALS | apps/api/migrations/20260423000003_staff_leave_periods.ts | retired | `createTable\('reassignment_proposals'` | USER-C.2 — auto-generated per-patient proposals that pair a leave_period_id with (patient_id, episode_id NULLable, from_staff_id, to_staff_id). `proposal_status` CHECK-enforced (pending/approved/rejected/expired), default 'pending'. CHECK `reassignment_distinct_staff_check` rejects from_staff_id == to_staff_id. vetted_reason + vetted_by_id + vetted_at hold the admin decision trail. Indexes on (clinic_id), (leave_period_id), (patient_id), (proposal_status), + composite (clinic_id, proposal_status) named `idx_reassignment_pending_queue` for the pending-queue page. RLS policy enforces clinic tenant scope. ON DELETE CASCADE from leave_period → proposal (cancel leave = cancel pending proposals). Anchor pins the createTable call. |

| R-FIX-RATING-SCALES-SEED | apps/api/migrations/20260423000002_rating_scales_seed.ts | present | `SCALES: ScaleTemplate\[\]` | USER-D.1 / BUG-USER-ITEM-8 — seed canonical psychiatric rating-scale templates per clinic. Pre-fix: `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM templates WHERE category = 'Rating Scales'` returned 0 per clinic, so the AssessmentsTab's rating-scale dropdown rendered empty ("No rating scale templates available") and clinicians couldn't start a rating scale. User reported "rating scales dropdown blank" as item 8. Fix: migration inserts 5 canonical scales (PHQ-9 9-item, GAD-7 7-item, HAM-D 17-item, MADRS 10-item, YMRS 11-item) for every clinic lacking any `category='Rating Scales'` template. Each template carries structured `items[]` JSON so the per-item storage + scoring introduced in USER-D.2/D.5 can consume them directly. Idempotent: clinics already carrying templates in the category are skipped; re-running the migration is safe. Taxonomy: builder-first alterTable semantics N/A — seed migration uses `.insert()` via Knex query-builder on `templates`; one `knex.raw<{rows}>` for the clinic introspection carries `// @migration-raw-exempt: introspection`. Down removes only the 5 canonical names in `('Rating Scales', 'rating_scale')` — clinic-authored custom scales survive. Anchor pins the typed scale array declaration; renaming or deleting the file fails the merge gate. Change class: seed migration (standard). Applied on 2026-04-23 as Batch 39 — log reported 35 templates inserted across 7 clinics. |

| R-FIX-BUG-402-MIGRATION-LOCK-VERSION | apps/api/migrations/20260701000038_bug_402_treatment_pathways_lock_version.ts | present | `t\.integer\('lock_version'\)\.notNullable\(\)\.defaultTo\(1\)` | BUG-402 — `lock_version` column added to treatment_pathways via builder-first migration (CLAUDE.md §12.1). | R-FIX-BUG-402-REPO-USES-HELPER | apps/api/src/features/treatment-pathways/pathwayRepository.ts | present | `updateWithOptimisticLock<TreatmentPathwayRow>` | BUG-402 — `pathwayRepository.update` routes through the shared opt-lock helper. Repository encapsulates the JSONB shallow-merge of `milestones` BEFORE the helper call so route handlers cannot accidentally bypass either step. Concurrent `+1 session` calls now produce a loud 409 instead of silently dropping one increment. |
| R-FIX-BUG-402-ZOD-REQUIRED | packages/shared/src/treatmentPathway.Schemas.ts | present | `expectedLockVersion: z\.number\(\)\.int\(\)\.positive\(\)` | BUG-402 — `expectedLockVersion` REQUIRED at the Zod boundary for pathway mutation payloads. Asymmetric posture rationale: only controlled first-party clients mutate pathways — atomic REQUIRED flip safe. Pattern now appears in 6 pathway mutation schemas as coverage expanded; decisiveness is pinned via allowlist count-drift. |
| R-FIX-BUG-402-ROUTE-PATCH-OPTLOCK | apps/api/src/features/treatment-pathways/pathwayRoutes.ts | present | `pathwayService\.update\(auth, req\.params\.id, parsed\)` | BUG-402 — PATCH `/:id` now delegates to service SSoT; service forwards caller-supplied `expectedLockVersion` into repository/helper (`updateWithOptimisticLock`) and preserves loud 409 conflict behavior. Pre-fix: free-form route-level writes with no version predicate. |
| R-FIX-BUG-402-ROUTE-SESSION-OPTLOCK | apps/api/src/features/treatment-pathways/pathwayRoutes.ts | present | `RecordSessionSchema\.parse` | BUG-402 — POST `/:id/session` validates expectedLockVersion at the Zod boundary then routes through the opt-locked update. The +1 increment is computed server-side from a fresh re-read; the helper's `WHERE lock_version = ?` predicate enforces serial-of-equals. Two concurrent session-record calls observing the same N: first writes N+1, second gets 409. |
| R-FIX-BUG-402-FRONTEND-PATHWAYSTAB | apps/web/src/features/patients/components/detail/tabs/PathwaysTab.tsx | present | `expectedLockVersion: pathway\.lockVersion` | BUG-402 — `handleComplete`, `handleDiscontinue`, post-session-note `updateMut.mutate` all echo the `lockVersion` read from the cached pathway. `onError` distinguishes `OPTIMISTIC_LOCK_CONFLICT` from generic errors and invalidates the cache so the user sees the fresh state on retry. |
| R-FIX-BUG-402-FRONTEND-PATHWAYSPAGE | apps/web/src/features/treatment-pathways/pages/PathwaysPage.tsx | present | `expectedLockVersion: lockVersion` | BUG-402 — `recordSession.mutationFn` accepts `{ id, lockVersion }` and posts `{ expectedLockVersion: lockVersion }`. The Record-Session button is disabled when `lockVersion` is not a number (defence against stale cache shape during rolling deploy). 409 conflicts surface as a dismissible warning Alert. |

The `critical_low` arm is the unique signature of the criteria block. |

The original inline `recipients.add(...)` Set was replaced because it routed silently to deactivated staff; the helper enforces `staff.is_active = true AND deleted_at IS NULL` + clinic-admin fallback. |

| R-FIX-BUG-372A-DBADMIN | apps/api/src/jobs/schedulers/pathologyCriticalScheduler.ts | present | `dbAdmin\('pathology_results as pr'\)` | BUG-372a L5 absorb-1 F1 — scheduler uses `dbAdmin` (signacare_owner role; bypasses RLS) for the SELECT. Bare `db()` under empty `app.clinic_id` GUC evaluates RLS predicate `clinic_id = NULL` and rejects every row (silent zero). Per-row tenant isolation is preserved by propagating `row.clinic_id` into every emit. Removing this anchor's pattern re-opens the silent-zero clinical-safety harm. |

| R-FIX-BUG-394-CRITICAL-MAP-ANAPHYLAXIS | apps/api/src/mcp/scribeAllergyAssessor.ts | present | `lower === 'anaphylaxis'` | BUG-394 — severity mapping locked: anaphylaxis → SafetyAlert.severity='critical'. | R-FIX-BUG-447-MEDICATIONS-MAR-KEYBOARD | apps/web/src/features/medications/components/MarChartPanel.tsx | present | `aria-label={\`Record administration for \${row.name} \${row.dose} at \${t}\`}` | BUG-447-medications (child 1/15) — MAR cell is now keyboard-operable. Decorative branch (admin set) is non-interactive (no onClick / role / tabIndex) — keyboard users can't activate already-recorded cells. | R-FIX-BUG-447-CLINICAL-NOTES-SCRIBE-NOTES-LIST-NO-CARD-ONCLICK | apps/web/src/features/patients/components/notes/NotesList.tsx | absent | `<Card[^>]*onClick=` | BUG-447-clinical-notes-scribe (child 5/15) — anti-anchor pinning the redundant-handler-removal fix at NotesList.tsx. Originally landed as Shape B trio (role="button" + tabIndex={0} + onKeyDown) per child 6/15; migrated to canonical `<Box component="button" type="button">` (escape-hatch (b) — native button semantics) per the L5 advisory from child 7/15 which surfaced the inner-trigger divergence between the two children. Browser handles Enter/Space natively; no manual onKeyDown. The right-side Box (Chip + IconButton) is sibling so the IconButton's previous `e.stopPropagation()` is no longer needed. `:focus-visible` outline guarantees the focus ring is visible on the trigger area. The previous defensive `<Box onClick={e => e.stopPropagation()}>` on the expanded content section remains REMOVED. Anchor uses a unique substring of the aria-label that avoids escaping `??` (the BUG-510 escaped-operator defect class); the regex matches both pre-migration and post-migration shape because the aria-label is identical. |

With the trigger moved to a sibling Box, neither defensive handler is required. | R-FIX-BUG-447-CORRESPONDENCE-LETTER-NO-INNER-TRIO | apps/web/src/features/patients/components/detail/tabs/CorrespondenceTab.tsx | absent | `onKeyDown=.*setExpandedLetterId` | BUG-447-FOLLOWUP-SHAPE-B-PRIME-DOC — anti-anchor pinning the absence of the pre-migration Shape B trio inner trigger on the letter Card. Pre-migration the inner Box had `onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); setExpandedLetterId(isLetterExpanded ? null : l.id); } }}` — an `onKeyDown` handler that referenced `setExpandedLetterId` directly. Post-migration the canonical `<Box component="button">` lets the browser handle Enter/Space natively; no `onKeyDown` handler at the letter trigger site references `setExpandedLetterId`. The pattern `onKeyDown=.*setExpandedLetterId` is unique to the pre-migration letter trigger (the thread Card's `onKeyDown` references `toggleThread()`, not `setExpandedLetterId`; the file's only other `setExpandedLetterId` references are inside `onClick` and the `useState` declaration). Re-introducing this exact pattern signals re-introduction of the manual onKeyDown trio at the letter trigger site, which would re-open the cross-child divergence with child 7/15 pathology dropzone (canonical inner = `<Box component="button">`). The absent-anchor + the present-anchor on `component="button"` form the bidirectional guard around the canonical Shape B′ inner trigger. |
| R-FIX-BUG-447-APPOINTMENTS-PATIENT-SEARCH-AUTOCOMPLETE-COMPONENT | apps/web/src/features/patients/components/PatientSearchAutocomplete.tsx | present | `Autocomplete<PatientOption` | BUG-447-appointments (child 8/15) — NEW shared component implementing Shape C (MUI native primitive) for patient typeahead. Replaces the prior 3 hand-rolled `<Paper>` + `<Box onClick>` popups in AppointmentsPage.tsx (lines 173, 355) + AppointmentsTab.tsx (line 367). The `<Autocomplete<PatientOption, false, false, false>>` generic signature pins the option type AND the multiple/disable-clearable/free-solo flags as `false` (single-select, clearable, no free text). Re-introducing the hand-rolled popup pattern would re-open the BUG-447 violations the §2.5 decision tree directs to Shape C. |

Keyboard users tab to the Button and press Enter/Space (native `<button>` activation). Drag-drop path preserved. |

| R-FIX-BUG-608-MARCHART-TRYASYNC | apps/web/src/features/medications/components/MarChartPanel.tsx | present | `await tryAsync\(\(\) => apiClient\.get(<[^>]+>)?\('medication-administrations'` | BUG-608 closes the silent-catch lie-about-success class on the MAR administrations fetch (sibling of BUG-441/445/548). Pre-fix queryFn used `.catch(() => ({ data: [] }))` collapsing fetch failure into "no doses recorded today" on the AHPRA-required medication chart audit trail surface — a nurse on the MAR after a network blip would see green "not-due" cells when actual dose status is unknown, with double-dosing or missed-dosing harm class. Post-fix uses tryAsync per BUG-530 SSoT (CLAUDE.md §16.2); err-arm rethrows so React-Query surfaces isError + UI renders `<Alert severity="error">` failure banner with explicit "Do not rely on this view for dose decisions" guidance. |
| R-FIX-BUG-608-MARCHART-NO-SILENT-CATCH | apps/web/src/features/medications/components/MarChartPanel.tsx | absent | `\.catch\(\(\) => \(\{ data: \[\] \}\)\)` | BUG-608 — anti-anchor pinning absence of the pre-fix silent-fallback shape on the MAR administrations fetch. Re-introducing it would re-open the BUG-441/445/548/608 lie-about-success class on the AHPRA-required medication chart audit trail. |
| R-FIX-BUG-608-MARCHART-ERROR-ALERT | apps/web/src/features/medications/components/MarChartPanel.tsx | present | `Do not rely on this view for dose decisions while the error persists` | BUG-608 — present-anchor pinning the clinical-safety belt copy on the MAR error banner. Path-updated 2026-04-29 (BUG-612 cycle): the regex was originally `Failed to load medication administrations` but BUG-612's atomic fold-in widened the banner to dynamic copy ("Failed to load MAR {medications and administrations\|medications\|administrations}"). The "Do not rely on this view for dose decisions while the error persists" phrase is the invariant clinical-safety belt across both rails (medsError + adminsError) — anchoring on this phrase guarantees the safety guidance is surfaced regardless of which rail failed. |
| R-FIX-BUG-609-MARCHART-AI-FALLBACK-MARKER | apps/web/src/features/medications/components/MarChartPanel.tsx | present | `\(intentional silent — BUG-609\)` | BUG-609 closes the missing intentional-silent comment marker on the AI summary catch arm. The fallback IS the correct posture (deterministic local computation already produces a complete numeric summary; the LLM call is a presentational enhancement only). The marker satisfies BUG-531 ESLint rule's allowlist convention (CLAUDE.md §16.2 — `// (intentional silent\|allowed silent) — <reason>`) so future ESLint sweeps recognise the silent-degradation as deliberate rather than accidental. |
| R-FIX-BUG-611-SIDEEFFECTS-TRYASYNC | apps/web/src/features/medications/components/SideEffectsPanel.tsx | present | `await tryAsync\(\(\) => apiClient\.get` | BUG-611 closes the silent-catch lie-about-success class on the side-effect monitoring schedules fetch (sibling of BUG-441/445/548/608). Pre-fix queryFn used a silent fallback collapsing fetch failure into "No side-effect monitoring schedules configured" empty-state UI on a clinical-safety surface — AIMS / metabolic / extrapyramidal / clozapine FBC monitoring schedules are AHPRA-required for antipsychotic prescribing and a clinician on a clozapine patient after a network blip would not know to follow up on overdue checks. Post-fix uses tryAsync per BUG-530 SSoT (CLAUDE.md §16.2); err-arm rethrows so React-Query surfaces isError + UI renders explicit `<Alert role="alert" severity="error">` failure banner. Fourth consumer of the BUG-530 SSoT (after BUG-545 + BUG-548 + BUG-608) — pattern catalogue is now demonstrably stable as house style. Path-update 2026-04-29 (BUG-613 cycle): the `<any>` generic narrowed to `<{ data: SideEffectScheduleResponse[] } | SideEffectScheduleResponse[]>` post-mapper-consolidation; anchor regex relaxed to match either form. |
| R-FIX-BUG-611-SIDEEFFECTS-NO-SILENT-CATCH | apps/web/src/features/medications/components/SideEffectsPanel.tsx | absent | `\.catch\(\(\) => \(\{ data: \[\] \}\)\)` | BUG-611 — anti-anchor pinning absence of the pre-fix silent-fallback shape on the side-effect schedules fetch. Re-introducing it would re-open the BUG-441/445/548/608/611 lie-about-success class on the AHPRA-required antipsychotic monitoring surface. |
| R-FIX-BUG-611-SIDEEFFECTS-ERROR-ALERT | apps/web/src/features/medications/components/SideEffectsPanel.tsx | present | `Do not assume AIMS, metabolic, or other monitoring is current` | BUG-611 — present-anchor pinning the explicit clinical-safety belt copy on the error banner. The copy names the failure mode ("Failed to load side-effect monitoring schedules") + names the affected monitoring classes (AIMS, metabolic, "other") + names the clinical guidance ("Do not assume monitoring is current while the error persists") — defence in depth over the structural tryAsync rail. |
| R-FIX-BUG-610-LAI-VALIDATIONS-TRYASYNC | apps/web/src/features/medications/components/LaiPanel.tsx | present | `await tryAsync\(\(\) => apiClient\.get<[^>]+>\(`lai/patients/\$\{patientId\}/validations` | BUG-610 closes the inverse-coalescing silent-fallback class on the LAI validations fetch (sibling of BUG-441/445/548/608/611). Pre-fix queryFn was `Array.isArray(r) ? r : []` — accepted ONLY raw arrays, silently rejected wrapped `{data:[...]}` shapes. On a wrapped response (or any fetch failure since the silent .then() coalescer also masked rejection), the panel rendered "No validation on record. Initial validation required." → clinician forced redundant revalidation of an already-valid LAI prescription. Post-fix uses tryAsync per BUG-530 SSoT (CLAUDE.md §16.2); err-arm rethrows so React-Query surfaces isError + the success path coalesces both raw-array AND wrapped-`{data:[...]}` shapes (mirrors the line 50 schedules pattern for symmetric defence). FIFTH consumer of BUG-530 SSoT — pattern is now mature house style. |
| R-FIX-BUG-610-LAI-SCHEDULES-TRYASYNC | apps/web/src/features/medications/components/LaiPanel.tsx | present | `await tryAsync\(\(\) => apiClient\.get<[^>]+>\(`lai/patients/\$\{patientId\}/lai-schedules` | BUG-610 fold-in (atomic per `feedback_no_silent_out_of_scope.md`): the sibling LAI schedules queryFn at line 50 had the same architectural class — `.then(r => { const arr = Array.isArray(r) ? r : r?.data ?? []; return arr; })` silently swallowed fetch failures (no `.catch()` but no `isError` surfacing either). Schedules drives the per-card validation status lookup (`getValidationStatus(scheduleId, ...)`); a missing schedule → no `validStatus` → no revalidation prompt visible to the clinician. Same harm class as the validations rail. Converted to tryAsync + isErr-throw + the existing both-shape coalescer. |
| R-FIX-BUG-610-LAI-NO-SILENT-COALESCING | apps/web/src/features/medications/components/LaiPanel.tsx | absent | `\.then\(r => Array\.isArray\(r\) \? r : \[\]\)` | BUG-610 — anti-anchor pinning absence of the pre-fix inverse-coalescing shape that silently rejected valid wrapped responses on the LAI validations rail. Re-introducing it would re-open the redundant-revalidation harm class on the depot-prescribing surface. |
| R-FIX-BUG-610-LAI-ERROR-ALERT | apps/web/src/features/medications/components/LaiPanel.tsx | present | `Do not approve a depot dose based on this view while the error persists` | BUG-610 — present-anchor pinning the explicit clinical-safety belt copy on the unified LAI error banner (fires when EITHER schedules or validations fetch fails). The copy names the failure mode + the clinical guidance (depot dose decisions = decompensation risk for psychotic disorders if administered without valid revalidation). Defence in depth over the structural tryAsync rail. |
| R-FIX-BUG-612-MARCHART-MEDS-TRYASYNC | apps/web/src/features/medications/components/MarChartPanel.tsx | present | `tryAsync\(\(\) => apiClient\.get(<[^>]+>)?\(`medications/patients/\$\{patientId\}/medications` | BUG-612 closes the active-meds rail of the MAR safety surface (sibling of BUG-608 which closed the administrations rail). Pre-fix queryFn called `apiClient.get(...)` directly without `tryAsync` (the function rejects on failure but the consumer did not destructure `isError` and did not render a failure banner — `medsData = undefined` → `activeMeds = []` → "No active medications" friendly empty-state UI instead of failure banner). Mitigation today via parent ActiveMedicationsTab's BUG-548 closure on the same query key (React-Query de-dups), but standalone mounts (testing, dashboard widgets, future re-parenting) re-open the harm class. Post-fix uses tryAsync per BUG-530 SSoT (CLAUDE.md §16.2) for symmetric defence with the administrations rail; panel is now internally fail-loud regardless of mount context. |
| R-FIX-BUG-612-MARCHART-MEDS-ISERROR | apps/web/src/features/medications/components/MarChartPanel.tsx | present | `isError: medsError` | BUG-612 — present-anchor pinning the `isError: medsError` destructure on the active-meds query. Pre-fix all 6 controllers did `res.json(rows)` directly with raw Knex rows; the frontend ClozapinePanel.tsx had to use defensive `?? snake_case` dual-shape access throughout, hiding 16+ `<any>` typings (BUG-606) AND read-rail silent-coalescing (BUG-617). | R-FIX-BUG-632-EXCLUDE-CEASED-MEDICATIONS | apps/api/src/features/roles/nurseFeatureRoutes.ts | present | `\.whereNot\('pm\.status', 'ceased'\)` | BUG-632 — present-anchor pinning the L4-FINDING-2 absorb (S0 administer-on-ceased harm class). The `medicationService.cease()` flip does NOT cascade to pending administrations (verified — no cease-cascade exists in the codebase). Post-fix `.whereNot('pm.status', 'ceased')` on the Knex builder query excludes ceased medications from the due-now view. Absorbed atomically per L4 strong-recommendation (`feedback_no_silent_out_of_scope.md`). |
| R-FIX-BUG-637-GUARD-EXISTS | scripts/guards/check-fk-aware-joins.ts | present | `BUG-637 — CI guard: Knex` | BUG-637 — present-anchor pinning the FK-aware join lint guard exists. Catches the wrong-table-join class structurally (the class that caused BUG-623 + BUG-632 silently for weeks). 715 FKs in schema; 501 TS files scanned; 3 pre-existing intentional non-FK joins allowlisted. |
| R-FIX-BUG-637-SNAPSHOT-CARRIES-FKS | apps/api/scripts/generate-schema-snapshot.ts | present | `pg_constraint` | BUG-637 — present-anchor pinning that schema-snapshot.json now carries FK metadata. The generator queries `pg_constraint` (not `information_schema.constraint_column_usage`) because the latter requires constraint ownership for visibility — `app_user` sees 0 rows there. `pg_constraint` is readable by any role with USAGE on the public schema. Single-column FKs are captured; composite FKs are joined into a comma-separated string and skipped by the guard (rare in this schema). |

| R-FIX-BUG-637-PARTITION-KEY-RESTRICTION | scripts/guards/check-fk-aware-joins.ts | present | `PARTITION_KEYS = new Set\(\['clinic_id', 'tenant_id', 'org_id'\]\)` | BUG-637 cycle-2 absorb-1 (FN-2 fix). | R-FIX-BUG-637-QUERY-SCOPED-ALIASES | scripts/guards/check-fk-aware-joins.ts | present | `findQueryScopedAliases` | BUG-637 cycle-2 absorb-1 (FN-4 fix). | R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR1.5-CYCLE2-EMPTY-FP-DEFENCE | scripts/guards/lib/allowlist-fingerprint.ts | present | `EMPTY_FINGERPRINT` | Phase R1 PR-R1-1.5 cycle-2 absorb (L3 finding #5). Whitespace-only line content returns null `EMPTY_FINGERPRINT` sentinel; `isAllowlisted()` short-circuits to `false` for empty fingerprints. Defends against a future allowlist entry whose fingerprint accidentally equals `e3b0c442` (sha256 of empty string), which would otherwise grant amnesty to every blank line in the named file. |

Multiplicity-aware count: a single fingerprint allowlist entry no longer grants amnesty to every same-text occurrence in the file. `getAllowlistedCount(file, fp, allowlist)` returns the count of matching entries; guards REJECT surplus violations. Without this, a 12th identical `res.json(rawRow)` in a file with 11 allowlisted occurrences would be silently approved. |

| R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR3-CYCLE2-CONCURRENTLY-FIX | scripts/guards/check-migration-rollback-discipline.ts | present | `\(\?:CONCURRENTLY\\\\s\+\)\?IF\\\\s\+EXISTS` | Phase R1 PR-R1-3 cycle-2 absorb (L3 finding #1 P0 false positive). The CONCURRENTLY token must appear AFTER the keyword in PG syntax (`DROP INDEX CONCURRENTLY IF EXISTS x`). Cycle-1 placed CONCURRENTLY in an optional non-capturing group BEFORE the IF EXISTS lookahead, allowing the regex engine to backtrack and falsely flag valid syntax. Cycle-2 collapses the optional CONCURRENTLY into the negative lookahead alongside IF EXISTS — `(?:CONCURRENTLY\s+)?IF\s+EXISTS` — eliminating the backtracking ambiguity. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR3-CYCLE2-NEGATIVE-TESTS | scripts/guards/__tests__/check-migration-rollback-discipline.test.ts | present | `does NOT flag DROP INDEX CONCURRENTLY IF EXISTS` | Phase R1 PR-R1-3 cycle-2 absorb (L3 finding #2 P0). Symmetric positive + NEGATIVE test fixtures for the rollback-discipline guard. Asserts the cycle-1 false positive (`DROP INDEX CONCURRENTLY IF EXISTS x`) is NOT flagged. 20 tests total — 9 positive (must flag) + 9 negative (must NOT flag) + 1 mixed + 3 stripSqlComments. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR3-CYCLE2-NOOP-OPTIN-STRUCTURE | scripts/guards/check-migration-rollback-discipline.ts | present | `NOOP_OPTIN_REGEX` | Phase R1 PR-R1-3 cycle-2 absorb (L3 finding #3 P1). The `@migration-down-noop:` opt-in regex requires a non-empty citation on the SAME LINE as the marker. Cycle-1 regex would capture from the NEXT line (closing `}`), letting `// @migration-down-noop:` with no reason silently pass. Cycle-2 anchors the capture to same-line characters via `[ \t]*(\S[^\n\r]*)`. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR3-CYCLE2-ARROW-DOWN | scripts/guards/check-migration-rollback-discipline.ts | present | `findDownBody` | Phase R1 PR-R1-3 cycle-2 absorb (L3 finding #4 P2). Renamed `findDownFunction` → `findDownBody`; now also detects `export const down = async (k) => { ... }` form. All 54 existing migrations use the function-declaration form so this is inert today; closes the false-negative class flagged by L3. Concise arrow body without a Block remains a documented coverage gap. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR3-CYCLE2-COMMENT-STRIP | scripts/guards/check-migration-rollback-discipline.ts | present | `stripSqlComments` | Phase R1 PR-R1-3 cycle-2 absorb (L3 finding #5 P2). SQL line comments (`-- DROP TABLE foo`) and block comments (`/* DROP ... */`) are stripped before scanning, eliminating the false-positive class where DROP keywords appear in SQL comment prose. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR3-CYCLE2-MULTI-VIOLATION | scripts/guards/check-migration-rollback-discipline.ts | present | `re\.exec\(stripped\)` | Phase R1 PR-R1-3 cycle-2 absorb (L3 finding #6 P3). Replaced `re.test(text)` (returns boolean on first match) with `re.exec()` loop to enumerate ALL violations per string. A migration with 3 unsafe `DROP TABLE` statements now reports 3 violations (with offsets), not 1. |

| R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR4-CYCLE2-TYPED-KNEX | scripts/guards/check-jsonb-extraction.ts | present | `db\(\?:<\[\^>\]` | Phase R1 PR-R1-4 cycle-2 absorb (L3 finding #1 P0). Cycle-1 regex `\bdb\s*\(` did NOT match the typed-Knex form `db<RowT>('table')`. `pathwayRepository.ts` (the CLAUDE.md §1.7 canonical example, querying `treatment_pathways` with JSONB-bearing `milestones`) was silently uncovered. Cycle-2 extends the regex to allow optional `<...>` between the function name and the opening paren — covering `db<T>(...)`, `dbRead<T>(...)`, `trx<T>(...)`. Allowlist grew from 73 to 79 entries; the 6 new files are the previously-masked typed-Knex query sites. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR4-CYCLE2-VITEST-SPEC | scripts/guards/__tests__/check-jsonb-extraction.test.ts | present | `from '\.\./check-jsonb-extraction'` | Phase R1 PR-R1-4 cycle-2 absorb (L3 finding #2 P0). Symmetric POSITIVE + NEGATIVE vitest spec for `findJsonbTablesInFile` + `hasJsonbExtractionMapper`. 18 tests total: 7 query-pattern coverage (typed + non-typed) + 4 mapper accept (dot / bracket / parseCol / arrow) + 5 mapper reject (no JSONB ref / column in comment only / column in string only / brace-balance window / no mapper) + 2 generic-name false-positive defence. The cycle-1 typed-Knex blind spot would fail test "detects typed db<RowT>(\"table\") form". |
| R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR4-CYCLE2-PROP-ACCESS | scripts/guards/check-jsonb-extraction.ts | present | `bracketRe` | Phase R1 PR-R1-4 cycle-2 absorb (L3 finding #3 medium). Cycle-1 `\b<col>\b` heuristic auto-passed mappers that mentioned the column name for ANY reason — including comments, string literals, unrelated identifiers, or destructure-inside-block. Cycle-2 requires PROPERTY-ACCESS context: `r.col` / `row.col` / `r['col']` / `parseCol(`. Bracket-access scans the original body (column name is inside string literal); dot-access + parseCol scan a body with strings + comments stripped. Defends against the generic-name false-positive class for `content`, `metadata`, `details`, `items` etc. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR4-CYCLE2-BRACE-BALANCE | scripts/guards/check-jsonb-extraction.ts | present | `findMatchingBrace` | Phase R1 PR-R1-4 cycle-2 absorb (L3 finding #4 low). Cycle-1 used a fixed 4000-char window which falsely accepted mappers when unrelated code 100 lines after the mapper happened to mention the JSONB column. Cycle-2 finds the actual matching close brace via bracket-balance (skipping string literals, line comments, block comments) so the mapper body is precisely scoped. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR4-CYCLE2-WARN-CATCH | scripts/guards/check-jsonb-extraction.ts | present | `console\.warn.*could not` | Phase R1 PR-R1-4 cycle-2 absorb (L3 finding #5 low). Cycle-1 had bare `} catch { return ... }` blocks in `loadAllowlist` and `walk`. Cycle-2 surfaces the failure via `console.warn(...)` so a corrupt allowlist or unreadable directory shows up in the guard log rather than silently no-oping. |

AST-walks every TS file under `apps/api/src/features/`, finds every `db.transaction(callback)` / `dbRead.transaction(callback)` invocation, and REJECTs any nested `db(...)` / `db.raw(...)` / `dbRead(...)` / `dbRead.raw(...)` calls inside the callback body. | R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR5-VITEST-SPEC | scripts/guards/__tests__/check-trx-not-db-inside-transaction.test.ts | present | `scanForForbiddenCalls` | Phase R1 PR-R1-5 — present-anchor pinning the symmetric POSITIVE + NEGATIVE vitest spec. Cycle-2 expanded to 22 direct-import tests (0.36s) per L3 finding #5 P2: 6 POSITIVE flag (direct/raw/dbRead/repo-no-trx/multi/function-expression) + 7 NEGATIVE accept (trx/repo-with-trx/repo-with-{trx}/mapper/annotation/custom-name/nested) + 3 isTransactionCall + 3 isRepoHelperCall + 3 callPassesTrx unit tests. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR5-CYCLE2-REPO-HELPER | scripts/guards/check-trx-not-db-inside-transaction.ts | present | `isRepoHelperCall` | Phase R1 PR-R1-5 cycle-2 absorb (L3 finding #1 P0). Cycle-1 missed the canonical CLAUDE.md §2.1 leak shape: a repo helper called inside a transaction without `trx` propagation (`escalationRepository.findById(clinicId, id)`). The repo's internal `db()` call opens a fresh pool connection silently. Cycle-2 introduces `isRepoHelperCall(node)` (matches `xxxRepository.method` / `xxxRepo.method`) + `callPassesTrx(node, trxName)` (verifies trx propagation as positional arg or `{ trx }` shorthand). Surfaced 3 real pre-existing leaks: templateRepository.findById ×2 + appointmentAttendeeRepository.listForAppointment — all fixed in this same PR by extending repo signatures with optional `trx?: Knex.Transaction`. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR5-CYCLE2-TEMPLATE-FINDBYID | apps/api/src/features/templates/template.repository.ts | present | `trx\?: Knex\.Transaction` | Phase R1 PR-R1-5 cycle-2 absorb. Real pre-existing leak: `templateRepository.findById` was called inside its own `create` and `update` transactions without `trx`, opening a fresh pool connection per call. Cycle-2 fix: optional `trx?: Knex.Transaction` parameter, with `hydrateSections` also accepting an optional Knex query builder. Both transaction-internal call sites (lines 162 + 206) updated to pass `trx`. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR5-CYCLE2-ATTENDEE-LIST | apps/api/src/features/appointments/appointmentAttendeeRepository.ts | present | `trx\?: Knex\.Transaction` | Phase R1 PR-R1-5 cycle-2 absorb. Cycle-2 fix: optional `trx?: Knex.Transaction` parameter; the service call site updated to pass `trx`. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR5-CYCLE2-DIRECT-IMPORT | scripts/guards/__tests__/check-trx-not-db-inside-transaction.test.ts | present | `import \{` | Phase R1 PR-R1-5 cycle-2 absorb (L3 finding #5 P2). Cycle-1 spec used `spawnSync('npx tsx', ...)` per test (~6.4s, polluted apps/api/src/features/ with synthetic files). Cycle-2 exports the guard's helpers (`isTransactionCall`, `getTransactionCallback`, `getTrxParamName`, `isRepoHelperCall`, `callPassesTrx`, `scanForForbiddenCalls`) and the spec imports them directly. Runtime: 0.36s. No filesystem pollution. Mutation resistance: stronger because tests assert internal function returns, not stderr text patterns. Matches PR-R1-3 sibling pattern. |

Cycle-2 explicitly documents: (a) shared/middleware paths use raw pool primitives outside the §2.1 identifier convention, (b) aliased imports tracked as BUG-PR-R1-5-FOLLOWUP-ALIASED-DB-IMPORT. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR6-GUARD | scripts/guards/check-bugs-remaining-uniqueness.ts | present | `findBugIdOccurrences` | Phase R1 PR-R1-6 — present-anchor pinning the catalogue uniqueness guard. Parses `docs/quality/bugs-remaining.md`, extracts the BUG-ID from every row's "Bug" / "BUG" column, asserts each ID appears in EXACTLY ONE row. Closes the BUG-633/634b split-state class. Cascade siblings (`BUG-XXX-CASCADE-N`, `BUG-XXX-FOLLOWUP-Y`) are distinct IDs counted separately. Dot-suffix IDs (BUG-A5.0 vs BUG-A5.3) preserved correctly via `[A-Za-z0-9._-]+` character class. |

Without `.` in the class, `BUG-A5.0` / `BUG-A5.3` would silently collapse to the same ID `BUG-A5`, producing a false-positive duplicate. The synthetic spec test asserts these are kept distinct. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR6-CYCLE2-FENCED-BLOCK | scripts/guards/check-bugs-remaining-uniqueness.ts | present | `inCodeFence` | Phase R1 PR-R1-6 cycle-2 absorb (L3 finding A1). Walks lines tracking fenced code-block state via `inCodeFence` boolean, toggled on lines starting with ` ``` `. Pipe-tables inside fenced blocks are NOT parsed as catalogue tables. Defends against future catalogue text that uses fenced examples. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR6-CYCLE2-NO-SEPARATOR-WARN | scripts/guards/check-bugs-remaining-uniqueness.ts | present | `tablesWithoutSeparator` | Phase R1 PR-R1-6 cycle-2 absorb (L3 finding A2). Cycle-1 silently skipped Bug-header tables that lacked the `\|---\|` separator row. Cycle-2 collects them into `tablesWithoutSeparator` and FAILS the merge-gate. A future maintainer can't bypass the guard by deleting the separator row. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR6-CYCLE2-PARSEROW-NO-TRAILING-PIPE | scripts/guards/check-bugs-remaining-uniqueness.ts | present | `parts\.pop\(\)` | Phase R1 PR-R1-6 cycle-2 absorb (L3 finding A3). Cycle-1 used `.split('\|').slice(1,-1)` which dropped the LAST cell on lines without a trailing `\|`. Cycle-2 splits on `\|`, drops the leading-empty, then conditionally pops the final empty (only when present). A line `\| open \| BUG-X` (no trailing pipe) now correctly yields `['open', 'BUG-X']`. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-R1-PR6-CYCLE2-EXPECTED-COUNT | scripts/guards/check-bugs-remaining-uniqueness.ts | present | `expectedCount` | Phase R1 PR-R1-6 cycle-2 absorb (L3 finding A5). Allowlist format upgraded from `<BUG-ID>  # comment` to `<BUG-ID> expected=<N>  # comment`. Guard asserts the actual occurrence count for each allowlisted ID EXACTLY matches `<N>`. Drift in either direction (consolidation OR regression / state-flip) fails the merge-gate. Sibling pattern of PR-R1-1.5 cycle-2 multiplicity-aware fingerprint allowlist. |

Hard-coded `MAX_ALLOWLIST_SIZE = 5` constant caps allowlist size at the current 5 baseline entries. Growth beyond the cap requires deliberate code change to the constant + cascade-BUG documentation. NEW split-states cannot be silently allowlisted as a workaround. |

| R-FIX-BUG-569-NULL-PRESCRIBER-FILTER | apps/api/src/jobs/schedulers/clozapineAlertScheduler.ts | present | `whereNotNull\('cr\.prescriber_staff_id'\)` | BUG-569 L4+L5 absorb-1 — `clozapine_registrations.prescriber_staff_id` is nullable (FK SET NULL on staff delete). | R-FIX-PHASE-0A.10-RUNTIME-EVIDENCE-FILE | docs/quality/runtime-verification-evidence.md | present | `Phase 0a.10 deliverable` | Phase 0a.10 closure (2026-05-03; partial-baseline-allowed completion model per operator-authorized modified-C). NEW repo-tracked evidence file capturing actual probe verdicts for 4 Layer 0a agent runtime probes (shortcut-detector / confidence-label-enforcer / dod-completion-checker / gold-standard-enforcer) + 5 memory recall probes. Each row: agent_file/memory_file path + content_hash (SHA-256) + STATUS (VERIFIED_THIS_SESSION OR PENDING_FRESH_SESSION) + last_verified_session_id + invocation reference + runtime_confidence (HIGH for verified; LOW for pending). | R-FIX-PHASE-0A.10-STALENESS-GUARD | scripts/guards/check-runtime-evidence-staleness.ts | present | `check-runtime-evidence-staleness \(Phase 0a\.10\)` | Phase 0a.10 closure (2026-05-03). NEW companion guard `npm run guard:runtime-evidence-staleness` parses `runtime-verification-evidence.md` for `content_hash:` rows + `agent_file/memory_file:` paths + `status:` markers. Recomputes SHA-256 hash of each referenced file. FAILS CI if any recorded content_hash != current file hash → forces re-verification when agent prompt or memory file changes. Tolerant of CI environments where `~/.claude` memory files are unreachable (treats as "skip with note", not failure). Three exit codes: 0=clean, 1=staleness violation, 2=evidence file malformed/missing. Wired into `guard:claude-discipline` umbrella + `guard:claude-discipline:ci`. |
