Parses Knex builder calls (`knex.schema.createTable` + `knex.schema.alterTable` + `t.<knex_type>('<col>', ...modifiers).chained()` + `t.dropColumn('<col>')` + `t.renameColumn('<from>','<to>')`) directly from `apps/api/migrations/*.ts`. Replays alterTable evolution chronologically (sorted filename). Maps 13 Knex types to TS + Zod. Emits per-table Row interface + Zod DTO scaffold + Zod Response scaffold (snake_case Row, camelCase scaffolds). Import-safe via `if (require.main === module) main()`. Flags: `--table <name>` (filter), `--dry-run`, `--verbose`. Wired as `npm run generate:types-from-migrations`. Phase 0b.1a scope: generator + tests only (no full run yet); 0b.1b lands full run + scaffold-extension guard; 0b.1c lands second-line defense. |

| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1B-I-ADVISORY-1-CALLBACK-PATH | scripts/generate-types/parser.ts | present | `onFailure\?: \(failure: ParseFailure\) => void,` | Phase 0b.1b-i absorb of L5 0b.1a advisory #1 (2026-05-04; refined in absorb-2 per operator-authorized gold-standard). `findTableEvents` accepts optional `onFailure` callback. Driver passes a collector callback so it can decide warn-vs-hard-fail based on `--dry-run` flag. Without callback (legacy callers + tests), falls back to `console.warn`. Removing this signature re-collapses the warn-only-no-hard-fail regression. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1B-I-ADVISORY-1-DRIVER-HARD-FAIL | scripts/generate-types/driver.ts | present | `MIGRATION PARSE FAILURE — CI BLOCKING \(full-run mode\)` | Phase 0b.1b-i absorb-2 of L5 0b.1a advisory #1 (2026-05-04; operator-authorized gold-standard 2026-05-04 — warn in --dry-run, process.exit(1) in full-run mode). Driver collects parse failures via callback. In `--dry-run` mode emits `console.warn` per failure (allows triage); in full-run mode emits `console.error` + `process.exit(1)` (prevents silent-skip from generating empty Row interfaces that crash at runtime). Removing this branch re-introduces the silent-skip-undermines-source-of-truth class. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1B-I-ADVISORY-3-DECIMAL-PRECISION-PARSING | scripts/generate-types/parser.ts | present | `if \(method === 'decimal'\)` | Phase 0b.1b-i absorb of L5 0b.1a advisory #3 (2026-05-04). Knex `t.decimal('amount', 10, 2)` carries precision (10) + scale (2) for Postgres NUMERIC(10,2). Pre-absorb the parser discarded both numbers. Post-absorb: extracts via `argsRaw.matchAll(/,\s*(\d+)/g)` and stores into `ColumnDef.decimalPrecision` + `ColumnDef.decimalScale`. Consumed downstream by `zodExpressionForColumn` to emit a Zod regex that pins the precision contract. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1B-I-ADVISORY-3-DECIMAL-PRECISION-EMIT | scripts/generate-types/emitter.ts | present | `Math\.max\(1, col\.decimalPrecision - col\.decimalScale\)` | Phase 0b.1b-i absorb of L5 0b.1a advisory #3 (2026-05-04; absorb-2 of L3 cycle-1 REJECT). NEW exported function `zodExpressionForColumn(col)` factors per-col Zod expression. Integer-digit count clamped to >=1 via `Math.max(1, ...)` so NUMERIC(P, S>=P) (correlation coefficients, normalized scores; Knex emits "0.12345" with leading zero) produces a valid regex. Cycle-1 used `Math.max(0, ...)` which emitted invalid quantifier `\d{1,0}` and crashed RegExp() at scaffold-import time. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1B-I-CLI-SHIM-RUN-DIRECTLY | scripts/generate-types-from-migrations.ts | present | `if \(require\.main === module\) \{` | Phase 0b.1b-i (2026-05-04). Post-split, the original file is a CLI shim — re-exports public API for test/consumer backwards-compat AND invokes `driverMain()` when executed directly via `tsx scripts/generate-types-from-migrations.ts`. Without this guard, `npm run generate:types-from-migrations` silently does nothing. Pinned so the shim cannot regress to import-only. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1B-I-TESTS-ADVISORY-1 | scripts/__tests__/generate-types-from-migrations.test.ts | present | `Phase 0b\.1b-i absorb of L5 0b\.1a advisory #1 \(silent-skip-on-unparseable\)` | Phase 0b.1b-i (2026-05-04). NEW describe-block pinning the 3 tests for L5 advisory #1 absorb (2 cycle-1 + 1 absorb-2): malformed migration (unbalanced braces) emits `console.warn` via `vi.spyOn(console, 'warn')`; clean migration does NOT warn; with `onFailure` callback supplied, parser routes failure to callback INSTEAD of console.warn. Removing the describe block re-enables silent-skip regression + warn-only-no-callback regression. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1B-I-TESTS-ADVISORY-3 | scripts/__tests__/generate-types-from-migrations.test.ts | present | `Phase 0b\.1b-i absorb of L5 0b\.1a advisory #3 \(decimal precision preservation\)` | Phase 0b.1b-i (2026-05-04). NEW describe-block pinning the 8 tests for L5 advisory #3 absorb (6 cycle-1 + 2 absorb-2): decimal tuple extraction for `t.decimal('a', 10, 2)` / `t.decimal('a', 10)` / `t.decimal('a')`; `zodExpressionForColumn` regex emission for precision+scale / precision-only / bare decimal fallback / NUMERIC(4,5) / NUMERIC(3,3). The 2 absorb-2 tests verify the regex literal constructs as a valid RegExp and matches "0.12345" / "0.999" — pinning the L3 cycle-1 REJECT fix. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1B-II-A-EXTRACT-UP-FUNCTION-BODY | scripts/generate-types/parser.ts | present | `export function extractUpFunctionBody` | Phase 0b.1b-ii-A (2026-05-04). NEW exported helper `extractUpFunctionBody(source)` locates the body of `export async function up(knex: ...): Promise<void> { ... }` via brace-balanced extraction. Returns null if not found. Used by `findTableEvents` to scope event detection to up() only — without this, `dropTableIfExists` calls in `down()` (rollback path) were silently treated as in-band events, removing 34 real tables (scribe_*, letters/letter_*, capacity_assessments, etc.) from the post-replay state. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1B-II-A-PARSER-SCOPED-TO-UP | scripts/generate-types/parser.ts | present | `Phase 0b\.1b-ii-A: only inspect the up\(\) body` | Phase 0b.1b-ii-A (2026-05-04). `findTableEvents` now operates on `extractUpFunctionBody(source)` output instead of the entire source. A migration file without a balanced `up(...)` declaration emits a fail-loud signal (warn or onFailure callback) — same class as advisory #1 silent-skip absorption. This is THE foundational correctness fix for the full-run discovery: pre-fix 207 tables emitted; post-fix 239 (+32, recovering all 31 down()-poisoned tables + the const-bound llm_prompts_outputs). |
| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1B-II-A-PARSE-TOP-LEVEL-STRING-CONSTS | scripts/generate-types/parser.ts | present | `export function parseTopLevelStringConsts` | Phase 0b.1b-ii-A (2026-05-04, operator-authorized "extend the parser now" for the const-bound case). NEW exported helper resolves `const TABLE = 'foo';` (or `"foo"` / `\`foo\``) bindings at file top level. Operator-scoped: plain string-literal RHS only; identifier-without-binding triggers fail-loud in `findTableEvents`. Recovers `llm_prompts_outputs` (the only production migration using this pattern). |
| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1B-II-A-CONST-BINDING-FAIL-LOUD | scripts/generate-types/parser.ts | present | `not bound to a plain string literal at file top level` | Phase 0b.1b-ii-A (2026-05-04). When the createTable/alterTable/dropTable opener captures an identifier (not a quoted literal) AND the identifier has no binding in `parseTopLevelStringConsts` output, the parser fails loud (warn if no callback, onFailure if collector supplied). The fail-loud signal IS what surfaced the BUG-371 for-of-loop case during the rerun, leading to the for-of-expansion fix. Removing this branch re-introduces the silent-skip class. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1B-II-A-FOR-OF-EXPANSION | scripts/generate-types/parser.ts | present | `export function expandForOfLoops` | Phase 0b.1b-ii-A (2026-05-04, operator-authorized "Option A — gold-standard structural fix"). NEW exported helper pre-processes the up() body to expand `for (const X of CONST_NAME) { BODY }` loops into N synthetic copies of BODY with `X` substituted by the quoted string literal. Recovers BUG-371 lock_version migration's effect on prescriptions/patient_medications/episodes (verified `lock_version: number` present in all 3 emitted Row interfaces). |
| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1B-II-A-PARSE-TOP-LEVEL-STRING-ARRAY-CONSTS | scripts/generate-types/parser.ts | present | `export function parseTopLevelStringArrayConsts` | Phase 0b.1b-ii-A (2026-05-04). NEW exported helper resolves `const X = ['a', 'b', 'c'] as const;` bindings (or without `as const`). All array elements must be plain string literals matching the table-name shape; arrays containing any non-literal element are NOT registered (fail-loud propagates downstream). Consumed by `expandForOfLoops`. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1B-II-A-TESTS | scripts/__tests__/generate-types-from-migrations.test.ts | present | `Phase 0b\.1b-ii-A: extractUpFunctionBody \+ up\(\)-only event scoping` | Phase 0b.1b-ii-A (2026-05-04). 18 net new tests added (17 in 3 new describe blocks + 1 NEW test in the existing advisory-#1 block for "no balanced up() declaration found" warn): (a) up()-only scoping (5 tests — extractUpFunctionBody isolation, no-up()-warn, canary createTable-up + dropTableIfExists-down preservation, forward-drop-still-works, psychology_session_notes live-fixture); (b) const-bound table names (6 tests — parseTopLevelStringConsts, fail-loud branches, llm_prompts_outputs live-fixture); (c) for-of-of-const-string-array (6 tests — parseTopLevelStringArrayConsts, expandForOfLoops shape, BUG-371 live-fixture confirms 3 alterTable events on prescriptions/patient_medications/episodes); plus the +1 test in advisory-#1 block. Total: 48→66 PASS — `npx vitest run scripts/__tests__/generate-types-from-migrations.test.ts` reported "Test Files 1 passed (1) | Tests 66 passed (66)" at landing time. |

| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1B-II-B-PARITY-EXCLUDES-SCAFFOLDS | scripts/guards/check-zod-schema-parity.ts | present | `Phase 0b\.1b-ii-B \(operator-authorized 2026-05-04\): exclude the` | Phase 0b.1b-ii-B (2026-05-04, operator-authorized via "A: with the narrow sequencing above"). The Zod parity guard's `walk()` function now skips `packages/shared/src/_scaffolds/` (the auto-generated scaffold directory). Pre-fix the parity guard scanned scaffolds and emitted convention-violations (e.g. `webauthn_credentials.credentialId: z.string()` flagged because `*Id fields must use .uuid()` — but the migration's source-of-truth declares `credentialId` as `t.string`, not a UUID). Operator-rejected the alternative (teaching the generator suffix heuristics like `*Id → .uuid()`) as "a structural mistake that forces generator output to satisfy hand-written DTO naming conventions." Convention enforcement on generated scaffolds, if needed in future, must be schema-driven (NOT name-pattern based). |
| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1B-II-B-SCAFFOLD-EXTENSION-GUARD | scripts/guards/check-scaffold-extension.ts | present | `RESPONSIBILITY: enforce that hand-written Zod object schemas` | Phase 0b.1b-ii-B (2026-05-04). NEW guard `check-scaffold-extension.ts` enforces that hand-written Zod schemas in `packages/shared/src/*.ts` which have a corresponding migration-driven scaffold under `_scaffolds/` EITHER (a) import + extend the scaffold OR (b) carry a `// @scaffold-divergence: <reason>` annotation. Heuristic table-name derivation (camelCase → snake_case + simple pluralization) — schemas with no matching scaffold are out-of-scope. Allowlist supports lenient baseline (per CLAUDE.md §15 + Phase 0b.1 plan). Stale-allowlist detection rejects entries that no longer violate (forces self-cleaning as schemas migrate). |

| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1C-GUARD-EXISTS | scripts/guards/check-no-column-ddl-in-raw-sql.ts | present | `RESPONSIBILITY: hard-block column DDL inside ` | Phase 0b.1c (2026-05-04). NEW second-line-defense guard. Layer 1 (`check-migration-convention.ts`) uses taxonomy-exemption (any raw SQL must carry `// @migration-raw-exempt: <category>` annotation). Layer 2 (this guard): NO exemption mechanism — column DDL inside `knex.raw(...)` is structurally never legitimate because the Knex builder fully expresses CREATE TABLE / ADD COLUMN / DROP COLUMN / ALTER COLUMN / RENAME COLUMN, `t.specificType()` is the canonical builder escape for Postgres-specific types, and JS-level `hasColumn` + `alterTable` is the canonical idempotency-guard pattern. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1C-GUARD-TESTS | scripts/guards/__tests__/check-no-column-ddl-in-raw-sql.test.ts | present | `Phase 0b\.1c — symmetric tests for the second-line-defense guard` | Phase 0b.1c (2026-05-04). 21 tests across 3 describe blocks: (a) `findRawCalls` (4 tests — single-quoted, backtick-template, multiple calls, false-positive on string literals); (b) POSITIVE detect (7 tests — CREATE TABLE / ADD COLUMN / DROP COLUMN / ALTER COLUMN / RENAME COLUMN / IF NOT EXISTS misuse / multi-line ALTER); (c) NEGATIVE non-flag (10 tests — CREATE INDEX / CREATE EXTENSION / ENABLE RLS / CREATE POLICY / GRANT / COMMENT ON COLUMN / ADD CONSTRAINT / DROP TABLE / SELECT / UPDATE). 21/21 PASS at landing time — `npx vitest run scripts/guards/__tests__/check-no-column-ddl-in-raw-sql.test.ts` reported "Test Files 1 passed (1) | Tests 21 passed (21)". |
| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1C-PHONE-TRIAGE-COLUMNS-EMITTED | apps/api/src/db/types/phone_triage.ts | present | `receptionist_summary\?: string` | Phase 0b.1c absorb (2026-05-04). The `phone_triage.receptionist_summary` + `clinical_risk_flags` columns were previously hidden inside `await knex.raw('ALTER TABLE phone_triage ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS ...')` — invisible to the type generator. Migration `20260701000009_phone_triage_split_clinical_notes.ts` converted to JS-level `hasColumn` + `alterTable` + `t.text()` / `t.jsonb()` builder pattern. Both columns now appear in the generated Row interface — proves the second-line defense's end-to-end effect. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1C-EMBEDDING-COLUMN-EMITTED | apps/api/src/db/types/llm_interactions.ts | present | `embedding\?: unknown` | Phase 0b.1c absorb (2026-05-04). The `llm_interactions.embedding` Postgres `vector(1536)` column was previously hidden inside `await knex.raw('ALTER TABLE llm_interactions ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS embedding vector(1536)')` — invisible to the type generator. Migration `20260701000021_tier13_scribe_safety_and_search.ts` converted to JS-level `hasColumn` + `alterTable` + `t.specificType('embedding', 'vector(1536)')` builder pattern (specificType is the canonical builder escape for Postgres-specific types). The column now appears in the generated Row interface as `embedding: unknown` (specificType has unknown TS type per Phase 0b.1a TS_TYPE_MAP). |

| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1C-PHONE-TRIAGE-MIGRATION-FIX | apps/api/migrations/20260701000009_phone_triage_split_clinical_notes.ts | present | `await knex\.schema\.hasColumn\('phone_triage', 'receptionist_summary'\)` | Phase 0b.1c absorb (2026-05-04). Migration converted from `ALTER TABLE phone_triage ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS receptionist_summary text NULL, ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS clinical_risk_flags jsonb NULL` (raw SQL with `idempotency_guard` taxonomy exemption) to JS-level `hasColumn` + `alterTable` + `t.text()` / `t.jsonb()`. Same shape applied symmetrically in `down()` (drops via `hasColumn` + `t.dropColumn`). Removing this fix re-introduces the silent column-DDL class. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1C-EMBEDDING-MIGRATION-FIX | apps/api/migrations/20260701000021_tier13_scribe_safety_and_search.ts | present | `t\.specificType\('embedding', 'vector\(1536\)'\)` | Phase 0b.1c absorb (2026-05-04). Migration converted from `ALTER TABLE llm_interactions ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS embedding vector(1536)` to JS-level `hasColumn` + `alterTable` + `t.specificType('embedding', 'vector(1536)')`. `t.specificType` is the canonical Knex builder escape for Postgres-specific types (vector, citext, etc.) — preserves builder visibility for the type generator while supporting non-builder-native types. Same shape applied symmetrically in `down()`. |
| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.1C-RECEPTIONIST-JSONB-EXEMPT | apps/api/src/features/roles/receptionistFeatureRoutes.ts | present | `@jsonb-extraction-exempt: role-based SQL projection is the privacy boundary` | Phase 0b.1c design-scope exemption (operator-authorized 2026-05-04). permanent: the phone_triage migration's clinical_risk_flags JSONB column became visible to `check-jsonb-extraction`'s auto-discovery once the migration was rewritten in builder form (Phase 0b.1c absorb). receptionistFeatureRoutes.ts uses role-based SQL projection (PHONE_TRIAGE_COLUMNS_FULL for nurses; PHONE_TRIAGE_COLUMNS_REDACTED for receptionists, omitting clinical_risk_flags entirely) as the privacy boundary. A *ToResponse extraction mapper would push role-sensitive behavior into a mapper layer and risk weakening or obscuring the SQL-layer privacy mechanism. Operator's gold-standard call: "1, with explicit rationale that the exemption preserves the existing privacy mechanism" — design-scope exemption preserving an existing sound mechanism, not deferred debt. Removing the annotation re-introduces the guard's false-positive class. |

| R-FIX-PHASE-0B.2B-GUARD-EXISTS | scripts/guards/check-no-hardcoded-column-lists.ts | present | `Phase 0b\.2b — guard against hand-written` | Phase 0b.2b (2026-05-04). NEW guard rejects hand-written `*_COLUMNS` / `*_COLS` constants in `apps/api/src/features/` unless either: (a) annotated with `// @column-list-projection-exempt: <reason>` within ±10 lines of the declaration (or in file-header first-30-lines), OR (b) listed in `scripts/guards/check-no-hardcoded-column-lists.allowlist` with class marker (A = drift debt, B = projection exemption). Stale-allowlist self-cleaning: entries that no longer correspond to violations exit 2. Class-marker enforcement: entries lacking `class: A|B` annotation exit 2. Naming convention shape: matches `_COLUMNS` / `_COLS` substring (not necessarily as suffix), so `PHONE_TRIAGE_COLUMNS_FULL` and `PHONE_TRIAGE_COLUMNS_REDACTED` paired projections are detected. |

| R-FIX-BUG-EPISODE-MDT-SAVE-RACE-FIXED-ADVISORY-LOCK | apps/api/src/features/episode/episodeRoutes.ts | present | `SELECT pg_advisory_xact_lock` | BUG-EPISODE-MDT-SAVE-RACE (S2) closure 2026-05-07. Advisory lock keyed by `(clinic_id, org_unit_id)` acquired via SQL `SELECT pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(? :: ':' :: ?))` (markdown-pipe-escaped — actual SQL uses `\|\|` concat) at start of transaction. Lock auto-releases on commit/rollback. Cross-team saves don't block; same-team saves serialize. Hash collisions cause false-sharing (over-serialization), not incorrect unlocking. |
| R-FIX-BUG-EPISODE-MDT-SAVE-RACE-FIXED-INTEGRATION-TEST | apps/api/tests/integration/bugEpisodeMdtSaveRace.int.test.ts | present | `BUG-EPISODE-MDT-SAVE-RACE \(S2\)` | BUG-EPISODE-MDT-SAVE-RACE (S2) closure 2026-05-06. NEW integration test proves race-free behavior: concurrent `Promise.all([req1(compositionA), req2(compositionB)])` for same episode + org_unit returns 200 on both; post-fix run: final active set EXACTLY one composition (size=2 Set; no UNION); zero duplicate (staff_id, clinical_role_id) active rows. |
| R-FIX-BUG-EPISODE-MDT-SAVE-RACE-DEACTIVATE-END-DATE | apps/api/src/features/episode/episodeRoutes.ts | present | `\.update\(\{ is_active: false, end_date: today, updated_at: new Date\(\) \}\)` | BUG-EPISODE-MDT-SAVE-RACE (S2) L4 cycle-1 absorb option (a) 2026-05-06. The deactivate UPDATE on `staff_role_assignments` (line 222-224 in the new transaction wrap) now also sets `end_date = today` alongside `is_active = false`, restoring effective-dating semantics. Pre-fix posture left `end_date IS NULL` on logically-terminated rows — silent corruption of the temporal audit trail used by AHPRA Standard 8 record-keeping + coronial point-in-time queries. Operator-authorized inline absorb (not split to separate BUG) because it lives on the exact deactivate state-transition line being modified for the concurrency closure. |

| R-FIX-BUG-EPISODE-MDT-SAVE-RACE-INTENTIONAL-ASYMMETRY | apps/api/src/features/referrals/referralRoutes.ts | present | `INTENTIONAL SEMANTIC ASYMMETRY` | BUG-EPISODE-MDT-SAVE-RACE L4 cycle-3 absorb F1(a) 2026-05-06. Comment block at referralRoutes.ts (after the L5 cycle-1 advisory-lock comment) documents the intentional asymmetry between the two writer surfaces: episodeRoutes.ts is REPLACE SEMANTICS (deactivate-then-insert; episode owner is authoritative on "who is on this team right now"); referralRoutes.ts is ADDITIVE SEMANTICS (INSERT only; referral letter adds names; episode owner curates). Cross-handler concurrent-save outcomes therefore differ by writer order BY DESIGN, not by race. The integration test's two valid serialized outcomes (E-then-R: size 4; R-then-E: size 2) are the EXPECTED, CLINICALLY-CORRECT states given the workflow each handler models — not vague nondeterminism. |

| R-FIX-P-CLAUDE-4B-MMSE-PENTAGONS-DRAWING | apps/api/src/features/assessments/builtinAssessmentDefinitions.ts | present | `Intersecting pentagons — copy the figure \(tablet capture\)` | P-CLAUDE-LANE 4B step 2 closure. Built-in MMSE template includes the tablet-capture drawing field adjacent to the 0-1 pentagons likert. Drawing field is non-scorable so the existing score total remains unchanged. |
| R-FIX-P-CLAUDE-4B-MOCA-CUBE-DRAWING | apps/api/src/features/assessments/builtinAssessmentDefinitions.ts | present | `Visuospatial — copy the cube \(tablet capture\)` | P-CLAUDE-LANE 4B step 2 closure. Built-in MoCA template includes the tablet-capture drawing field for the cube-copy item adjacent to the 0-5 visuospatial likert. Drawing field is non-scorable. |
| R-FIX-P-CLAUDE-4B-MOCA-CLOCK-DRAWING | apps/api/src/features/assessments/builtinAssessmentDefinitions.ts | present | `Visuospatial — draw the clock showing 11:10 \(tablet capture\)` | P-CLAUDE-LANE 4B step 2 closure. Built-in MoCA template includes the tablet-capture drawing field for the clock-draw 11:10 item adjacent to the 0-5 visuospatial likert. Drawing field is non-scorable. |
| R-FIX-P-CLAUDE-4B-READONLY-EARLY-RETURN | apps/web/src/shared/components/DrawingFieldCanvas.tsx | present | `if \(readOnly\) return;` | P-CLAUDE-LANE 4B step 3 closure. DrawingFieldCanvas's three mutator handlers (handlePointerDown / handleUndo / handleClear) each open with `if (readOnly) return;`. Removing any one would let a clinician reviewing a signed assessment accidentally mutate the captured drawing — the read-back path's structural guarantee. |
| R-FIX-P-CLAUDE-4B-RENDERER-READONLY-PASSTHROUGH | apps/web/src/shared/components/TemplateFormRenderer.tsx | present | `<DrawingFieldCanvas` | P-CLAUDE-LANE 4B step 3 closure. Template form renderer instantiates DrawingFieldCanvas in its 'drawing' dispatch case so signed-assessment review surfaces reach the readOnly-honouring canvas. Removing the JSX dispatcher would silently drop drawing fields from the read-back path. |
| R-FIX-P-CLAUDE-4B-CANVAS-BACKINGSTORE-PAYLOAD | apps/web/src/shared/components/DrawingFieldCanvas.tsx | present | `width=\{payload\.width\}` | P-CLAUDE-LANE 4B step 3 closure. Canvas backing store dimensions are sourced from the payload (device-independent coordinates) — NOT from the rendered CSS rect. Two clinicians on different-sized tablets reviewing the same stored payload see the same geometry. A regression that ties the backing store to the on-screen rect would break the cross-device invariant. |
| R-FIX-P-CLAUDE-4B-EXTRACT-CAPTURED-HELPER | apps/web/src/features/patients/components/detail/tabs/assessmentsTemplateUtils.ts | present | `export function extractCapturedDrawings` | P-CLAUDE-LANE 4B step 4 closure. The canonical helper that walks the template + FormValues at save time and emits a self-describing artefact (label + serialised payload) for each populated drawing field. Empty / unparseable slots are skipped so the persisted record stays lean. |
| R-FIX-P-CLAUDE-4B-PERSIST-CAPTURED-ON-SAVE | apps/web/src/features/patients/components/detail/tabs/AssessmentsTab.tsx | present | `extractCapturedDrawings\(ratingFields, ratingFormValues\)` | P-CLAUDE-LANE 4B step 4 closure. AssessmentsTab's save mutation extracts captured drawing payloads from FormValues and persists them inside contactMeta (conditionally, only when at least one was captured). Without this, the clinician's saved record loses the patient's actual figure even though it shows the `[drawing captured]` text marker. |
| R-FIX-P-CLAUDE-4B-READBACK-EXPAND-VIEW | apps/web/src/features/patients/components/detail/tabs/AssessmentsTab.tsx | present | `Captured Drawings` | P-CLAUDE-LANE 4B step 4 closure. AssessmentsTab's expand-view renders the persisted DrawingPayloads via DrawingFieldCanvas readOnly. Pinning the section header prevents the read-back surface from being silently removed; the readOnly pass-through is pinned by R-FIX-P-CLAUDE-4B-READONLY-EARLY-RETURN + DrawingFieldCanvas.contract.test.ts. |
| R-FIX-P-CLAUDE-4B-ANALYTICS-SSOT | packages/shared/src/drawingAnalytics.ts | present | `export function computeDrawingMetrics` | P-CLAUDE-LANE 4B step 5 closure. Canonical SSoT for stroke metrics derived from a stored DrawingPayload (stroke count, cumulative pen-down time, mean/min/max pressure, bounding box, canvas coverage). Consumed by the AssessmentsTab read-back metrics strip and any future analytics surface — the .NET parity reads the same numbers. |
| R-FIX-P-CLAUDE-4B-ANALYTICS-EXPORT | packages/shared/src/index.ts | present | `export \* from './drawingAnalytics';` | P-CLAUDE-LANE 4B step 5 closure. The drawing-analytics SSoT is re-exported from the package index so consumers can import via `@signacare/shared` rather than reaching into the file structure. Removing the export would silently break the metrics strip on every read-back surface. |
| R-FIX-P-CLAUDE-4B-METRICS-CHIPS-FORMATTER | apps/web/src/shared/components/drawingField.ts | present | `export function formatDrawingMetricsChips` | P-CLAUDE-LANE 4B step 5 closure. UI formatter that turns a DrawingMetrics object into compact clinician-facing chip labels (singular/plural-aware stroke count, sub-minute / minute-formatted drawing time, two-decimal pressure mean, whole-percent canvas coverage). Empty-metric fields produce no chip rather than a placeholder. |
| R-FIX-P-CLAUDE-4B-METRICS-STRIP-A11Y | apps/web/src/features/patients/components/detail/tabs/AssessmentsTab.tsx | present | `aria-label="Drawing metrics"` | P-CLAUDE-LANE 4B step 5 closure. The chip strip below each captured drawing carries an accessible name so screen readers announce "Drawing metrics" before traversing the chip list. Pinning the aria-label prevents the accessibility affordance from silently regressing. |
| R-FIX-P-CLAUDE-4B-MINICOG-TAXONOMY | packages/shared/src/assessmentTaxonomy.ts | present | `slug: 'minicog'` | P-CLAUDE-LANE 4B step 6 closure. Mini-Cog (Borson et al., 2000) registered as a clinician-rated cognitive-dementia screen for older adults in the canonical SCALE_REGISTRY. Required so the catalogue + grouping UI surfaces the instrument alongside MMSE / MoCA. |
| R-FIX-P-CLAUDE-4B-MINICOG-SCORING-SSOT | packages/shared/src/assessmentScoring.ts | present | `Positive screen — further workup indicated` | P-CLAUDE-LANE 4B step 6 closure. Mini-Cog scoring metadata (maxScore 5, two-band severity at ≤2 vs ≥3) lives in the canonical SSoT next to MMSE / MoCA. The template `ranges` mirror these via the parity guard so they cannot drift. |
| R-FIX-P-CLAUDE-4B-MINICOG-TEMPLATE | apps/api/src/features/assessments/builtinAssessmentDefinitions.ts | present | `Mini-Cog \(3-word recall \+ clock drawing\)` | P-CLAUDE-LANE 4B step 6 closure. Built-in Mini-Cog template carries a 3-word recall likert + clock-drawing likert + tablet-capture drawing field + 0-5 total score with severity bands. Drawing field is non-scorable so the Borson 0-5 total cannot be perturbed. |
| R-FIX-P-CLAUDE-4B-CDT-TAXONOMY | packages/shared/src/assessmentTaxonomy.ts | present | `slug: 'cdt-shulman'` | P-CLAUDE-LANE 4B step 7 closure. Standalone Clock Drawing Test (Shulman 6-band) registered in the canonical SCALE_REGISTRY with rubric-bearing kebab-case slug `cdt-shulman` so future rubrics (Sunderland, Mendez) can be added without a slug collision. |
| R-FIX-P-CLAUDE-4B-CDT-SCORING-SSOT | packages/shared/src/assessmentScoring.ts | present | `Inaccurate representation of 10 past 11` | P-CLAUDE-LANE 4B step 7 closure. Shulman 6-band CDT scoring metadata (maxScore 5, 6 single-point bands) lives in the canonical SSoT next to MMSE / MoCA / Mini-Cog. The grade-by-grade label alignment is the longitudinal-tracking invariant — clinicians comparing CDTs over time depend on identical labels at each grade. |
| R-FIX-P-CLAUDE-4B-CDT-TEMPLATE | apps/api/src/features/assessments/builtinAssessmentDefinitions.ts | present | `CDT \(Shulman Clock Drawing Test, 6-band\)` | P-CLAUDE-LANE 4B step 7 closure. Built-in standalone CDT template carries an instruction, a tablet-capture drawing field (BEFORE the grade likert — administration order matters), the Shulman grade likert (0-5), and the 6-band total score. Drawing is non-scorable so the 0-5 total cannot be perturbed. |

ERE patterns that consist of just a function name (e.g. `snakeToCamel`) will
still match a renamed version like `snakeToCamelXXX` because the regex finds
the substring. To make a pattern decisive, anchor it with surrounding context:

- For function definitions, use `function snakeToCamel\(` or
  `export[^=]*snakeToCamel`.
- For named exports, use `\bsnakeToCamel\b` (note: word boundaries work in
  GNU grep / `git grep -E`; if your pattern needs to be portable, prefer
  surrounding punctuation).
- For Knex aliases, use `as some_name['"]` (with the closing quote).
- For HTTP route definitions, use `router\.(post|get|put|delete)\('/path'`.

Test your pattern locally with the synthetic-break protocol:
1. Run `./.github/scripts/check-fix-registry.sh` — should be 0 failures.
2. Manually rename or weaken the protected code in the file.
3. Re-run the script — your entry must now FAIL.
4. Restore the file. Re-run — must pass again.

If step 3 doesn't fail, your pattern is too loose and will not catch real
regressions.

## §NO-SMS — the WebSocket-discipline delivery policy

Phase 10F of the multi-specialty plan locks in a strict rule: staff
notifications and inter-staff messaging flow through in-app push
(Server-Sent Events on web, FCM on mobile in Phase 11) and durable
in-app records only. Telecom SMS, voice gateways, Twilio, MessageBird,
Vonage and `@azure/communication-*` are OUT of the delivery matrix.

**Two surgical exemptions exist**, both allowlisted in
`.github/scripts/check-no-telecom.sh` and documented here so future
contributors don't silently unwind them:

1. **`apps/api/src/integrations/escript/**`** — regulated ETP2 eRx
   prescription token delivery to pharmacy gateways (MySL / eRx). Not
   a patient notification path; the SMS protocol is dictated by the
   prescribing regulation and must not change.

2. **`apps/api/src/integrations/acs/**`** — Phase 12's ACS SMS fallback
   for patients who do NOT have the Viva mobile app installed. Used
   exclusively by `patientOutreachService` which routes through the
   `patient-outreach` dispatcher queue. A second guard (Phase 12D)
   will enforce that nothing outside `patient-outreach/` may import
   from `integrations/acs/**`.

**Two layers of defence**:

- **CI guard** — `.github/scripts/check-no-telecom.sh` flags any new
  telecom import, any `addJob('sms', …)` / `enqueue('sms', …)` call
  site, and any `sendSms` identifier outside the allowlisted dirs.
  Runs on every PR alongside the naming-conventions and fix-registry
  guards. First line of defence, PR-time.

- **Runtime allowlist** — `apps/api/src/shared/jobBus.ts` has an
  `ALLOWED_QUEUES` set that `assertAllowedQueue()` enforces on every
  `enqueue` call. The raw `'sms'` queue name is deliberately absent;
  a runtime call throws synchronously with a clear error message
  naming the forbidden queue. Second line of defence, production
  runtime.

**When a legitimate new queue is needed:** add its kebab-case name
to `ALLOWED_QUEUES` in `jobBus.ts` AND add an `NO-SMS*` row to the
registry above explaining why, so future contributors can trace the
decision back to the rationale.

**When a contributor insists on raw SMS:** refuse. The correct
integration point is the `patient-outreach` dispatcher (Phase 12B)
which already handles the FCM / ACS-SMS / skip decision tree with
clinician-override support, audit logging, per-clinic cost caps,
and a manual override reason field.

| R-FIX-BUG-341-NPDS-DYNAMIC-DB-IMPORT | apps/api/src/integrations/escript/npdsClient.ts | present | `await import\('\.\.\/\.\.\/db\/db'\)` | BUG-341 closure anchor. | R-FIX-BUG-263-HL7-STAT-RETRY-PROFILE | apps/api/src/integrations/hl7/hl7OutboundRetryProfile.ts | present | `alertAtAttempt:\s*2` | BUG-263 closure anchor. HL7 outbound retry behavior is now urgency-aware with canonical SSoT: `routine/urgent` keep `attempts=5` + `30s` exponential, while `stat` uses `attempts=3` + `10s` exponential and defines early alert threshold at failed attempt 2. Enqueue path (`pathologyService.placeOrder`) applies profile options and carries urgency into job payload; failure handler (`handleOutboundHl7JobFailure`) emits early `integration_unreachable` signal when STAT reaches attempt 2. Regression pins: `apps/api/tests/unit/bug263Hl7RetryProfile.test.ts` and `apps/api/tests/integration/hl7Transport.int.test.ts` (`BUG-263` case). |
| R-FIX-HL7-ORM-OUTBOUND | apps/api/src/integrations/hl7/hl7OrmBuilder.ts | present | `buildPharmacyOrmO01\(` | BUG-300 closure anchor. Added canonical pharmacy HL7 module with outbound ORM^O01 builder (`MSH/PID/ORC/RXO/RXE`), transport dispatch seam (`dispatchPharmacyOrmO01` via existing `dispatchHl7`), and fail-closed RDE^O11 dispense parser (`parseRdeO11DispenseConfirmation`). Parser rejects non-RDE message types and missing structural segments with explicit `AppError` codes. Regression pins: `apps/api/tests/unit/bug300Hl7OrmOutboundBuilder.test.ts` and transport contract replay `apps/api/tests/unit/hl7Transport.test.ts`. |
| R-FIX-BUG-333-MTLS-KEEPALIVE-SHUTDOWN-DRAIN | apps/api/src/server.ts | present | `name:\s*'mtls-agent-drain'` | BUG-333 closure anchor. Graceful shutdown now includes dedicated hook `mtls-agent-drain` (priority 45) that drains cached mTLS keep-alive agents before DB/Redis teardown, preventing outbound TLS sockets from lingering across shutdown boundaries. Hook delegates to canonical `drainMtlsAgentCacheForShutdown()` in `apps/api/src/shared/mtls.ts`. eRx Adapter was also converged to shared mTLS cache creation (`createMtlsAgent`) so all outbound mTLS agents share the same drain path. Regression pin: `apps/api/tests/mtlsHelper.test.ts` T6 asserts seeded cache entries are destroyed and cache drains to zero. |

| R-FIX-PART6-NO-INLINE-DATE-BUCKET-MATH-GUARD | scripts/guards/check-no-inline-date-bucket-math.ts | present | `SCAN_ROOT = path\.join\(REPO_ROOT, 'apps', 'web', 'src', 'features', 'lists'\)` | Plan PART 6 DoD#5. Merge-gate guard (npm `guard:no-inline-date-bucket-math`, wired in ci.yml) banning literal day-ms (`86400000`/`86_400_000`) and canonical day-ms compositions in `apps/web/src/features/lists/**` after comment/string stripping. Empty allowlist by mandate (`scripts/guards/check-no-inline-date-bucket-math.allowlist`). Negative-proof verified: injecting either shape → exit 1; comment/string-only → exit 0 (no false-positive); clean tree → exit 0. Forces all bucket/cadence date math through the dueDateBuckets/reviewCycle SSoT so the count cards and the date-range filter can never silently drift. |

## §NO-STRAY-DB-NAMES — Postgres naming normalization

Phase 0.5 of the multi-specialty plan locks in a single canonical set of
Postgres identifiers and stops drift from silently recurring:

- **Owner role (migrations / DDL / schema owner):** `signacare_owner`
- **Runtime role (RLS-scoped app pool):** `app_user`
- **Database:** `signacaredb`

These names come from `docs/gold-standard-reports/08-deployment-guide.md §2
"Postgres role model"` and are pinned in `apps/api/.env.example`. Any other
identifier (`nous`, `nousdev`, `nousemr`, `noususer`, `signacareemr`) is
forbidden.

**Background.** On 2026-04-15 a local-dev login failure exposed a three-way
drift: `.env.production` pinned the real historical `noususer`/`nousdev`,
`apps/api/.env.example` aspirationally claimed `signacare`/`signacareemr`
(never existed anywhere), the working-tree `apps/api/.env` had
`nous`/`nousemr`/`app_user` (a fourth set matching nothing), and the RLS
migration `apps/api/src/db/migrations/20260329_rls_app_user.sql` hardcoded
`GRANT ... ON DATABASE signacareemr` and `ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES FOR ROLE
signacare` — a ticking bomb that would have failed the next production
migration run. Phase 0.5 brings every file into line with the deployment
guide, rewrites the RLS migration to be name-agnostic, and adds this guard
so the drift cannot recur silently.

**Four whitelisted exception files** (may contain the forbidden literals
for documented reasons):

1. `apps/api/migrations/20260505000000_rename_db_and_role.ts` — the
   one-time rename migration itself. Must reference both the old and new
   names to execute the rename.

2. `docs/fix-registry.md` — this file. The registry preserves the history
   of *why* the guard exists; scrubbing the old names would erase the
   rationale.

3. `docs/phase-0.5-rename-runbook.md` — the operator runbook for the
   rename window. Must show the exact pre-rename and post-rename commands
   using both name sets.

4. `.github/scripts/check-no-stray-db-names.sh` — the guard itself. Its
   own documentation must name the forbidden literals.

**Two layers of defence**:

- **CI guard** — `.github/scripts/check-no-stray-db-names.sh` scans every
  file that can hold a DB or role name (env files, migrations,
  docker-compose, CI workflows, deploy scripts, `.claude/settings.json`,
  scripts, docs) for the forbidden literals AND for hardcoded identifiers
  inside `GRANT` / `REVOKE` / `ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES` statements in
  migration SQL. Runs on every PR. Two modes: `warn` (logs hits, exits 0
  — used in Phase 0.5 PR 1 so the prep PR can merge) and `fail` (blocks
  the build — flipped on in Phase 0.5 PR 2).

- **CLAUDE.md §7.4** — *"Migration SQL must never hardcode database or
  role names"* — the written rule that migration authors are expected to
  follow. The guard is the machine enforcement; §7.4 is the documented
  norm and the reviewer's reference.

**When a new canonical identifier is needed** — for example, a new
dedicated role for a background worker — add it to `.env.example`, add it
to the deployment guide, and update the guard's allowlist in one commit.
Never add it only to one place.

**When the rename migration is run** — exactly once per environment, in
the rename window documented in `docs/phase-0.5-rename-runbook.md`. Gated
by `ALLOW_DB_RENAME=1` and `SUPERUSER_DATABASE_URL`. Rollback is instant
and zero-data-loss (rename is metadata-only).

| R-FIX-BUG-SA-103-ALLOWLIST-BURNDOWN-CONTRACT | .github/allowlist-burndown-contract.json | present | `"response-shape-validated"` | BUG-SA-103 closure slice. High-volume allowlist lanes are now governed by executable checkpoint budgets ending at zero by 2026-12-31. |
| R-FIX-BUG-SA-106-DASHBOARD-KEY-CLINIC-SCOPE | scripts/guards/check-dashboard-query-key-clinic-scope.ts | present | `dashboard query-key clinic scope guard passed` | BUG-SA-106 closure slice. Dashboard query key factory must stay clinic-tokenized and cannot regress to flat dash-* keys that break context isolation/invalidation semantics. |
| R-FIX-BUG-SA-115-LIFECHART-SCHEMA-SPLIT | apps/web/src/features/patients/components/detail/tabs/lifeChartSchemaDomain.ts | present | `from './lifeChartSchemaHeuristics'` | BUG-SA-115 closure slice. `lifeChartSchemaDomain.ts` is now a thin façade over bounded modules (`lifeChartSchemaCore`, `lifeChartSchemaNormalize`, `lifeChartSchemaHeuristics`) so schema model/normalization/heuristic logic no longer lives in one hotspot file. |
| R-FIX-BUG-SA-105-FILE-SIZE-GUARD-GREEN | scripts/guards/check-file-size.ts | present | `check-file-size: OK` | BUG-SA-105 closure slice. Active file-size threshold pressure is controlled by enforced `guard:file-size`, with dedicated follow-on decompositions tracked separately (SA-112/SA-113/SA-114). |
| R-FIX-BUG-SA-107-CRITICAL-PATH-P95-BASELINE | apps/api/tests/integration/bugSa010CriticalPathPerformance.int.test.ts | present | `plannedTransitions` | BUG-SA-107 closure slice. Planned-transition and related allocation reads are pinned behind enforced p95 ceilings so high-variance/N+1 regressions fail in CI before deployment. |
| R-FIX-2026-06-SAFETY-CALENDAR-ACCESS-TRANCHE | apps/api/src/shared/authGuards.ts | present | `context.isManager || context.isTeamLeader` | 2026-06 cross-cutting safety/access hardening tranche. Managers and team leaders now receive organisation-wide read-only patient review access while note-entry still requires a write relationship or duty relationship; shipped atomically with calendar/time-blocking, template, and deploy-drift hardening so the safety-surface changes remain catalogued in the same delivery wave. |

## Notes on entries that cannot be machine-checked

Some Fix Registry items in the team's session memory describe semantic
properties that cannot be reduced to a single grep (e.g. "team-summary route
mounted BEFORE /:id" or "all 19 parseInt calls have radix 10"). Those are
not in this table — they are protected by other CI checks (the
`naming-checks` step from S0.2 will cover the radix and URL-prefix cases) or
by code review. Do not add such entries here unless you can express them
as a single ERE pattern.
